## BFA CONFLICT RULES — FLOW-18 (CF-295 through CF-328)

### Group 1: Canvas Integrity (CF-295–CF-301)

| CF | Rule | Cross-Flow Check | Severity |
|----|------|------------------|----------|
| CF-295 | Canvas ID unique per tenant — no collision with FLOW-15 definition IDs | FLOW-15 F466 definition registry | CRITICAL |
| CF-296 | Canvas quota enforced per tenant tier — FLOW-15 F508 quota check | FLOW-15 F508 IPaywallGateService | HIGH |
| CF-297 | Node type must exist in palette — palette synced with factory registry | FLOW-15 F478 step contract registry | HIGH |
| CF-298 | Max nodes per canvas enforced — prevents resource exhaustion | Self-contained | MEDIUM |
| CF-299 | Max edges per node — prevents graph explosion | Self-contained | MEDIUM |
| CF-300 | Condition expression complexity bounded — JSON Logic depth limit | Self-contained | MEDIUM |
| CF-301 | Auto-layout timeout enforced — prevents UI freeze | Self-contained | LOW |

### Group 2: Code Generation Safety (CF-302–CF-309)

| CF | Rule | Cross-Flow Check | Severity |
|----|------|------------------|----------|
| CF-302 | DNA compliance mandatory (9/9) before code injection | ALL flows — DNA patterns are universal | CRITICAL |
| CF-303 | Security scan pass mandatory before injection | ALL flows — security is universal | CRITICAL |
| CF-304 | Factory ID globally unique — no collision with F1–F630 | ALL flows F1–F630 registry check | CRITICAL |
| CF-305 | Fabric resolution mapping mandatory — no orphan factories | ALL flows — fabric registry | CRITICAL |
| CF-306 | Code injection must be reversible — rollback guaranteed | Self-contained | CRITICAL |
| CF-307 | Feature flag required for every injection — no direct deploy | Self-contained + FLOW-15 F491 feature flags | HIGH |
| CF-308 | No duplicate BFA entity registration — merge with existing | ALL flows CF-1–CF-294 | HIGH |
| CF-309 | Event bus consistency — new events don't conflict with FLOW-01–FLOW-17 events | ALL flows — event namespace check | HIGH |

### Group 3: Sandbox Isolation (CF-310–CF-314)

| CF | Rule | Cross-Flow Check | Severity |
|----|------|------------------|----------|
| CF-310 | Sandbox environment completely isolated from production | ALL flows — production DB isolation | CRITICAL |
| CF-311 | Test data PII check — synthetic only, no real PII | ALL flows — data privacy | CRITICAL |
| CF-312 | Sandbox cannot read/write production databases | ALL flows — connection isolation | CRITICAL |
| CF-313 | Step execution timeout enforced per step | Self-contained | HIGH |
| CF-314 | Resource limits (CPU/memory/network) per sandbox | Self-contained | HIGH |

### Group 4: Promotion Safety (CF-315–CF-319)

| CF | Rule | Cross-Flow Check | Severity |
|----|------|------------------|----------|
| CF-315 | Quality gate pass required before any promotion | ALL flows — quality gate registry | CRITICAL |
| CF-316 | Test report must exist before promotion | Self-contained | CRITICAL |
| CF-317 | No platform-specific imports in UI code | Self-contained | HIGH |
| CF-318 | Debug panel reads only sandbox data — never production | FLOW-15 F493 audit, production DBs | CRITICAL |
| CF-319 | AI operations pass same validation as manual operations | Self-contained | HIGH |

### Group 5: Collaboration Safety (CF-320–CF-325)

| CF | Rule | Cross-Flow Check | Severity |
|----|------|------------------|----------|
| CF-320 | Permission check before session join | FLOW-01 F174 user auth | CRITICAL |
| CF-321 | Cross-tenant isolation in collaboration | ALL flows — tenant isolation universal | CRITICAL |
| CF-322 | CRDT eventual consistency guarantee | Self-contained | HIGH |
| CF-323 | No data loss on conflict — every edit preserved or merged | Self-contained | HIGH |
| CF-324 | Owner cannot remove own owner role | Self-contained | MEDIUM |
| CF-325 | Permission check on every canvas operation | Self-contained | HIGH |

### Group 6: Marketplace Integrity (CF-326–CF-328)

| CF | Rule | Cross-Flow Check | Severity |
|----|------|------------------|----------|
| CF-326 | Test report required before marketplace listing | Self-contained + CF-316 | CRITICAL |
| CF-327 | Unique listing per flow version — no duplicate listings | Self-contained | HIGH |
| CF-328 | Imported flow must register in BFA — no untracked factories | ALL flows — BFA registry | CRITICAL |

---

## STRESS TESTS — FLOW-18 (ST-164 through ST-183)

### Canvas Performance (ST-164–ST-166)

```
ST-164: Canvas Max Complexity
  SCENARIO: Canvas with 200 nodes + 300 edges + simultaneous auto-layout
  FACTORIES TESTED: F631, F634, F635
  PASS CRITERIA: Render < 2s, auto-layout < 2s, save < 500ms
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-298, CF-299, CF-301

ST-165: Canvas Concurrent Save
  SCENARIO: 10 users simultaneously saving to same canvas via CRDT
  FACTORIES TESTED: F631, F669, F668
  PASS CRITERIA: No data loss, eventual consistency < 2s, all saves persisted
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-322, CF-323

ST-166: Canvas History Depth
  SCENARIO: 500 sequential operations → undo all → redo all
  FACTORIES TESTED: F636, F631
  PASS CRITERIA: All operations undoable, redo restores exact state, < 100ms per undo
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-295
```

### Code Generation Speed (ST-167–ST-169)

```
ST-167: Multi-Model Code Generation
  SCENARIO: Generate service code via Claude + GPT + Gemini in parallel
  FACTORIES TESTED: F643, F648
  PASS CRITERIA: All 3 models respond < 30s, consensus achieved, DNA 9/9
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-302, CF-303

ST-168: Full Pipeline Code → Inject
  SCENARIO: T251 → T252 → T253 → T254 sequential execution
  FACTORIES TESTED: F643, F644, F645, F646, F650, F651, F682
  PASS CRITERIA: End-to-end < 60s, all validations pass, feature flag active
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-302, CF-304, CF-305, CF-306, CF-307

ST-169: Bulk Code Generation
  SCENARIO: Generate code for 10 nodes simultaneously
  FACTORIES TESTED: F643, F644
  PASS CRITERIA: All 10 complete < 120s, no resource exhaustion, all DNA pass
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-302
```

### Sandbox Execution (ST-170–ST-172)

```
ST-170: Sandbox Isolation Verification
  SCENARIO: Sandbox attempts to connect to production database
  FACTORIES TESTED: F652, F654
  PASS CRITERIA: Connection REJECTED with clear error, zero production data read
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-310, CF-312

ST-171: Sandbox Step-Through Full Flow
  SCENARIO: 20-step flow executed step-by-step with breakpoints at steps 5, 10, 15
  FACTORIES TESTED: F654, F655, F664
  PASS CRITERIA: All steps complete, breakpoints hit accurately, state captured per step
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-313, CF-314

ST-172: Sandbox TTL Enforcement
  SCENARIO: Sandbox reaches TTL while execution in progress
  FACTORIES TESTED: F652
  PASS CRITERIA: Sandbox destroyed gracefully, resources freed, no zombie processes
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-310
```

### Collaborative Editing (ST-173–ST-175)

```
ST-173: CRDT Convergence Under Load
  SCENARIO: 5 users make 100 concurrent edits (add/remove/move nodes) within 10s
  FACTORIES TESTED: F669, F668, F670
  PASS CRITERIA: All users converge to same state < 3s, zero edits lost
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-322, CF-323

ST-174: Permission Enforcement During Collaboration
  SCENARIO: Viewer user attempts edit operation during active session
  FACTORIES TESTED: F672, F668
  PASS CRITERIA: Edit REJECTED, viewer session not disrupted, audit logged
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-320, CF-325

ST-175: Session Recovery After Disconnect
  SCENARIO: User disconnects for 60s then reconnects
  FACTORIES TESTED: F668, F669, F670
  PASS CRITERIA: State synced within 2s of reconnect, no data loss, cursor positions restored
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-322
```

### Marketplace & Import (ST-176–ST-178)

```
ST-176: Marketplace Search Performance
  SCENARIO: Search marketplace with 10,000+ published flows
  FACTORIES TESTED: F674, F677
  PASS CRITERIA: Search results < 500ms, relevance ranked, category filters work
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-327

ST-177: Flow Import with Factory ID Conflict
  SCENARIO: Import flow that contains factory ID colliding with existing
  FACTORIES TESTED: F676, F679, F650
  PASS CRITERIA: Conflict detected, new IDs assigned, lineage preserved, BFA registered
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-304, CF-328

ST-178: Cross-Tenant Marketplace Isolation
  SCENARIO: Tenant A publishes flow, Tenant B imports, verify no data leakage
  FACTORIES TESTED: F674, F676, F679
  PASS CRITERIA: Tenant A data never visible to Tenant B, tenant-scoped IDs assigned
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-321
```

### Promotion Pipeline (ST-179–ST-181)

```
ST-179: Full Promotion Ladder
  SCENARIO: GENERATED → TESTED → INJECTED → MINIMAL → CORE (end-to-end)
  FACTORIES TESTED: F681, F682, F683, F684, F685, F686, F687
  PASS CRITERIA: All stages complete, feature flags manage traffic, health checks pass
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-306, CF-307, CF-315

ST-180: Rollback Under Load
  SCENARIO: Canary at 20% traffic, health degradation detected → automatic rollback
  FACTORIES TESTED: F685, F686, F683
  PASS CRITERIA: Rollback < 2s, zero traffic to failed version, previous version healthy
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-306

ST-181: Concurrent Promotions
  SCENARIO: Two flows promoting simultaneously in same tenant
  FACTORIES TESTED: F681, F682, F683
  PASS CRITERIA: No cross-contamination, independent feature flags, both complete
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-307
```

### Git Integration & Cross-Flow (ST-182–ST-183)

```
ST-182: External Git Sync Round-Trip
  SCENARIO: Push flow to GitHub → modify in GitHub → pull back → verify consistency
  FACTORIES TESTED: F688, F692
  PASS CRITERIA: Round-trip preserves flow definition, no data loss, lineage tracked
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: None (self-contained)

ST-183: DNA Compliance Sweep
  SCENARIO: Validate ALL 66 FLOW-18 factories against ALL 9 DNA patterns
  FACTORIES TESTED: F631–F696 (all 66)
  PASS CRITERIA: 594 checks (66 × 9) = 100% pass rate
  BFA RULES VALIDATED: CF-302 (DNA universal)
```


# FLOW-19 — CI/CD & DevOps Control Plane — BFA Rules CF-329-CF-355 | Stress Tests ST-184-ST-198
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## FLOW-19 CHANGELOG
| Date | Flow | BFA Rules | Stress Tests | Notes |
|------|------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| 2026-02-27 | FLOW-19 CI/CD & DevOps Control Plane | CF-329-CF-355 (+27) | ST-184-ST-198 (+15) | 5 groups: DevOps ordering, cross-flow, multi-tenant, backup/DR, GitOps safety |

## System State Update (Post FLOW-19)

Added: ST-184-ST-198 (15 stress tests — FLOW-19)

## BFA CONFLICT RULES — CF-329-CF-355

### GROUP A: DEVOPS INTERNAL ORDERING (CF-329-CF-335) — 7 rules

```
CF-329: IaC Concurrency Guard
  RULE: Maximum N concurrent IaC apply operations per cloud account+region
        (N configurable, default 3). No new apply starts if limit reached.
  ENTITIES: F702 IIaCRunnerService, EnvironmentInstance (Provisioning state)
  VIOLATION: Multiple parallel IaC applies to same account/region exceed N
  DETECTION: F702 checks active-apply count via DATABASE FABRIC before Apply call
  RESOLUTION: Queue apply request; retry with backoff after in-flight apply completes
  BFA INDEX: iac-concurrency-locks:{account}:{region}
  SEVERITY: ERROR — blocks apply until concurrency slot available

CF-330: Graph Before Promotion
  RULE: Dependency graph (F699) must be consistent (no pending refresh) before
        any promotion gate (T281) can evaluate critical path.
  ENTITIES: F699 IDependencyGraphService, F712 IPromotionGateService
  VIOLATION: T281 runs with stale graph (graph refresh in-flight or failed)
  DETECTION: T281 checks graph.last_successful_refresh_at before proceeding
  RESOLUTION: Wait for graph refresh or trigger refresh and retry
  SEVERITY: ERROR — blocks promotion

CF-331: Config Before Provisioning
  RULE: T274 (Config Layer Resolution) MUST complete before T271 (Env Request Gate)
        enqueues IaC provisioning. Never provision with unresolved config.
  ENTITIES: F705 IConfigLayerResolverService, F701 IEnvironmentProvisionerService
  VIOLATION: env.provision_step.queued emitted without valid ConfigBundle
  DETECTION: F701 checks ConfigBundle presence before state transition
  RESOLUTION: T274 must be replayed; T271 blocks until ConfigBundle confirmed
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE

CF-332: Policy Before Provisioning
  RULE: T275 (Policy Evaluation Gate) MUST produce PolicyDecision.Allow before
        T272 (IaC Provision Saga) begins. Policy deny blocks provisioning entirely.
  ENTITIES: F707 IPolicyEngineService, F702 IIaCRunnerService
  VIOLATION: IaC plan/apply triggered without PolicyDecision.Allow
  DETECTION: F702 checks PolicyDecision in FactoryResolutionContext before proceeding
  RESOLUTION: Return PolicyDecision.Deny with obligations to requester
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE

CF-333: Rollback On Post-Deploy Failure
  RULE: Any failed post-deploy health check (F715) or integration test (F720)
        within 5 minutes of GitOps sync MUST trigger automatic rollback via F712.
  ENTITIES: F715 IDeploymentHealthService, F712 IPromotionGateService
  VIOLATION: Post-deploy failure detected but rollback not triggered within 5 min
  DETECTION: F715 monitors health; timeout triggers automatic rollback event
  RESOLUTION: F712 TriggerRollbackAsync → revert to previous artifact version
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE

CF-334: Readiness Before Promotion
  RULE: ReadinessReport (F719) with overall_pass=true MUST exist for the specific
        FlowInstance before F712 can approve any promotion.
  ENTITIES: F719 IReadinessReportService, F712 IPromotionGateService
  VIOLATION: Promotion approved without linked ReadinessReport or with failed report
  DETECTION: F712 queries F719.IsGatePassingAsync before approval
  RESOLUTION: Block promotion; require new test run
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE (DR-117)

CF-335: TTL Cleanup Non-Negotiable
  RULE: Every ephemeral environment (F703) MUST be cleaned up within TTL period.
        No environment may persist beyond TTL without explicit extension.
        Extension requires new policy evaluation (CF-332).
  ENTITIES: F703 IEphemeralEnvService, F702 IIaCRunnerService
  VIOLATION: Environment exists in cluster past TTL without deletion or extension
  DETECTION: Redis TTL expiry event triggers T273 (Expiry Gate)
  RESOLUTION: T273 → F702 compensation (namespace delete)
  SEVERITY: ERROR — resource leak, escalates to CRITICAL if unresolved > 2×TTL
```

---

### GROUP B: DEVOPS VS PRIOR FLOWS (CF-336-CF-342) — 7 rules

```
CF-336: No Secrets in Git (Cross-Flow)
  RULE: F697 (ICatalogIngestionService) MUST scan descriptor content for secret
        patterns (API keys, connection strings, tokens) during ingestion.
        Any detected secret MUST block ingestion and alert component owner.
  ENTITIES: F697, all prior-flow factories with config (F427 FLOW-14 credentials)
  VIOLATION: Secret value found in component descriptor YAML committed to Git
  DETECTION: F697 content scan via AI ENGINE FABRIC (pattern matching)
  RESOLUTION: Ingestion blocked; alert sent; descriptor rejected
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE

CF-337: Catalog Must Not Duplicate Prior-Flow Factories
  RULE: ICatalogIngestionService (F697) must detect when a new component descriptor
        references the same logical factory as existing F1-F696 and flag as
        potential duplicate registration rather than creating a parallel entry.
  ENTITIES: F697, F700, all F1-F696 factory definitions
  VIOLATION: New catalog entry has same logical service name as existing factory
  DETECTION: F700 IInventoryQueryService checks for name collision on ingest
  RESOLUTION: Flag as duplicate candidate; require explicit disambiguation comment
  SEVERITY: WARNING — escalates to ERROR if component activated

CF-338: Drift Detection Blocks Promotion (Cross-Flow)
  RULE: Unacknowledged configuration drift (F714) on any component that participates
        in a multi-flow interaction (FLOW-01 through FLOW-14) blocks that component's
        promotion through T281.
  ENTITIES: F714 IDriftDetectorService, F712 IPromotionGateService
  VIOLATION: Promotion proceeds with unacknowledged drift on inter-flow component
  DETECTION: T281 checks F714 drift status before approval
  RESOLUTION: Acknowledge drift via F714.AcknowledgeDriftAsync with reason, then re-promote
  SEVERITY: ERROR — blocks promotion

CF-339: DevOps Audit Must Not Shadow FLOW-14 DWH Audit
  RULE: IControlPlaneAuditService (F726) and FLOW-14 IWarehouseAuditService (F459)
        are separate audit planes. Events must never be cross-written.
        DevOps plane events go to F726 only; DWH events go to F459 only.
  ENTITIES: F726 (FLOW-19), F459 (FLOW-14)
  VIOLATION: F726 receives FLOW-14 DWH events or vice versa
  DETECTION: F726 validates event_source tag before appending
  RESOLUTION: Reject misrouted events; escalate as routing config error
  SEVERITY: ERROR

CF-340: DR Drill Sandbox Must Not Use FLOW-14 Warehouse Data
  RULE: IRestoreSandboxService (F724) must never seed sandbox from FLOW-14
        warehouse zones (F438-F453). Sandbox is seeded ONLY from backup artifacts.
        This prevents warehouse data corruption during drill operations.
  ENTITIES: F724 (FLOW-19), F438-F453 (FLOW-14)
  VIOLATION: Sandbox seeded from live warehouse index/table
  DETECTION: F724 validates seed source — must be backup artifact pointer only
  RESOLUTION: Reject seed request; sandbox must use backup artifact
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE (DR-118)

CF-341: Tenant Onboarding Must Use FLOW-08 Isolation Primitives
  RULE: ITenantOnboardingService (F730) and ITenantBindingResolverService (F729)
        must leverage FLOW-08 ITenantWarehouseIsolationService (F461) and
        FLOW-14 IRowLevelSecurityService (F463) for data plane isolation.
        DevOps plane must not introduce parallel isolation mechanisms.
  ENTITIES: F729, F730 (FLOW-19), F461 F463 (FLOW-08/FLOW-14)
  VIOLATION: New isolation mechanism created in DevOps plane bypassing F461/F463
  DETECTION: AF-7 compliance check for isolation primitives
  RESOLUTION: Reuse existing isolation factories via factory registry
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE

CF-342: Pipeline Contracts Must Respect FLOW-05 Gamification Events
  RULE: Any CI/CD pipeline (managed by FLOW-19) that deploys FLOW-05 components
        must include gamification event replay validation in its integration suite.
        T279 (Integration Test Orchestration) must include FLOW-05 event contract tests.
  ENTITIES: F710 (FLOW-19), F176-F224 (FLOW-05)
  VIOLATION: Pipeline deploys FLOW-05 components without gamification event contract test
  DETECTION: F709 IPipelineContractService checks component scope for FLOW-05 factories
  RESOLUTION: Add gamification event contract test to integration suite
  SEVERITY: WARNING — escalates to ERROR if FLOW-05 in production scope
```

---

### GROUP C: MULTI-TENANT DEVOPS ISOLATION (CF-343-CF-347) — 5 rules

```
CF-343: Sensitive Data Routing — Local Models Only
  RULE: For environments or tenants with data_classification=sensitive or
        local-sensitive profile, ALL AI operations (F707 policy reasoning,
        F716 manifest rendering, F727 hallucination drift) MUST route to
        local AI providers only. External AI providers (Claude API, OpenAI,
        Gemini) are FORBIDDEN for sensitive data.
  ENTITIES: F707, F716, F727 (FLOW-19), IAiProvider (AI ENGINE FABRIC)
  VIOLATION: Sensitive-classified data sent to external AI provider
  DETECTION: F707 PolicyDecision includes routing obligation; AI ENGINE FABRIC
             checks obligation before CreateAsync resolution
  RESOLUTION: PolicyDecision.Deny with obligation: "route-to-local-only"
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE (DR-112)

CF-344: Per-Tenant Config Cache Isolation
  RULE: IConfigLayerResolverService (F705) and ITenantConfigLayerService (F732)
        Redis cache keys MUST include tenantId as prefix. No shared cache keys
        across tenants. Cache invalidation MUST be tenant-scoped only.
  ENTITIES: F705, F732, Redis cache
  VIOLATION: Cache key missing tenantId prefix; cross-tenant cache pollution
  DETECTION: AF-7 compliance scan; DNA-5 enforcement
  RESOLUTION: Reject write without tenantId; invalidate polluted cache keys
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE (DNA-5)

CF-345: Ephemeral Environments Must Be Tenant-Scoped
  RULE: Every environment created by IEphemeralEnvService (F703) MUST have
        tenantId in its namespace name and ResourceQuota. Namespaces must not be
        shared across tenants. Naming: {tenant_id}-{env_type}-{pr_number}.
  ENTITIES: F703 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Namespace created without tenantId prefix; shared namespace across tenants
  DETECTION: F703 validates namespace name format before creation
  RESOLUTION: Reject namespace creation; enforce naming convention
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE

CF-346: Binding Resolver Never Cross-Tenant
  RULE: ITenantBindingResolverService (F729) MUST validate that the resolved
        binding (shard/schema/db) is assigned to exactly one tenant_id.
        Cross-tenant binding resolution is a catastrophic isolation failure.
  ENTITIES: F729 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Two different tenant_ids resolve to the same binding shard/schema
  DETECTION: F729 validates binding uniqueness constraint on every resolve
  RESOLUTION: Alert immediately; suspend affected tenants; require manual review
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — IMMEDIATE INCIDENT

CF-347: Onboarding Compensation Must Not Affect Other Tenants
  RULE: When ITenantOnboardingService (F730) runs compensation (rollback on failure),
        compensation scope is LIMITED to the onboarding tenant. No shared resources
        may be deleted or modified during compensation.
  ENTITIES: F730 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Compensation step deletes/modifies shared infrastructure
  DETECTION: F730 validates each compensation step target against new tenant scope
  RESOLUTION: Compensation limited to tenant-specific resources only
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE
```

---

### GROUP D: BACKUP/DR CONSTRAINTS (CF-348-CF-351) — 4 rules

```
CF-348: Backup Before Drill
  RULE: IRestoreDrillService (F722) MUST require a completed, verified backup
        artifact from IBackupAdapterService (F721) before starting restore phase.
        Drills on missing or unverified backups are forbidden.
  ENTITIES: F721, F722 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Restore drill started without verified backup.completed event
  DETECTION: F722 checks backup.completed event presence + integrity flag
  RESOLUTION: Block drill; trigger T282 backup run first
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE

CF-349: Drill Evidence Immutability
  RULE: IDrillEvidenceService (F723) and IControlPlaneAuditService (F726)
        records are append-only. Any attempt to update or delete evidence records
        is a BUILD FAILURE and an audit violation.
  ENTITIES: F723, F726 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: UPDATE or DELETE executed against evidence/audit index
  DETECTION: AF-7 compliance scan; index write policy enforcement
  RESOLUTION: Reject mutation; alert security team
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE (DR-119)

CF-350: RTO Target Enforcement
  RULE: If IRestoreDrillService (F722) DrillResult.rto_actual_minutes exceeds
        DrillResult.rto_target_minutes, the drill is marked FAILED and promotion
        eligibility is NOT granted. RTO target is configurable per tier in FREEDOM config.
  ENTITIES: F722, F723 (FLOW-19), promotion pipeline T281/T284
  VIOLATION: Promotion granted even though RTO exceeded target
  DETECTION: T284 DR Evidence Gate checks DrillResult.rto_actual vs target
  RESOLUTION: Component owner must optimize restore process before next drill
  SEVERITY: ERROR — blocks promotion until passing drill exists

CF-351: Backup Retention Alignment with Tier Policy
  RULE: Backup artifact retention_until date set by IBackupAdapterService (F721)
        MUST match the component's tier retention policy stored in F698 profile.
        Tier-1: 30 days minimum. Tier-2: 14 days. Tier-3: 7 days.
  ENTITIES: F721, F698 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Backup retention < tier policy minimum
  DETECTION: F721 reads tier from F698 profile before setting retention_until
  RESOLUTION: Extend retention to policy minimum; alert if already expired
  SEVERITY: ERROR
```

---

### GROUP E: GITOPS/DEPLOY SAFETY (CF-352-CF-355) — 4 rules

```
CF-352: DR Evidence Required for Tier-1 Production Promotion
  RULE: Any component with criticality=tier1 requires passing DrillEvidence
        (from F723) within 7 days before production promotion. T284 enforces this.
        No exceptions without explicit security-team override event.
  ENTITIES: F723, F712, T281, T284 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Tier-1 component promoted to production without fresh drill evidence
  DETECTION: T284 gate is mandatory in T281 for tier1 components
  RESOLUTION: Run DR drill (T283) and await passing evidence
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL — BUILD FAILURE (DR-118)

CF-353: Manifest Immutability Post-Deploy
  RULE: After GitOps sync confirms Synced state, the deployed manifest reference
        (commit SHA) is immutable for that environment until next planned deployment.
        Ad-hoc manifest mutations outside GitOps are a drift violation (CF-338).
  ENTITIES: F713, F714 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Deployed manifest modified outside GitOps reconciliation cycle
  DETECTION: F714 IDriftDetectorService periodic scan
  RESOLUTION: Drift alert; optional auto-revert depending on env profile
  SEVERITY: ERROR (production) / WARNING (ephemeral)

CF-354: Secret Rotation Before Production Promotion
  RULE: ISecretRefValidatorService (F706) must confirm that secret references
        in production ConfigBundle are not past their rotation schedule before
        production promotion is approved.
  ENTITIES: F706, F712 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Production promotion with stale (past rotation schedule) secrets
  DETECTION: F706.CheckRotationScheduleAsync called by T281 before approve
  RESOLUTION: Trigger secret rotation; re-validate; re-promote
  SEVERITY: ERROR — blocks production promotion

CF-355: Pipeline Contract Version Lock
  RULE: A published pipeline contract version (IPipelineContractService F709)
        is immutable. Updating a pipeline to a new contract version requires
        creating a new contract version and explicitly migrating the pipeline.
        No in-place mutation of published contracts.
  ENTITIES: F709 (FLOW-19)
  VIOLATION: Published contract version mutated in place
  DETECTION: F709 validates version immutability on update attempt
  RESOLUTION: Reject update; create new version; migrate pipeline
  SEVERITY: ERROR
```

---

## STRESS TESTS — ST-184-ST-198

```
ST-184: Parallel Ephemeral Env Storm
  SCENARIO: 20 PRs open simultaneously → 20 env.requested events fire concurrently
  EXPECTED: IaC concurrency limit (CF-329) queues excess applies; all 20 envs eventually
            reach Ready state; no duplicate namespace creation (idempotency DR-114)
            no tenant cross-contamination (CF-345)
  FACTORIES: F701, F702, F703
  BFA RULES: CF-329, CF-344, CF-345
  PASS CRITERIA: All 20 envs reach Ready within 3× single-env TTL; zero cross-tenant leaks

ST-185: Config Secret Ref Validation Failure
  SCENARIO: Deploy attempted with 3 of 8 required secret refs missing from vault
  EXPECTED: T274 fails with DataProcessResult.Error listing missing refs
            provisioning blocked (CF-331); alert sent to component owner
  FACTORIES: F705, F706
  BFA RULES: CF-331, CF-336
  PASS CRITERIA: Zero provisioning started; all 3 missing refs identified in error result

ST-186: Sensitive Data Routing Enforcement
  SCENARIO: Tenant with data_classification=sensitive requests AI-assisted manifest
             rendering (F716) that would route to external Claude API
  EXPECTED: PolicyDecision.Deny with "route-to-local-only" obligation (CF-343)
            F716 resolves to local AI provider via AI ENGINE FABRIC config
            audit event logged
  FACTORIES: F707, F716
  BFA RULES: CF-343
  PASS CRITERIA: External AI provider NEVER called; local provider used; audit logged

ST-187: IaC Partial Failure + Compensation
  SCENARIO: IaC apply succeeds for 60% of resources, then fails (quota exceeded)
  EXPECTED: F702 compensation plan executes (destroys 60%); env reaches Deleted
            no orphaned resources remain; StepRun state preserved for each resource
            rollback verified via cloud API
  FACTORIES: F701, F702
  BFA RULES: CF-329
  PASS CRITERIA: All created resources destroyed; env state = Deleted; audit complete

ST-188: GitOps Drift Detection
  SCENARIO: K8s operator modifies a ConfigMap outside GitOps cycle post-deploy
  EXPECTED: F714 detects drift within scan interval; drift.detected event emitted
            T278 blocks subsequent promotion for affected component (CF-338)
            drift acknowledged before promotion allowed
  FACTORIES: F713, F714, F715
  BFA RULES: CF-338, CF-353
  PASS CRITERIA: Drift detected within 1 scan interval; promotion blocked until acknowledged

ST-189: DR Drill Full Lifecycle
  SCENARIO: Tier-1 component triggers weekly restore drill
  EXPECTED: T282 backs up; T283 provisions sandbox → restores → smoke passes
            DrillResult.rto_actual ≤ rto_target; evidence stored in ES WORM (CF-349)
            sandbox destroyed; promotion eligibility updated (CF-352)
  FACTORIES: F721, F722, F723, F724, F718
  BFA RULES: CF-348, CF-349, CF-350, CF-352
  PASS CRITERIA: Complete drill in < rto_target; evidence immutable; sandbox destroyed

ST-190: RTO Exceeded — Drill Fails
  SCENARIO: Restore takes 2× RTO target due to large data volume
  EXPECTED: DrillResult.rto_actual > rto_target → drill FAILED; evidence stored (FAILED)
            promotion eligibility NOT granted (CF-350); owner alerted
            next drill scheduled immediately
  FACTORIES: F722, F723
  BFA RULES: CF-350, CF-352
  PASS CRITERIA: Promotion blocked; FAILED evidence stored immutably; alert sent

ST-191: Cross-Tenant Binding Isolation
  SCENARIO: Two tenants (A and B) onboarded in parallel with shared schema isolation
  EXPECTED: Each tenant gets unique binding (CF-346); no binding overlap
            all A queries include tenantId=A only; B queries tenantId=B only
            Redis cache keys are tenant-prefixed (CF-344)
  FACTORIES: F728, F729, F730, F732
  BFA RULES: CF-344, CF-346
  PASS CRITERIA: Zero cross-tenant data access; binding uniqueness validated

ST-192: Tenant Onboarding Rollback
  SCENARIO: Tenant onboarding fails at step 5 (quota allocation error)
  EXPECTED: F730 compensation runs in reverse: remove quotas → remove config →
            remove binding → remove identity mapping → delete tenant record
            no partial tenant visible in registry; idempotency key still valid for retry
  FACTORIES: F730, F728, F729, F732
  BFA RULES: CF-347
  PASS CRITERIA: No partial tenant state; retry with same Idempotency-Key succeeds

ST-193: Pipeline Contract Missing Phase
  SCENARIO: GitHub Actions pipeline submitted with security-scan phase removed
  EXPECTED: T276 validation detects missing mandatory phase (CF, IR-276-1)
            gate result = FAIL reported to PR status; build blocked
            artifact NOT registered (no package phase completed)
  FACTORIES: F709, F710
  BFA RULES: CF-334
  PASS CRITERIA: PR status = failed; missing phase identified in gate result

ST-194: Promotion Without Readiness Report
  SCENARIO: Attempt to promote directly to production bypassing test suite
  EXPECTED: T281 checks F719 — no ReadinessReport found for FlowInstance
            promotion BLOCKED (CF-334, IR-281-1); audit event logged
            requester receives explicit "readiness report required" response
  FACTORIES: F712, F719
  BFA RULES: CF-334
  PASS CRITERIA: Promotion blocked; clear error message; audit logged

ST-195: Secret in Component Descriptor
  SCENARIO: Developer commits API key directly into catalog-info.yaml
  EXPECTED: F697 AI-powered content scan detects secret pattern (CF-336)
            ingestion BLOCKED with StoredInvalid result; alert sent to repo owner
            secret NOT stored in ES catalog; audit event logged
  FACTORIES: F697
  BFA RULES: CF-336
  PASS CRITERIA: Secret never reaches ES; owner alerted within 30 seconds

ST-196: Tenant Offboarding — Audit Log Preservation
  SCENARIO: Full tenant offboarding requested for tenant with 3 years of audit history
  EXPECTED: F733 offboarding saga purges tenant data per retention policy
            F726 audit log rows for this tenant PRESERVED (CF not deleted — DR-120)
            F723 drill evidence PRESERVED (DR-119); billing finalized
            all tenant credentials revoked; binding removed from cache (CF-346)
  FACTORIES: F733, F726, F723, F729
  BFA RULES: CF-346, CF-349
  PASS CRITERIA: Tenant data purged; audit logs intact; drill evidence intact; billing closed

ST-197: Catalog Dependency Graph Circular Detection
  SCENARIO: Developer commits descriptor A → depends on B → depends on A
  EXPECTED: T270 graph refresh detects circular dependency on F699
            circular_dependency.detected event emitted; alert to owners of A and B
            promotion blocked for components A and B until resolved (CF-330)
  FACTORIES: F699, F700
  BFA RULES: CF-330
  PASS CRITERIA: Circular detected; both components' promotions blocked; alert sent

ST-198: Multi-Flow Integration — FLOW-05 Gamification in Pipeline
  SCENARIO: Pipeline deploys service that includes FLOW-05 gamification factories (F176-F224)
  EXPECTED: F709 IPipelineContractService detects FLOW-05 scope (CF-342)
            T279 integration suite includes gamification event contract tests
            FLOW-05 event replay verified; ReadinessReport includes FLOW-05 gate result
  FACTORIES: F709, F710, F720
  BFA RULES: CF-342
  PASS CRITERIA: Gamification event contract test included and passes
```

## CONFLICT RULES — CF-356 through CF-379

### CF-356 — Graph Permission vs. Cross-Flow Entity Overlap
```
CONFLICT:    T287 (Graph Read Gate) returns nodes also managed by FLOW-04 (Post Publishing)
             and FLOW-02 (Social Graph). Permission filter (F756) must not expose
             FLOW-04 draft posts or FLOW-02 pending connection requests via graph paths.
DETECTION:   Entity namespace scan: graph node types (Post, User, Group) overlap with
             FLOW-02/FLOW-04 entity registrations in BFA index.
RESOLUTION:  T287 read path applies FLOW-04 publication status gate before returning Post nodes.
             FLOW-02 pending edges are excluded from T287 edge traversal until confirmed.
ENFORCED BY: F756 IPermissionDecisionService; DR-124 partial-error format
AFFECTED T:  T287, T288
SEVERITY:    HIGH — privacy violation if draft/pending state leaks via Graph API
```

### CF-357 — Graph Write vs. FLOW-04 Content Moderation Pipeline
```
CONFLICT:    T288 (Graph Write Gate) post.created event and FLOW-04 ContentPublished event
             are emitted to the same QUEUE FABRIC topic by different services.
             Consumer group confusion = duplicate moderation triggers.
DETECTION:   QUEUE event topic scan: T288 mutation event topic matches FLOW-04
             content_published event schema (same entity type: Post).
RESOLUTION:  T288 uses topic "graph.mutation.v1"; FLOW-04 uses topic "content.published.v1".
             Separate consumer groups. BFA index registers both; schema compat validated.
ENFORCED BY: F764 IWebhookDeliveryService; FLOW-04 queue schema registry
AFFECTED T:  T288, T289
SEVERITY:    MEDIUM — duplicate moderation is operationally wasteful but not data-corrupting
```

### CF-358 — Webhook HMAC Key vs. FLOW-08 Notification Secret
```
CONFLICT:    FLOW-08 (Multi-Tenant Platform) uses HMAC secrets for internal notification
             signing. T289 (Webhook Delivery) uses HMAC for outbound developer webhooks.
             Same HMAC service (F764) — key namespace collision possible.
DETECTION:   HMAC key prefix scan: FLOW-08 uses prefix "notif:" and FLOW-20 uses "webhook:".
             No collision currently; BFA flags if new FLOW uses bare key (no prefix).
RESOLUTION:  F764 enforces scoped key namespaces: "webhook:{appId}:{subscriptionId}".
             FLOW-08 continues using "notif:{tenantId}:{channelId}". No overlap.
ENFORCED BY: DR-125; SK-177 (HMAC delivery pattern)
AFFECTED T:  T289
SEVERITY:    LOW — namespace separation enforced; monitor key registration for bare keys
```

### CF-359 — OAuth Scope Registry vs. FLOW-08 Permission Schema
```
CONFLICT:    F748 (IScopeEnforcementService) defines platform scopes (read, write, ads:*).
             FLOW-08 defines tenant permission schema (ROLE_ADMIN, ROLE_ANALYST).
             Scope ≠ role; conflation = privilege escalation path.
DETECTION:   Permission check code scan: any service that maps OAuth scope directly to
             FLOW-08 tenant role is flagged.
RESOLUTION:  OAuth scopes authorize API capabilities; FLOW-08 roles authorize tenant resources.
             F748 enforces scope; FLOW-08 F247 enforces role. Never substitute one for other.
ENFORCED BY: F748, F747 (app review); DR-123 (scope catalog)
AFFECTED T:  T302, T287, T288
SEVERITY:    HIGH — scope/role conflation = cross-tenant privilege escalation
```

### CF-360 — Ad Account Schema vs. FLOW-13 Finance Billing Entity
```
CONFLICT:    F769 (IBillingAccountService) in FLOW-20 manages ad billing accounts.
             FLOW-13 (Enterprise Finance) has its own billing entity schema (F386).
             Overlapping field names (account_id, currency, billing_cycle) with different
             semantics cause BFA index false matches.
DETECTION:   Entity schema scan: F769 "billing_account" vs FLOW-13 "billing_entity" —
             field overlap detected; semantic annotation required.
RESOLUTION:  BFA registers distinct entity types: "ads_billing_account" (F769) vs
             "finance_billing_entity" (FLOW-13 F386). No cross-reference without explicit join.
ENFORCED BY: F838 audit log annotation; DR-126
AFFECTED T:  T290, T296, T303
SEVERITY:    MEDIUM — schema confusion; no data corruption if namespaced correctly
```

### CF-361 — PCI Tokenization vs. FLOW-13 Payment Vault
```
CONFLICT:    T290 (Payment Method Registration) tokenizes via F773 (external vault).
             FLOW-13 also uses external payment vault for enterprise billing (F409).
             Two vault clients = two token namespaces = reconciliation gap.
DETECTION:   Factory registry scan: F773 and F409 both resolve to payment vault fabric.
             Different token prefix schemas detected.
RESOLUTION:  Single vault client (F773) for all payment tokenization. FLOW-13 F409 updated
             to delegate to F773. Token prefix unified: "vault:{provider}:{last4}".
ENFORCED BY: DR-126 (PCI boundary); CF-360 entity separation
AFFECTED T:  T290
SEVERITY:    HIGH — dual vault = PCI scope doubles; consolidation required
```

### CF-362 — Per-Tenant Quota vs. FLOW-08 Shared Rate Limiter
```
CONFLICT:    T298 (Tenant Quota Gate) uses per-tenant Redis counters (DD-176).
             FLOW-08 uses a shared rate limiter (F254) for internal service calls.
             FLOW-20 services calling FLOW-08 APIs bypass T298 per-tenant accounting.
DETECTION:   Internal service call trace: FLOW-20 → FLOW-08 without T298 quota decrement.
RESOLUTION:  Internal service-to-service calls use system identity token; exempt from
             per-tenant quota. Quota applies only to external developer app calls.
             T298 FREEDOM config has "internal_exempt: true" flag.
ENFORCED BY: F849 IQuotaEnforcementService; F846 tenant edge resolver
AFFECTED T:  T298, T300
SEVERITY:    LOW — internal exemption is correct; document and enforce via config
```

### CF-363 — Schema Registry vs. FLOW-14 Data Warehouse Schema Catalog
```
CONFLICT:    F740 (ISchemaRegistryService) maintains graph object schemas for API use.
             FLOW-14 (Data Warehouse) has its own schema catalog (F439 ISchemaDriftService).
             Both use Elasticsearch; potential index name collision.
DETECTION:   ES index name scan: "schema_registry" vs "schema_catalog_v1" — no collision
             BUT both listen to schema change events on the same internal event topic.
RESOLUTION:  Distinct ES indices; distinct event topics: "graph.schema.updated" vs
             "dw.schema.drift.detected". No consumer group overlap.
ENFORCED BY: F740, F439; BFA cross-flow topic registry
AFFECTED T:  T299
SEVERITY:    LOW — naming separation sufficient; monitor topic subscriptions
```

### CF-364 — Auction Redis State vs. FLOW-05 Gamification Counters
```
CONFLICT:    T292 (Ad Auction) uses Redis INCR for frequency caps (F798) and pacing (F799).
             FLOW-05 (Gamification) uses Redis INCR for point scoring and streak counters.
             Same Redis instance — key namespace collision risk at scale.
DETECTION:   Redis key prefix scan: F798 uses "fc:{campaignId}:{userId}" and FLOW-05
             uses "gam:{userId}:{metric}" — no collision detected currently.
RESOLUTION:  F798/F799 enforce prefix "ads:{type}:{id}". FLOW-05 enforces "gam:{type}:{id}".
             BFA monitors key prefix registration; bare key usage = conflict escalation.
ENFORCED BY: DR-130 (Redis-only auction state); SK-178 (auction Redis pattern)
AFFECTED T:  T292
SEVERITY:    LOW — prefix isolation sufficient; test with 100M key scale test (ST-210)
```

### CF-365 — Budget Decrement vs. FLOW-13 Financial Ledger Transaction
```
CONFLICT:    T292 async budget decrement (F811 → Redis INCR) and T296 spend billing
             (F830 → PG append) are eventually consistent. FLOW-13 financial reconciliation
             (F386) expects strong consistency across billing records.
DETECTION:   Cross-service consistency model scan: FLOW-20 eventual (Redis → PG async)
             FLOW-13 expects transactional (PG synchronous).
RESOLUTION:  FLOW-13 reconciliation reads from F830 PG ledger only (strongly consistent).
             Redis budget counter is a fast approximation; PG ledger is the source of truth.
             T303 (Financial Reconciliation) is the reconciliation bridge.
ENFORCED BY: DD-166; DD-169; DR-130; T303 reconciliation gate
AFFECTED T:  T292, T296, T303
SEVERITY:    MEDIUM — eventual consistency gap must be bounded; reconciliation SLA defined
```

### CF-366 — Consent Gate vs. FLOW-08 GDPR Consent Manager
```
CONFLICT:    T301 (Consent Verification Gate) reads consent state from F793/F746.
             FLOW-08 (Multi-Tenant) has a GDPR Consent Manager (F258) that is the
             authoritative consent store for the platform. Two consent stores = split truth.
DETECTION:   Factory dependency scan: F746 (FLOW-20) and F258 (FLOW-08) both store
             user consent records. Same userId → different consent state possible.
RESOLUTION:  F746 is a FLOW-20 wrapper that delegates to FLOW-08 F258 as authoritative.
             F793 Redis cache is warmed from F258. Single truth = FLOW-08 F258.
ENFORCED BY: DD-167; DR-128; SK-180 (consent gate pattern)
AFFECTED T:  T301, T292
SEVERITY:    HIGH — consent split truth = GDPR violation risk
```

### CF-367 — Political Ad Verification vs. FLOW-08 Identity Verification
```
CONFLICT:    T295 political ad dual gate uses F806 (IPoliticalVerificationService).
             FLOW-08 identity verification (F255) handles general identity checks.
             Political verification is a specialized sub-case of identity verification
             risk of bypassing F806 by routing through F255.
DETECTION:   Service call path analysis: any political ad path that uses F255 directly
             instead of F806 is flagged.
RESOLUTION:  F806 explicitly wraps F255 for identity component + adds political-specific
             verification records. No bypass path exists. IR-295-2 enforced.
ENFORCED BY: DD-168; DR-129; IR-295-2
AFFECTED T:  T295
SEVERITY:    HIGH — regulatory violation if political gate bypassed
```

### CF-368 — Creative Review Status vs. FLOW-03 Content Moderation Status
```
CONFLICT:    T295 (Ad Review Gate) sets creative status in F801 (IAdCatalogService).
             FLOW-03 (Content Moderation) has its own moderation_status field for posts.
             Both statuses apply to some content (sponsored posts) — conflicting values possible.
DETECTION:   Entity field overlap scan: F801 "approval_status" vs FLOW-03 "moderation_status"
             for entity type "sponsored_post" — overlap detected.
RESOLUTION:  Sponsored posts have BOTH fields. F801 governs ad eligibility; FLOW-03
             governs platform publication. Both must be "approved/passed" for delivery.
             T292 checks F801 status only; FLOW-03 gate is upstream of ad slot injection.
ENFORCED BY: DD-178; IR-291-1; CF-357 (event separation)
AFFECTED T:  T291, T295, T292
SEVERITY:    MEDIUM — dual status is correct behaviour; document dependency order
```

### CF-369 — Fraud Gate vs. FLOW-14 Data Quality Gate
```
CONFLICT:    T293/T294 fraud gate (F822/F823) quarantines events before billing.
             FLOW-14 (Data Warehouse) data quality gate (F441 IDataQualityService) also
             evaluates event records for warehouse ingestion.
             Quarantined events should NOT enter the data warehouse (even for analytics).
DETECTION:   Event routing analysis: fraud quarantine DLQ events must not be consumed by
             FLOW-14 ingestion consumer group.
RESOLUTION:  Quarantined events routed to "ads.fraud.quarantine.v1" DLQ topic.
             FLOW-14 ingestion subscribes to "ads.impression.v1" and "ads.click.v1" only.
             Separate topic = no DW contamination.
ENFORCED BY: DD-177; SK-183 (fraud gate pattern); F828 quarantine service
AFFECTED T:  T293, T294, T305
SEVERITY:    HIGH — fraud events in DW = polluted analytics; advertiser insights corrupted
```

### CF-370 — Spend Ledger vs. FLOW-13 General Ledger
```
CONFLICT:    T296 spend ledger (F830) is an ad-specific append-only table.
             FLOW-13 general ledger (F386) is the enterprise financial record.
             Period-end reconciliation (T303) must sync these without float-counting.
DETECTION:   Schema cross-reference: F830 "spend_ledger" vs F386 "general_ledger" —
             both have amount/currency/timestamp fields; join key undefined.
RESOLUTION:  T303 (Financial Reconciliation) is the explicit bridge: reads F830,
             emits summary journal entries to FLOW-13 F386 via queue event.
             F830 never writes directly to F386. One-way sync with audit trail.
ENFORCED BY: DD-169; DR-131; T303 engine contract
AFFECTED T:  T296, T303
SEVERITY:    HIGH — float-count in F386 = financial misstatement
```

### CF-371 — Developer Analytics vs. FLOW-09 Search Analytics
```
CONFLICT:    T306 (Developer Analytics Aggregator) stores API usage metrics in F835.
             FLOW-09 (Search & Discovery) stores search query analytics in its own ES index.
             Both serve "analytics dashboards" — risk of index confusion in shared ES.
DETECTION:   ES index name scan: F835 "developer_analytics_v1" vs FLOW-09 "search_analytics_v1".
             No collision; different index names confirmed.
RESOLUTION:  No conflict. BFA registers both indices; confirms no cross-consumer.
             Developer analytics is tenant-scoped (per app); search analytics is content-scoped.
ENFORCED BY: F835, F844; FLOW-09 search analytics service
AFFECTED T:  T306
SEVERITY:    LOW — confirmed no conflict; annotate for future index sprawl monitoring
```

### CF-372 — Flow Version Immutability vs. FLOW-14 Data Pipeline Versioning
```
CONFLICT:    T297 (Flow Version Publish Gate) creates immutable flow spec snapshots (F839).
             FLOW-14 pipeline versions (F439 schema drift) also version transformation logic.
             Both use "version" concept but different immutability guarantees.
DETECTION:   Version document schema scan: F839 "flow_version" and F439 "pipeline_version"
             have same field names (version_id, spec, status) with different mutation rules.
RESOLUTION:  Distinct ES indices with distinct mutation policies. F839 index has
             "put_only" mapping (no update). F439 allows schema drift records.
             BFA confirms each index has its own write policy.
ENFORCED BY: DD-173; DR-132; IR-297-2
AFFECTED T:  T297
SEVERITY:    LOW — naming distinction sufficient; policy enforcement via ES mapping
```

### CF-373 — Audit Log vs. FLOW-13 Compliance Audit Trail
```
CONFLICT:    F838 (IAuditLogService) in FLOW-20 logs Graph API and Ads events.
             FLOW-13 (Enterprise Finance) has its own compliance audit trail (F404).
             Both append to Elasticsearch; both require int retention.
DETECTION:   ES index audit trail overlap: F838 "audit_log_v1" vs F404 "compliance_audit_v1".
             Distinct indices; BUT both emit audit events on the same internal event bus.
RESOLUTION:  Distinct topics: "audit.graph_ads.v1" (F838) vs "audit.finance.v1" (F404).
             No consumer group overlap. Retention policy per index via F840 governance.
ENFORCED BY: F838; F840 IGovernancePolicyService; DR-122
AFFECTED T:  T287, T288, T290, T291, T295, T296, T302, T303
SEVERITY:    LOW — topic separation sufficient
```

### CF-374 — Tenant Edge Resolver vs. FLOW-08 Tenant Provisioning
```
CONFLICT:    F846 (ITenantEdgeResolverService) resolves tenantId from token claims at the
             Graph API edge. FLOW-08 (Tenant Provisioning) is the authoritative tenant
             registry. Token claim tenantId must match FLOW-08 tenant record.
DETECTION:   Token validation path: F846 validates tenantId claim against FLOW-08 registry.
             If FLOW-08 tenant is suspended/deleted, F846 must reject resolution.
RESOLUTION:  F846 validates token claim against FLOW-08 F244 (ITenantRegistryService).
             Suspended or deleted tenant → F846 returns empty scope context → all requests 403.
ENFORCED BY: DD-172; IR-298-1; DR-133
AFFECTED T:  T287, T288, T292, T298, all FLOW-20 tasks
SEVERITY:    HIGH — stale tenant token = access for suspended tenant
```

### CF-375 — Attribution Model vs. FLOW-04 Post Analytics
```
CONFLICT:    T293 impression attribution credits ad touchpoints to conversion.
             FLOW-04 (Post Publishing) tracks organic post reach as an analytics event.
             Both track "user saw content → user converted" path.
             Risk of ad attribution crediting organic reach as a paid touchpoint.
DETECTION:   Touchpoint event schema scan: F812 impression event vs FLOW-04 reach event —
             same userId + contentId combination possible for organic + paid content.
RESOLUTION:  Ad impressions have mandatory is_paid=true flag in event schema.
             Attribution engine (F818) only credits touchpoints where is_paid=true.
             Organic reach events excluded from paid attribution path by schema filter.
ENFORCED BY: F812; F818; IR-293-1 (fraud gate before billing)
AFFECTED T:  T293, T294
SEVERITY:    MEDIUM — attribution pollution = advertiser overspend; schema gate sufficient
```

### CF-376 — App OAuth Token vs. FLOW-08 Internal Service Token
```
CONFLICT:    F744 (ITokenIssuanceService) issues OAuth tokens for developer apps.
             FLOW-08 issues internal service identity tokens for service-to-service calls.
             Both use JWT format; token validation must distinguish app vs service tokens.
DETECTION:   Token type header scan: developer app tokens use "typ": "at+JWT"
             internal service tokens use "typ": "service+JWT" (FLOW-08 convention).
RESOLUTION:  F748 scope enforcement checks token type header before scope validation.
             Service tokens bypass developer rate limiting (CF-362) but are not valid
             for user-context API calls (Graph Read/Write).
ENFORCED BY: F744; F748; CF-362 (internal exemption)
AFFECTED T:  T302, T287, T288
SEVERITY:    HIGH — service token used for user-context API = privilege escalation
```

### CF-377 — Creative Asset Storage vs. FLOW-04 Media Storage
```
CONFLICT:    F776 (ICreativeAssetService) stores ad creatives in object store + ES metadata.
             FLOW-04 (Post Publishing) stores post media in the same object store.
             Same storage namespace = ad creative accessible via post media path (or vice versa).
DETECTION:   Object store path scan: F776 uses path "ads/creatives/{accountId}/{assetId}".
             FLOW-04 uses "content/media/{userId}/{mediaId}". No collision currently.
RESOLUTION:  Namespace separation enforced at storage layer. F776 read permission scoped
             to "ads/" prefix only. FLOW-04 read permission scoped to "content/" only.
             Cross-prefix read = BUILD FAILURE.
ENFORCED BY: F776; DR-126; AF-8 security scan
AFFECTED T:  T291
SEVERITY:    LOW — path separation enforced; periodic storage audit recommended
```

### CF-378 — Spend Forecast vs. FLOW-13 Cash Flow Forecast
```
CONFLICT:    F837 (ISpendForecastService) generates ad spend projections for advertisers.
             FLOW-13 (Finance Engine) generates cash flow forecasts for the platform (F396).
             Both use AI ENGINE FABRIC for projection; different data inputs/horizons.
DETECTION:   AI model usage scan: F837 and F396 both call IAiProvider.GenerateAsync()
             with "forecast" task type. Prompt collision risk if shared model context.
RESOLUTION:  F837 uses task_type="ads_spend_forecast"; F396 uses task_type="finance_cashflow".
             Distinct model contexts, distinct output schemas. No shared context window.
ENFORCED BY: F837; FLOW-13 F396; AI ENGINE FABRIC task_type routing
AFFECTED T:  T303
SEVERITY:    LOW — task_type isolation sufficient
```

### CF-379 — Attribution Window Config vs. FLOW-05 Reward Expiry Config
```
CONFLICT:    T304 (Attribution Window Config Gate) sets per-advertiser time windows (F829).
             FLOW-05 (Gamification) sets reward expiry windows (F199).
             Both are "time window FREEDOM configs" stored in Elasticsearch.
             FREEDOM config key namespace collision possible.
DETECTION:   ES FREEDOM config key scan: F829 uses "attribution_config:{accountId}" and
             F199 uses "reward_expiry:{userId}". No collision; different key schemas.
RESOLUTION:  No conflict. BFA annotates both for future FREEDOM config sprawl monitoring.
             Governance policy (F840) tracks all FREEDOM config namespaces.
ENFORCED BY: DD-179; F840 IGovernancePolicyService; DR-133
AFFECTED T:  T304
SEVERITY:    LOW — confirmed no collision; register both in FREEDOM config manifest
```

## STRESS TESTS — ST-199 through ST-214

### ST-199 — Graph Read Fan-out Under Load
```
STRESS TEST: ST-199
TARGET:      T287 (Graph Read Gate) + F742 (QueryPlanner) + F757 (DomainFederator)
SCENARIO:    10,000 concurrent graph read requests each requesting 5 nested edge hops
             across 4 domain services (Profile, Posts, Media, Social Graph).
LOAD:        10,000 RPS for 60 seconds; 5-hop depth; 4 domain services per request.
EXPECTED:    p99 < 200ms; depth limit enforced (F734 FREEDOM config = 3 hops max)
             requests exceeding depth return partial-error (not 400); no total failures.
FAILURE MODE: Domain service cascade failure — F757 allSettled ensures partial result,
              not total failure. One service timeout = partial response.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - p99 latency < 200ms
  - Depth > 3 hops: partial-error response with errors array, not 400
  - Domain service failure: partial result returned, not HTTP 500
  - F758/F759 cache hit rate > 80% for hot nodes
RECOVERY:    F758/F759 cache TTL reduced on failure; F757 timeout per domain service
             configurable via FREEDOM config.
SAVE POINT:  ST-199:COMPLETE
```

### ST-200 — Permission Filter at Scale (10M Nodes)
```
STRESS TEST: ST-200
TARGET:      T287 + F756 (PermissionDecisionService) per-node/field/edge
SCENARIO:    Single graph request returns 10,000 nodes; F756 called per node + per field.
             Mixed authorization: 60% authorized, 30% partially authorized, 10% unauthorized.
LOAD:        1,000 such requests concurrent; F756 = 10,000 decisions per request.
EXPECTED:    p99 < 500ms total; partial-auth nodes appear in errors array; no unauthorized
             field leaks (AF-8 verified); F756 Redis cache hit rate > 95%.
FAILURE MODE: F756 overload → cache miss storm → PG overload. Mitigation: circuit breaker.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - No unauthorized field in any response (automated field leak scanner)
  - errors array correctly populated for partial-auth nodes
  - p99 < 500ms with 10,000-node responses
  - F756 Redis cache hit > 95% (hot nodes cached correctly)
SAVE POINT:  ST-200:COMPLETE
```

### ST-201 — Webhook Delivery Retry Storm
```
STRESS TEST: ST-201
TARGET:      T289 (Webhook Delivery Orchestrator) + F763 (DeliveryRetryService)
SCENARIO:    All subscribed developer endpoints return 500 for 10 minutes.
             1,000 active subscriptions × 50 events/minute = 50,000 pending retries.
LOAD:        50,000 retry queue entries; exponential backoff; DLQ after 5 attempts.
EXPECTED:    No event dropped silently; DLQ populated after 5 attempts; subscription
             marked FAILED after DLQ; developer dashboard shows FAILED status.
FAILURE MODE: Redis Streams backpressure → consumer lag spikes → memory pressure.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Every event appears exactly once in delivery log or DLQ (no silent drop)
  - Subscription status = FAILED after 5 exhausted attempts
  - Retry backoff: attempt timings match 2^n × 1000ms ± 20% jitter
  - No duplicate delivery on retry (F765 dedup verified)
SAVE POINT:  ST-201:COMPLETE
```

### ST-202 — Ad Auction p99 Latency Under Concurrency
```
STRESS TEST: ST-202
TARGET:      T292 (Ad Auction Orchestrator) + F810 (AuctionEngineService) Redis path
SCENARIO:    50,000 concurrent auction requests (feed load for 5M active users).
             Each request: 20 eligible candidates, quality scoring, freq cap, pacing, winner.
LOAD:        50,000 RPS sustained for 120 seconds.
EXPECTED:    p99 < 50ms (DR-127 SLO); p50 < 20ms; Redis INCR atomic at 50,000 RPS
             no PG/ES call in auction critical path.
FAILURE MODE: Redis connection pool exhaustion → latency spike → SLO breach.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - p99 < 50ms sustained (not just burst)
  - Zero non-Redis fabric calls in auction path (trace verified)
  - No-fill rate < 5% (adequate eligible inventory)
  - Budget decrement events in QUEUE FABRIC within 200ms (async verified)
SAVE POINT:  ST-202:COMPLETE
```

### ST-203 — Consent Withdrawal Propagation Speed
```
STRESS TEST: ST-203
TARGET:      T301 (Consent Verification Gate) + F793 (ConsentLookup) cache invalidation
SCENARIO:    100,000 users withdraw targeting consent simultaneously.
             F746 emits withdrawal events; F793 Redis cache must invalidate within 30s.
LOAD:        100,000 consent withdrawal events in a 60-second window.
EXPECTED:    All F793 caches invalidated within 30s (IR-301-4 SLA).
             Auctions running post-withdrawal must not use stale consent.
FAILURE MODE: Cache invalidation event queue backlog → stale consent in Redis > 30s.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - 99% of F793 caches invalidated within 30s
  - Auction after withdrawal: no targeted ad for withdrawn users (integration trace)
  - F746 PG is authoritative fallback on cache miss (zero mis-authorization)
SAVE POINT:  ST-203:COMPLETE
```

### ST-204 — Creative Review Queue Backlog
```
STRESS TEST: ST-204
TARGET:      T291 (Creative Ingestion) + T295 (Ad Review Gate) + F785 (ReviewQueue)
SCENARIO:    100,000 creatives submitted simultaneously (product launch wave).
             Review queue fills; automated review runs; human escalation triggered for 20%.
LOAD:        100,000 creative ingestion events in 1 hour; 20,000 human escalation tickets.
EXPECTED:    All creatives set PENDING status before any auction eligibility check.
             No creative enters T292 auction before T295 decision recorded.
             Review SLA tracked per priority lane.
FAILURE MODE: F785 queue overflow → some creatives never reach T295 → stuck in PENDING forever.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Zero PENDING creatives in T292 eligible set (integration scan)
  - F785 queue depth monitoring alert at 80% capacity
  - T295 automated decision rate > 80% (human queue bounded)
  - No creative auto-approved on review timeout (IR-295-5)
SAVE POINT:  ST-204:COMPLETE
```

### ST-205 — Fraud Detection Under Click Flood
```
STRESS TEST: ST-205
TARGET:      T294 (Click Attribution Gate) + F823 (ClickFraudService) + F824 (BotDetection)
SCENARIO:    Simulated click flood: 500,000 clicks in 60 seconds from 50 IP addresses.
             Normal baseline: 1,000 clicks/minute from 50,000 unique IPs.
LOAD:        500× baseline click rate from 1/1000th the IP diversity.
EXPECTED:    F824 bot detection fires within 5 seconds; F823 fraud score threshold crossed
             T305 quarantine triggered; zero fraudulent clicks billed.
FAILURE MODE: AI ENGINE FABRIC latency spike under flood → fraud gate bypassed due to timeout.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Zero fraudulent clicks in F830 spend ledger (end-to-end test)
  - F823/F824 detection latency < 500ms at flood rate
  - T305 quarantine records all 500,000 events
  - Advertiser credit issued for quarantined clicks (F831 offset entries)
SAVE POINT:  ST-205:COMPLETE
```

### ST-206 — Spend Ledger Append Under Billing Surge
```
STRESS TEST: ST-206
TARGET:      T296 (Spend Billing Gate) + F830 (SpendLedger) append-only PG
SCENARIO:    Black Friday-level ad delivery: 10M impressions/minute billing surge.
             All impressions pre-fraud-gated (arriving at T296 already clean).
LOAD:        10,000,000 billing events per minute sustained for 30 minutes.
EXPECTED:    F830 append-only inserts sustain 167,000 TPS; no UPDATE operations
             idempotency key prevents duplicate billing on QUEUE redelivery.
FAILURE MODE: PG connection pool saturation → billing events back-queue → budget mismatch.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - 100% billing events inserted (no silent drop)
  - Zero UPDATE operations on spend_ledger (constraint test throughout)
  - Duplicate event_id: exactly one ledger entry (idempotency verified)
  - Budget counter (Redis) matches ledger sum within 0.5% (consistency check)
SAVE POINT:  ST-206:COMPLETE
```

### ST-207 — Multi-Tenant Quota Isolation (Noisy Neighbor)
```
STRESS TEST: ST-207
TARGET:      T298 (Tenant Quota Gate) + F851 (NoisyNeighborGuard)
SCENARIO:    Tenant A sends 100× its quota limit (malicious/buggy client).
             Tenants B through Z run at normal traffic levels simultaneously.
LOAD:        Tenant A: 100,000 RPS (quota = 1,000 RPS); 24 other tenants at normal rate.
EXPECTED:    Tenant A throttled to quota limit (429 responses); tenants B–Z unaffected.
             F851 detects burst, throttles Tenant A within 100ms.
FAILURE MODE: Shared Redis pool saturation from Tenant A → other tenants see latency increase.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Tenants B–Z: p99 latency unchanged (< 5% degradation from baseline)
  - Tenant A: throttled within 100ms; 429 with Retry-After header
  - Per-tenant Redis counters: Tenant A counter isolated (does not affect Tenant B counter)
  - F845 error budget: Tenant A SLO breach does not affect platform SLO
SAVE POINT:  ST-207:COMPLETE
```

### ST-208 — Graph Depth Limit Enforcement at Scale
```
STRESS TEST: ST-208
TARGET:      T299 (Schema Field Projection Gate) + F734 (GraphDepthConfig)
SCENARIO:    Adversarial clients send requests with depth=20 (5× FREEDOM config limit=4).
             100,000 such requests concurrent; each would fan-out exponentially.
LOAD:        100,000 adversarial deep-traversal requests in 60 seconds.
EXPECTED:    All requests return partial-error at depth limit (not 400, not full result)
             fan-out amplification prevented; no domain service overwhelm.
FAILURE MODE: Depth limit check too late in pipeline → partial fan-out to domain services
              before limit enforced → domain service flood.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Depth limit enforced BEFORE F757 fan-out (trace verified)
  - All over-depth responses contain errors array with depth_limit_exceeded error
  - Domain service call count: capped at FREEDOM config depth × breadth
  - FREEDOM config depth change takes effect within 60s (no redeploy)
SAVE POINT:  ST-208:COMPLETE
```

### ST-209 — PCI Tokenization Throughput
```
STRESS TEST: ST-209
TARGET:      T290 (Payment Method Registration Gate) + F773 (PaymentMethodService)
SCENARIO:    10,000 payment method registrations in 1 hour (platform-wide advertiser onboarding).
LOAD:        10,000 tokenization requests; vault latency simulated at p99=2s.
EXPECTED:    Zero raw PAN in any log, queue, or database; all vaulted within SLA
             no orphaned vault tokens (failed binding triggers cleanup).
FAILURE MODE: Vault timeout → orphaned token created, binding not completed.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - PCI scan: zero card_number/CVV in any ES/PG/Redis/queue field
  - Orphaned token cleanup: vault token without binding triggers compensating delete
  - F838 audit: every registration attempt (success/fail) has audit entry
  - Idempotency: same card tokenized twice → two valid tokens (vault decides; no raw re-use)
SAVE POINT:  ST-209:COMPLETE
```

### ST-210 — Multi-Model Quality Score Divergence (DD-175)
```
STRESS TEST: ST-210
TARGET:      T292 + T295 AF-5 multi-model orchestration + AF-10 conservative merge
SCENARIO:    10,000 ad quality scoring runs; synthetic dataset with known correct scores.
             30% of runs designed to produce > 10% model divergence.
LOAD:        10,000 scoring requests; 4 models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek).
EXPECTED:    For divergence > 10%: conservative (lower) score used
             Average of all 4 models < conservative score in 0% of cases.
             For divergence ≤ 10%: standard merge used.
FAILURE MODE: AF-10 uses average instead of conservative → over-optimistic scores →
              low-quality ads win auctions at higher rates.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Divergence > 10%: final score = min(model scores) in 100% of divergent cases
  - Divergence ≤ 10%: final score = weighted average (verified)
  - Model agreement rate tracked to F844 developer analytics for model alignment monitoring
  - Score accuracy vs synthetic ground truth > 90% (model quality baseline)
SAVE POINT:  ST-210:COMPLETE
```

### ST-211 — Webhook HMAC Signature Verification at Volume
```
STRESS TEST: ST-211
TARGET:      T289 (Webhook Delivery) + F764 (WebhookDelivery) HMAC signing
SCENARIO:    1,000,000 webhook deliveries in 24 hours; 100 different developer apps.
             All deliveries must have valid HMAC signature verifiable by app secret.
LOAD:        ~12 deliveries/second sustained; burst to 500/second for 5 minutes.
EXPECTED:    100% of deliveries verifiable by subscriber; zero unsigned deliveries
             burst rate sustained without HMAC signing becoming bottleneck.
FAILURE MODE: HMAC key rotation during delivery window → in-flight deliveries use old key,
              subscriber rejects with new key.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - 100% of deliveries: subscriber HMAC verification passes
  - Key rotation: graceful dual-key window (old + new accepted for 60s transition)
  - Signing throughput: p99 HMAC compute < 1ms (not a latency bottleneck)
  - F765 dedup: zero duplicate delivery of same eventId
SAVE POINT:  ST-211:COMPLETE
```

### ST-212 — Financial Reconciliation Correctness
```
STRESS TEST: ST-212
TARGET:      T303 (Financial Reconciliation Gate) + F830 (SpendLedger) + F831 (Reversals)
SCENARIO:    30-day reconciliation run over 500M billing events
             Intentionally inject 0.05% fraudulent events that reached billing before
             fraud gate (simulating race condition window).
LOAD:        500,000,000 events; 250,000 fraudulent entries requiring offset corrections.
EXPECTED:    T303 detects all 250,000 discrepancies; issues offset corrections
             variance < 0.1% alerts; final reconciled total within $0.01 of correct.
FAILURE MODE: T303 misses some fraud reversals → net overspend → advertiser overcharged.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Detection rate: 100% of injected fraudulent entries identified
  - Offset entries: append-only in F830 (no updates to original entries)
  - Idempotency: reconciliation re-run = same offsets (no float-correction)
  - FLOW-13 F386 general ledger: receives correct summary journal from T303
SAVE POINT:  ST-212:COMPLETE
```

### ST-213 — Platform-Wide Tenant Quota Enforcement Accuracy
```
STRESS TEST: ST-213
TARGET:      T298 + T300 + F849 + F850 + F851 across all 25 tenant profiles
SCENARIO:    25 tenants simultaneously at different traffic levels (5 at quota limit,
             5 above limit, 15 at normal). Run for 1 hour.
LOAD:        Aggregate 50,000 RPS across all tenants; 5 tenants each exceeding quota by 3×.
EXPECTED:    5 over-quota tenants throttled; 15 normal tenants unaffected
             no cross-tenant quota bleeding; F845 error budget accurate per tenant.
FAILURE MODE: Redis per-tenant counter overflow → incorrect throttling of normal tenants.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Normal tenants: zero 429 responses
  - Over-quota tenants: throttled within 100ms of quota breach
  - F845 error budget: per-tenant SLO breach tracking accurate
  - FREEDOM config: quota changes applied within 60s across all 25 tenants
SAVE POINT:  ST-213:COMPLETE
```

### ST-214 — End-to-End Ad Delivery Pipeline (Full Flow Integration)
```
STRESS TEST: ST-214
TARGET:      Full FLOW-20 pipeline: T291 → T295 → T292 → T293/T294 → T296 → T303
SCENARIO:    Production-scale simulation: 1,000 advertisers, 10,000 active campaigns,
             1,000,000 eligible creatives (all pre-approved); 5M feed requests triggering
             auctions over 4 hours.
LOAD:        5,000,000 auctions; 4,000,000 impressions; 200,000 clicks
             10,000 fraud events; 50 political ads (requiring dual gate).
EXPECTED:    All 6 DNA patterns enforced across every service; no typed models
             all factory calls via CreateAsync(); all results DataProcessResult[T]
             spend ledger reconciles with budget decrements within 0.1%.
FAILURE MODE: Any single-service failure cascades to break downstream pipeline.
             MicroserviceBase health checks detect and isolate failures.
PASS CRITERIA:
  - Auction p99 < 50ms (sustained over 4 hours)
  - Fraud quarantine: 100% of 10,000 injected events quarantined before billing
  - Political dual gate: all 50 political ads verified before serving
  - Spend reconciliation: F830 total within 0.1% of F811 budget decrements
  - DNA compliance: AF-7 automated scan passes on all generated services
  - Backward compat: FLOW-01 through FLOW-19 stress tests re-run; all pass unchanged
SAVE POINT:  ST-214:COMPLETE
```
