## BUSINESS FLOW ARBITER (BFA) — CONFLICT RULES CF-380 to CF-401

### GROUP A — Forms Internal Ordering (CF-380–CF-383)

#### CF-380 — Schema Version Before Publish Event
```
RULE ID:    CF-380
CATEGORY:   Forms Internal
TRIGGER:    IFormPublishService.PublishForm() called
CONFLICT:   FORM_PUBLISHED event emitted before version snapshot created
RESOLUTION: F855.CreateVersion() must complete before F856.PublishForm() event emission
RATIONALE:  Version history must be consistent before downstream systems receive
            the published event. Otherwise renders may use outdated schema.
ENFORCED BY: AF-7, IR-307-3
TASK TYPES: T307
```

#### CF-381 — Render Spec Invalidated on Every Publish
```
RULE ID:    CF-381
CATEGORY:   Forms Internal
TRIGGER:    FORM_PUBLISHED event consumed
CONFLICT:   Stale render spec served after schema publish
RESOLUTION: F857.GenerateRenderSpec() called synchronously after FORM_PUBLISHED received
RATIONALE:  Clients must never serve outdated render specs — field config changes
            must be reflected immediately.
ENFORCED BY: AF-7, QG-307-2
TASK TYPES: T307
```

#### CF-382 — Anti-Spam Before Persist
```
RULE ID:    CF-382
CATEGORY:   Forms Internal — submission pipeline ordering
TRIGGER:    Submission pipeline step sequencing
CONFLICT:   F868.PersistEntry() called before F867.CheckSpam() clears
RESOLUTION: T311 (Anti-Spam Gate) must precede T312 (Entry Persistence Gate)
            in all generated flow templates
RATIONALE:  DR-135 — spam entries must never reach ES/PG storage.
            Inline spam check in persist service = DNA violation (pipeline collapse).
ENFORCED BY: AF-7, DR-135, IR-311-1, Template-36 step ordering
TASK TYPES: T311, T312
```

#### CF-383 — Wizard Session Isolated Per Tenant
```
RULE ID:    CF-383
CATEGORY:   Forms Internal — MT isolation
TRIGGER:    IMultiStepWizardService.AdvanceStep() or GetCurrentStep()
CONFLICT:   Wizard session state readable by different tenant
RESOLUTION: F862 Redis key must be namespaced: {tenantId}:wizard:{sessionId}
RATIONALE:  Multi-step form state may contain sensitive field values.
            Cross-tenant session read = data breach.
ENFORCED BY: DNA-5, IR-307-8, F900 (IFormTenantIsolationService)
TASK TYPES: T307, T313
```

---

### GROUP B — Submission Pipeline Ordering (CF-384–CF-387)

#### CF-384 — Intake Deduplication Before Queue Enqueue
```
RULE ID:    CF-384
CATEGORY:   Submission Pipeline
TRIGGER:    ISubmissionIntakeService.IntakeSubmission() called
CONFLICT:   Duplicate submission enqueued without dedup check
RESOLUTION: F864.CheckDuplicate() must be called and return false before EnqueueAsync()
RATIONALE:  QUEUE FABRIC at-least-once delivery + client retries can produce
            duplicate intake events. Dedup at intake is the primary guard.
ENFORCED BY: IR-308-2, SK-190 (stage-gate pattern)
TASK TYPES: T308
```

#### CF-385 — Validation Before Persistence
```
RULE ID:    CF-385
CATEGORY:   Submission Pipeline
TRIGGER:    Entry persistence attempt
CONFLICT:   F868.PersistEntry() called before F866.ValidateSubmission() completes
RESOLUTION: T310 (Validation Gate) precedes T312 (Persistence Gate) — enforced by Template-36
RATIONALE:  Invalid entries must never reach the ES store. Downstream consumers
            (notifications, feeds) assume valid data — corrupt data = cascade failures.
ENFORCED BY: Template-36 step ordering, IR-310-1
TASK TYPES: T310, T312
```

#### CF-386 — Emit After Persist
```
RULE ID:    CF-386
CATEGORY:   Submission Pipeline — persist-before-emit
TRIGGER:    ENTRY_PERSISTED event emission
CONFLICT:   Event emitted before entry written to ES/PG
RESOLUTION: F868.PersistEntry() and F877.LogAction() must complete before QUEUE FABRIC
            event emission in T312
RATIONALE:  DR-134 — downstream consumers (T314, T315) read entry by entryId from ES.
            Event before persist = consumer reads non-existent entry = cascade failure.
ENFORCED BY: DR-134, IR-312-1, INV-15-1
TASK TYPES: T312
```

#### CF-387 — No Partial Entry in Feed Chain
```
RULE ID:    CF-387
CATEGORY:   Submission Pipeline
TRIGGER:    Feed execution trigger
CONFLICT:   T315 (Feed Execution) triggered by partial entry save (T313)
RESOLUTION: Feed chain (T315, T316, T317) only triggered by ENTRY_PERSISTED event,
            never by PARTIAL_ENTRY_SAVED event
RATIONALE:  Partial entries are incomplete — CRM sync with partial data creates
            incomplete contacts. Feeds must only process completed entries.
ENFORCED BY: Template-36, Template-37, F891 trigger index filtering
TASK TYPES: T313, T315
```

---

### GROUP C — Feeds & Notifications (CF-388–CF-391)

#### CF-388 — Feed Execution Idempotency Key Required
```
RULE ID:    CF-388
CATEGORY:   Feeds & Notifications
TRIGGER:    IFeedExecutorService.ExecuteFeed() called
CONFLICT:   Feed executed without idempotency key registration
RESOLUTION: F895.RegisterExecution() must complete before F885.ExecuteFeed() proceeds
RATIONALE:  DR-138 — QUEUE FABRIC at-least-once delivery + event replays will
            re-trigger feeds. Duplicate CRM contact creation = data integrity failure.
ENFORCED BY: DR-138, IR-315-2, INV-15-4, SK-192
TASK TYPES: T315, T316
```

#### CF-389 — Payment Feed Runs Last
```
RULE ID:    CF-389
CATEGORY:   Feeds & Notifications
TRIGGER:    Payment feed in multi-feed submission
CONFLICT:   Payment feed (F889) executes before CRM/mailing list feeds
RESOLUTION: T317 (Payment Feed) sequenced after T315 (Integration Feed)
            in all generated flow templates
RATIONALE:  Payment is irreversible — it must be the last action after all
            other integrations succeed. Partial payment with failed CRM = orphaned payment.
ENFORCED BY: Template-36, IR-317-1
TASK TYPES: T315, T317
```

#### CF-390 — Webhook Retry Idempotent
```
RULE ID:    CF-390
CATEGORY:   Feeds & Notifications
TRIGGER:    IWebhookFeedService retry attempt
CONFLICT:   Same webhook sent twice to external endpoint
RESOLUTION: F895 idempotency key checked before each retry attempt in T316
RATIONALE:  External systems (CRM, Slack, Jira) may not be idempotent —
            duplicate webhook delivery creates duplicate records/notifications.
ENFORCED BY: IR-316-2, QG-316-2
TASK TYPES: T316, T321
```

#### CF-391 — CRM Feed No Duplicate Contact
```
RULE ID:    CF-391
CATEGORY:   Feeds & Notifications
TRIGGER:    ICrmFeedService.SyncContact() called
CONFLICT:   Duplicate CRM contact created for same submitter
RESOLUTION: F887 must check existing contact by email before creating new one.
            Idempotency key = hash(feedId + entryId) prevents duplicate API calls.
ENFORCED BY: IR-315-2, QG-315-1, SK-192
TASK TYPES: T315
```

---

### GROUP D — Recipe Engine (CF-392–CF-394)

#### CF-392 — Trigger Before Execute
```
RULE ID:    CF-392
CATEGORY:   Recipe Engine
TRIGGER:    Recipe execution start
CONFLICT:   Recipe execution started without trigger evaluation + idempotency check
RESOLUTION: T319 (Trigger Evaluation) with F895 idempotency check must precede
            T320 (Step Execution) — enforced by Template-37
RATIONALE:  DR-139 — recipe DAG execution follows Flow Engine pattern (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry)).
            Execution without trigger evaluation = runaway automation.
ENFORCED BY: Template-37 step ordering, IR-319-1, INV-15-4
TASK TYPES: T319, T320
```

#### CF-393 — Retry Via DLQ Only
```
RULE ID:    CF-393
CATEGORY:   Recipe Engine
TRIGGER:    Recipe step failure
CONFLICT:   Inline retry loop in IRecipeExecutionService or IRecipeStepResolverService
RESOLUTION: All retry logic exclusively via F894 + DLQ queue path (T321)
RATIONALE:  Inline retry loops block QUEUE FABRIC consumer thread, reduce throughput,
            and bypass backoff policies. DLQ-based retry is the only safe pattern.
ENFORCED BY: IR-321-1, QG-321-1
TASK TYPES: T320, T321
```

#### CF-394 — Recipe Step Atomic
```
RULE ID:    CF-394
CATEGORY:   Recipe Engine
TRIGGER:    IRecipeExecutionService.AdvanceExecution() called
CONFLICT:   Next step triggered before current step result recorded in run state
RESOLUTION: F893 must write step result to PG run state before emitting next-step event
RATIONALE:  CF-394 mirrors CF-386 (persist-before-emit) for recipe steps.
            Next step consuming stale state = incorrect conditional branching.
ENFORCED BY: IR-320-1, QG-320-2
TASK TYPES: T320
```

---

### GROUP E — Connector Safety (CF-395–CF-397)

#### CF-395 — OAuth Token in PG Encrypted Only
```
RULE ID:    CF-395
CATEGORY:   Connector Safety
TRIGGER:    Any OAuth token write operation
CONFLICT:   OAuth access/refresh token written to ES, Redis, or application log
RESOLUTION: F897.ExchangeCode() and F897.RefreshToken() store tokens only in
            PG with column-level encryption. No other storage path allowed.
RATIONALE:  DR-140 — ES has no column-level encryption. Redis is ephemeral.
            Token in ES = searchable secret. Token in Redis = non-durable + no encryption.
ENFORCED BY: DR-140, IR-322-1, INV-15-5
TASK TYPES: T322
```

#### CF-396 — Mapping Before External Push
```
RULE ID:    CF-396
CATEGORY:   Connector Safety
TRIGGER:    Any feed attempting to push to external system
CONFLICT:   Raw entry Dictionary sent to external API without field mapping
RESOLUTION: T323 (Data Mapping Transform) must precede T315/T316 for all connector feeds
RATIONALE:  External systems have strict schema requirements. Raw form Dictionary
            contains internal field IDs, not external field names. Raw push = API error.
ENFORCED BY: IR-323-4, Template-38 step ordering
TASK TYPES: T323, T315, T316
```

#### CF-397 — Run History Immutable
```
RULE ID:    CF-397
CATEGORY:   Connector Safety
TRIGGER:    Any write to IRunHistoryService
CONFLICT:   Run history record updated or deleted after initial write
RESOLUTION: F899 ES run log is append-only — no update/delete methods exposed
RATIONALE:  Run history is audit trail for connector integrations.
            Mutable run history = compliance violation (cannot investigate failures).
ENFORCED BY: IR-T324-implied, DNA-3 (immutable pattern)
TASK TYPES: T315, T316, T325
```

---

### GROUP F — Multi-Tenant Forms Isolation (CF-398–CF-400)

#### CF-398 — Schema Per-Tenant Namespaced
```
RULE ID:    CF-398
CATEGORY:   MT Forms Isolation
TRIGGER:    Any IFormSchemaService operation
CONFLICT:   Form schema document readable by a different tenant
RESOLUTION: F852 ES index namespaced: {tenantId}-forms-schemas.
            F900 validates namespace on every schema operation.
RATIONALE:  Form schemas may contain business-sensitive field configurations
            (pricing formulas, approval workflows). Cross-tenant read = data leak.
ENFORCED BY: DNA-5, F900, IR-307-5, INV-15-9
TASK TYPES: T307, T326
```

#### CF-399 — Entry RLS at PG Level
```
RULE ID:    CF-399
CATEGORY:   MT Forms Isolation
TRIGGER:    Entry read from PG (audit, export, partial promotion)
CONFLICT:   PG query returns entries from multiple tenants
RESOLUTION: F900.EnforceEntryRLS() applies PG Row Level Security policy.
            All PG entry queries include tenantId in WHERE clause (DNA-5).
RATIONALE:  PG RLS as defense-in-depth — even if application bug omits tenantId,
            PG policy blocks cross-tenant read at database level.
ENFORCED BY: F900, DNA-5, IR-326-3
TASK TYPES: T326, T312, T313
```

#### CF-400 — Connector Credentials Isolated Per Tenant
```
RULE ID:    CF-400
CATEGORY:   MT Forms Isolation
TRIGGER:    Any IOAuthConnectorService operation
CONFLICT:   Connector token/credentials accessible by a different tenant
RESOLUTION: F897 PG token table includes tenantId column with RLS policy.
            F900 validates connector credential isolation on tenant provision.
RATIONALE:  INV-15-10 — connector credential cross-tenant access = integration
            hijack (sending data to wrong CRM, wrong Slack, wrong Stripe account).
ENFORCED BY: DR-140, CF-395, INV-15-10, IR-322-5
TASK TYPES: T322, T326
```

---

### GROUP G — Cross-Flow (CF-401)

#### CF-401 — Forms Entries Never Conflict with DWH Raw Zone
```
RULE ID:    CF-401
CATEGORY:   Cross-Flow (FLOW-21 vs FLOW-14)
TRIGGER:    Any cross-system write involving form entries + DWH raw zone
CONFLICT:   Form entry ES index written to same index as FLOW-14 raw landing zone
RESOLUTION: Form entry ES index: {tenantId}-forms-entries (FLOW-21 namespace)
            DWH raw zone ES index: {tenantId}-dwh-raw (FLOW-14 namespace)
            Both use DATABASE FABRIC but different index namespaces — never shared.
RATIONALE:  FLOW-14 raw zone is immutable (DR-58). If form entries land in raw zone,
            they become subject to DWH zone promotion rules — incorrect pipeline routing.
ENFORCED BY: DR-58 (FLOW-14), F438 (IRawLandingService namespace), F868 namespace
TASK TYPES: T312 (FLOW-21), T169 (FLOW-14)
```

---

## STRESS TESTS (ST-215–ST-226)

### ST-215 — Form Schema Publish Under Concurrent Edit
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-215
TASK TYPES:      T307
FACTORIES:       F852, F855, F856
SCENARIO:
  Two admins simultaneously edit and publish the same form schema.
  Race condition: both create version snapshots, both emit FORM_PUBLISHED events.
LOAD PROFILE:
  2 concurrent publish operations on formId="form-A", tenantId="tenant-1"
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - Only one version snapshot created (F855 optimistic lock on version sequence)
  - Second publish returns DataProcessResult.Failure with CONCURRENT_EDIT code
  - No partial version state in PG
  - Single FORM_PUBLISHED event in QUEUE FABRIC
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - Two conflicting render specs (F857) for same version number
  - Two FORM_PUBLISHED events with different schema payloads
  - PG version table has duplicate (formId, versionNumber) row
RECOVERY:
  Load latest version via F855.GetVersion(), re-apply changes, republish
```

### ST-216 — 10k Simultaneous Submissions
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-216
TASK TYPES:      T308, T309, T310, T311, T312
FACTORIES:       F864, F865, F866, F867, F868
SCENARIO:
  10,000 form submissions arrive simultaneously (form goes viral / load test).
  All submissions for the same formId, 500 different tenants (20/tenant).
LOAD PROFILE:
  10,000 POST requests in 1-second window
  20% are duplicate submissions (same idempotencyKey)
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - F864 dedup catches 2,000 duplicates (DataProcessResult.Failure, not enqueued)
  - 8,000 unique submissions enqueued to QUEUE FABRIC
  - Pipeline stages (T309-T312) process via consumer groups with backpressure
  - ES entry writes succeed for all 8,000 within 30 seconds
  - Redis rate limiter triggers for IPs exceeding threshold (F867)
  - Zero cross-tenant entry leaks
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - Queue overflow (DLQ receives non-failure messages)
  - Duplicate entries in ES (dedup failure)
  - Redis dedup key collision producing false negatives
  - Pipeline stage latency > 30s for 8,000 entries
RECOVERY:
  DLQ replay via F885.ReplayRun()
  Dedup key TTL audit via Redis SCAN {tenantId}:dedup:*
```

### ST-217 — Anti-Spam False Positive Under Load
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-217
TASK TYPES:      T311
FACTORIES:       F867
SCENARIO:
  1,000 legitimate submissions from a corporate IP submitting a registration form.
  Anti-spam AI model flags IP as suspicious due to high submission velocity.
LOAD PROFILE:
  1,000 submissions from same IP, 10-second window, all unique users
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - Rate limiter (Redis) does NOT trigger (per-user threshold, not per-IP only)
  - AI spam scoring returns < 0.3 (legitimate, not flagged)
  - All 1,000 submissions pass spam gate and reach T312
  - False positive rate < 0.1% (QG-311-1)
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - AI spam score > 0.8 for legitimate submissions (false positive)
  - Redis rate limit triggers per-IP instead of per-user+form
  - Spam gate blocks > 1% of legitimate submissions
MITIGATION:
  AI spam threshold configurable per form (FREEDOM config)
  IP-based rate limit only as secondary signal, not primary block
```

### ST-218 — Partial Entry TTL Expiry During Active Session
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-218
TASK TYPES:      T313
FACTORIES:       F870, F862
SCENARIO:
  User saves partial entry (T313), stops filling form. 48 hours pass.
  Redis TTL expires. User returns with resume token.
LOAD PROFILE:
  Single user, resume token presented 48h01m after save
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - F870.LoadPartial() returns DataProcessResult.Failure with TTL_EXPIRED code
  - Redis key confirmed deleted (no dangling state)
  - PG has no orphaned partial entry record
  - User redirected to form start (not crash/500)
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - Redis key not deleted after TTL (TTL not set correctly)
  - PG partial entry record exists without corresponding Redis key
  - Resume token accepted after TTL (security bypass)
RECOVERY:
  Redis TTL audit: redis-cli TTL {tenantId}:partial:{resumeToken}
  PG orphan scan: SELECT * FROM partial_entries WHERE promoted = false AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '48h'
```

### ST-219 — Notification Fan-Out 50 Recipients
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-219
TASK TYPES:      T314
FACTORIES:       F878, F879, F880, F881
SCENARIO:
  Sales lead form submission triggers notification to all 50 sales team members.
  Each recipient gets a personalized merge-tag resolved email.
LOAD PROFILE:
  1 submission → 50 notification events → 50 email deliveries
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - F880 routing rules evaluate and return 50 recipient entries
  - F879 resolves merge tags for each recipient (personalized sender name, etc.)
  - F881 enqueues 50 delivery events to QUEUE FABRIC (not 50 synchronous sends)
  - All 50 emails delivered within 60 seconds
  - Zero duplicate deliveries per recipient
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - F880 returns < 50 recipients (routing rule evaluation error)
  - F881 sends synchronously (blocks for 50 SMTP calls in one thread)
  - Recipient receives > 1 email for same submission
THROUGHPUT TARGET:
  50 notification dispatches in < 5 seconds queue enqueue time
```

### ST-220 — CRM Feed Duplicate Detection
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-220
TASK TYPES:      T315
FACTORIES:       F885, F887, F895
SCENARIO:
  ENTRY_PERSISTED event replayed 3 times (QUEUE FABRIC at-least-once delivery).
  Each replay triggers CRM feed execution.
LOAD PROFILE:
  entryId="entry-X", feedId="crm-feed-1", 3 identical events, 5-second window
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - First execution: F895 registers idempotency key, F887 creates CRM contact
  - Second execution: F895 detects duplicate, returns DataProcessResult.Failure (DUPLICATE_SKIP)
  - Third execution: F895 detects duplicate, same result
  - CRM has exactly 1 contact created for entry-X
  - PG run log records 1 success + 2 idempotent skips
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - CRM has 3 duplicate contacts for same entry
  - F895 idempotency key collision (birthday paradox scenario with ST-226)
  - PG run log records 3 successes (dedup not working)
RECOVERY:
  Redis key audit: redis-cli GET {tenantId}:idempotency:{feedId}:{entryId}
  CRM dedup cleanup: ICrmFeedService.DeduplicateContacts()
```

### ST-221 — Webhook Retry Storm / DLQ Overflow
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-221
TASK TYPES:      T316, T321
FACTORIES:       F886, F894, F885
SCENARIO:
  External webhook endpoint goes down for 2 hours. 5,000 webhook events fail.
  All 5,000 are queued to DLQ. Endpoint recovers. Retry storm begins.
LOAD PROFILE:
  5,000 webhook events in DLQ, endpoint recovers, all retried simultaneously
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - F894 exponential backoff prevents all 5,000 retrying at exactly same moment
  - Retry rate capped at 100/minute per tenant (FREEDOM config)
  - Endpoint receives retries at manageable rate (no overwhelming)
  - All 5,000 succeed within 1 hour of endpoint recovery
  - Idempotency keys prevent duplicate webhook sends during retry
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - DLQ overflow (messages lost, not just delayed)
  - Endpoint receives 5,000 requests simultaneously (retry storm)
  - Duplicate webhook sends (idempotency key expired before retry)
MITIGATION:
  DLQ capacity: min(10x normal queue depth, 1M messages)
  Retry rate limiting: F894 FREEDOM config retryRatePerMinute
```

### ST-222 — Recipe Chain 20+ Steps / Timeout / Saga Rollback
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-222
TASK TYPES:      T320, T321
FACTORIES:       F892, F893, F894
SCENARIO:
  Automation recipe with 25 steps: CRM sync → mailing list → Slack → Jira → email
  chain. Step 18 fails (Jira API rate limit). Retry exhausted after 5 attempts.
LOAD PROFILE:
  recipeId="onboarding-recipe", 25 steps, step 18 fails with 429 Too Many Requests
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - Steps 1-17 complete successfully, results in PG run state
  - Step 18 failures queued to DLQ (T321)
  - Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s — 5 attempts, then EXHAUSTED
  - Recipe marked FAILED in run state after exhaustion
  - Admin alert notification sent (F878 + F881)
  - Steps 1-17 results preserved in run state (not rolled back — saga, not 2PC)
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - Step 19-25 execute before step 18 completes (ordering violation)
  - PG run state corrupted by concurrent step writes
  - Recipe timeout without entering FAILED state
  - Steps 1-17 purged from run state on step 18 failure
```

### ST-223 — OAuth Token Refresh Race (2 Threads, 1 Expiry Window)
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-223
TASK TYPES:      T322
FACTORIES:       F897
SCENARIO:
  Connector OAuth token expires. Two feed executions attempt to refresh simultaneously.
  Both read expired token, both attempt refresh — float refresh race.
LOAD PROFILE:
  2 concurrent feed executions, token TTL = T+1 second, both detect expiry simultaneously
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - PG pessimistic lock on token refresh operation (one wins, one waits)
  - Winning thread refreshes token, writes new token to PG encrypted
  - Waiting thread reads refreshed token (not refreshes again)
  - External OAuth server receives exactly 1 refresh request
  - Both feed executions proceed with valid token
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - External OAuth server receives 2 refresh requests (token invalidation)
  - Second refresh invalidates first token (race produces invalid state)
  - One thread proceeds with expired token after race
IMPLEMENTATION:
  PG advisory lock: SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(connectorId || tenantId))
```

### ST-224 — File Upload Virus Scan Queue Saturation
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-224
TASK TYPES:      T324
FACTORIES:       F873, F874, F875
SCENARIO:
  1,000 file uploads arrive simultaneously (batch import form submission).
  Virus scan queue saturates, scan jobs back up.
LOAD PROFILE:
  1,000 file upload complete events in 10-second window
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - 1,000 scan jobs enqueued to QUEUE FABRIC scan queue
  - Backpressure: scan consumer groups process at max throughput
  - New signed URLs only generated as scans complete (not batched release)
  - Files remain quarantined until scan completes (no optimistic URL generation)
  - No scan jobs lost — DLQ catches scan failures
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - Signed URLs generated before scan complete (INV-15-6 violation)
  - Scan queue overflow (jobs lost, files stuck in quarantine permanently)
  - Scan timeout after 60s returns DataProcessResult.Failure (file stays quarantined)
THROUGHPUT TARGET:
  Scan queue handles 100 concurrent scans per consumer group
```

### ST-225 — Cross-Tenant Form Schema Leak Attempt
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-225
TASK TYPES:      T307, T326
FACTORIES:       F852, F900
SCENARIO:
  Attacker-controlled tenant-A account attempts to enumerate and read form schemas
  from tenant-B by guessing formIds. Direct ES query with formId but wrong tenantId.
LOAD PROFILE:
  1,000 GET /forms/{formId} requests with valid JWT for tenant-A but target tenant-B formIds
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - F852.GetSchema() checks tenantId against schema document tenantId field
  - All 1,000 attempts return DataProcessResult.Failure with NOT_FOUND (not 403 — no schema enumeration)
  - F900 tenant scope validation logs all cross-tenant attempts (F877 audit)
  - ES query includes tenantId filter (DNA-5) — never returns cross-tenant results
  - No tenant-B schema data in any response
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - Any 200 response containing tenant-B schema data
  - 403 response revealing that formId exists (enumeration risk)
  - ES query without tenantId filter returning all-tenant results
CRITICAL:       This test must pass with zero leaks — any failure = P0 security incident
```

### ST-226 — Recipe Idempotency Key Collision Under UUID-v4 Birthday Paradox
```
STRESS TEST ID:  ST-226
TASK TYPES:      T319, T320
FACTORIES:       F895
SCENARIO:
  1 billion recipe executions with triggerHash generated from UUIDv4.
  Birthday paradox probability analysis for Redis idempotency key collision.
LOAD PROFILE:
  10M executions simulated, 500k unique recipeIds, 20k executions per recipe
EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
  - Zero key collisions observed in 10M execution simulation
  - Collision probability < 1 in 2^64 for triggerHash space
  - triggerHash = SHA-256(recipeId + eventId + tenantId) — not raw UUIDv4
  - Redis key format: {tenantId}:idem:{recipeId}:{triggerHash[:16]}
FAILURE SIGNAL:
  - Any colliding triggerHash causes two different executions to share idempotency key
  - Recipe execution skipped (DUPLICATE_SKIP) for different trigger events
  - Collision detected in < 10M samples (birthday paradox threshold exceeded)
MITIGATION:
  Use SHA-256 of composite key (not UUIDv4) for triggerHash — collision space 2^256
  Monitor collision rate via F899 run history anomaly detection
```

## BFA CONFLICT RULES — FLOW-22

---

### CF-402 — Design Token Update vs Active Publish Job
```
RULE:         CF-402
TYPE:         WRITE_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Design token update (T341) must not proceed while an active publish job (T336)
              is running for the same siteId + tenantId
ENTITIES:     IDesignTokenService (F923), IPublishJobService (F917)
TRIGGER:      F923.SetTokenAsync called while F917 has status=RUNNING for same siteId
DETECTION:    BFA checks publish_jobs index for active jobs before allowing token write
RESOLUTION:   Queue token update — execute after active publish job completes
ENFORCED BY:  T341, IR-T341-1, AF-9 of T341
CROSS-FLOW:   None (internal FLOW-22)
SEVERITY:     HIGH — token change during publish = inconsistent build artifact
```

### CF-403 — Collaboration Lock Ownership Violation
```
RULE:         CF-403
TYPE:         SECURITY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Page tree mutation (T327) rejected if lock token does not belong to
              requesting userId + tenantId
ENTITIES:     ICollaborationLockService (F905), IPageTreeService (F901)
TRIGGER:      Lock token userId != authenticated userId
DETECTION:    F905 validates lock ownership before F901 write
RESOLUTION:   Reject mutation; return DataProcessResult 403 Forbidden
ENFORCED BY:  T327, IR-T327-1, AF-8 of T327
SEVERITY:     CRITICAL — unauthorized page tree mutations
```

### CF-404 — Component/PostType ID Collision
```
RULE:         CF-404
TYPE:         UNIQUENESS_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Component ID (F902) or PostType ID (F907) must not collide with existing
              registry entries within the same tenantId scope
ENTITIES:     IComponentRegistryService (F902), IPostTypeRegistryService (F907)
TRIGGER:      RegisterComponentAsync or RegisterPostTypeAsync with duplicate ID
DETECTION:    BFA checks registry before write
RESOLUTION:   Reject registration; return DataProcessResult with collision detail
ENFORCED BY:  T328, T331, AF-9 of both
SEVERITY:     HIGH — duplicate IDs break engine factory resolution
```

### CF-405 — Preview Cross-Tenant Data Exposure
```
RULE:         CF-405
TYPE:         DATA_ISOLATION_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Live preview (T330) must not read post/page data from other tenants
ENTITIES:     ILivePreviewService (F904), ICmsQueryService (F910)
TRIGGER:      Preview render with missing or mismatched tenantId filter
DETECTION:    AF-9 validates all ICmsQueryService calls include tenantId (DNA-5)
RESOLUTION:   Build failure if any query lacks tenantId
ENFORCED BY:  T330, DNA-5, AF-7 Compliance
SEVERITY:     CRITICAL — cross-tenant data exposure in preview
```

### CF-406 — Token Update Without Cache Invalidation
```
RULE:         CF-406
TYPE:         CONSISTENCY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Any design token write (F923) MUST be followed by cache invalidation (F918)
              within the same operation chain
ENTITIES:     IDesignTokenService (F923), ICacheInvalidationService (F918)
TRIGGER:      F923 write without subsequent F918 InvalidateTokensAsync
DETECTION:    AF-9 validates token update task type includes F918 in factory dependencies
RESOLUTION:   Build failure if token update task type omits F918
ENFORCED BY:  T341, IR-T341-1, IR-918-3
SEVERITY:     HIGH — stale cached pages after token change
```

### CF-407 — Duplicate Published Slug
```
RULE:         CF-407
TYPE:         UNIQUENESS_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Post save (T332) must not create a slug that duplicates an existing
              published post slug within the same siteId + tenantId
ENTITIES:     IPostService (F908), ISlugService (F927)
TRIGGER:      CreatePostAsync or UpdatePostAsync with duplicate slug
DETECTION:    F927.RegisterSlugAsync checks existing registry before commit
RESOLUTION:   Return DataProcessResult with slug conflict; suggest alternative
ENFORCED BY:  T332, IR-T332-1 (version conflict), F927 IR-927-2
SEVERITY:     HIGH — duplicate slugs break routing
```

### CF-408 — Dangling Category/Tag Reference
```
RULE:         CF-408
TYPE:         REFERENTIAL_INTEGRITY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Post save must not reference category or tag IDs that do not exist
              in the CMS collection for this siteId + tenantId
ENTITIES:     IPostService (F908), ICollectionRelationService (F912)
TRIGGER:      CreatePostAsync or UpdatePostAsync with non-existent categoryId/tagId
DETECTION:    F912 validates relation existence before post write
RESOLUTION:   Reject save; return DataProcessResult with missing reference detail
ENFORCED BY:  T332, AF-9 of T332
SEVERITY:     MEDIUM — content integrity issue
```

### CF-409 — Publish Event Without Cache Invalidation Chain
```
RULE:         CF-409
TYPE:         CONSISTENCY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Post Published status (T333) must trigger cache invalidation (F918) chain
ENTITIES:     IPostService (F908), ICacheInvalidationService (F918)
TRIGGER:      Status transition to Published without F918 in event chain
DETECTION:    T336 publish pipeline validates F918 step present
RESOLUTION:   Build failure if publish pipeline omits cache invalidation step
ENFORCED BY:  T333, T336, IR-T336-3
SEVERITY:     HIGH — stale pages served after publish
```

### CF-410 — Slug Change Without Redirect
```
RULE:         CF-410
TYPE:         CONSISTENCY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Any slug change (F927.ChangeSlugAsync) MUST auto-create a 301 redirect
              via IRedirectService (F928)
ENTITIES:     ISlugService (F927), IRedirectService (F928)
TRIGGER:      Slug changed without F928.CreateRedirectAsync called with old slug
DETECTION:    F927.ChangeSlugAsync validates F928 registration in same operation
RESOLUTION:   Atomic: slug change + redirect creation in same transaction
ENFORCED BY:  T333, IR-927-1, IR-T333-?
SEVERITY:     HIGH — broken links, SEO damage
```

### CF-411 — Circular Redirect Chain
```
RULE:         CF-411
TYPE:         LOOP_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Redirect chain A→B→C→A must be rejected at engine level
ENTITIES:     IRedirectService (F928)
TRIGGER:      CreateRedirectAsync where target is in existing chain originating from source
DETECTION:    F928 traverses existing redirect graph before write
RESOLUTION:   Reject; return DataProcessResult with cycle detail
ENFORCED BY:  F928 IR-928-2, AF-9 of routing tasks
SEVERITY:     HIGH — infinite redirect loop for visitors
```

### CF-412 — Hardcoded Workflow Config
```
RULE:         CF-412
TYPE:         FREEDOM_VIOLATION
DESCRIPTION:  Editorial workflow configuration (approval steps, required roles, timeouts)
              MUST come from FREEDOM config (F938) — never hardcoded in service
ENTITIES:     IEditorialWorkflowService (F916), ISiteConfigService (F938)
TRIGGER:      Workflow configuration found in generated service code instead of ES config
DETECTION:    AF-7 Compliance scans for hardcoded role strings or step counts
RESOLUTION:   Build failure; refactor to read from F938 ISiteConfigService
ENFORCED BY:  T334, IR-T334-1, DNA-4 (FREEDOM principle)
SEVERITY:     HIGH — workflow changes require code deploy (violates FREEDOM principle)
```

### CF-413 — Draft/Scheduled Content in Public Feed
```
RULE:         CF-413
TYPE:         DATA_EXPOSURE_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Public CMS feed queries (T335) and sitemap (T340) must NEVER surface
              Draft or Scheduled posts
ENTITIES:     ICmsQueryService (F910), ISitemapRssService (F921)
TRIGGER:      Query or sitemap generation without status=Published filter
DETECTION:    AF-9 validates build_search_filter includes status field
RESOLUTION:   Build failure if status filter absent from public query
ENFORCED BY:  T335, T340, IR-T335-1, IR-T340-2
SEVERITY:     CRITICAL — unreleased content exposure
```

### CF-414 — Publish Job Entity Conflict (Cross-Flow)
```
RULE:         CF-414
TYPE:         CROSS_FLOW_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Publish job (T336, FLOW-22) must check BFA for entity conflicts with
              FLOW-01 through FLOW-21 before executing pipeline
ENTITIES:     IPublishJobService (F917) and all prior flow BFA registrations
TRIGGER:      Publish job for entity types also managed by prior flows
DETECTION:    BFA index checked at publish job enqueue time
RESOLUTION:   Conflict logged; publish job may proceed with conflict notification
ENFORCED BY:  T336, AF-9 of T336
SEVERITY:     MEDIUM — cross-flow entity state inconsistency
```

### CF-415 — Concurrent Publish Jobs for Same Site
```
RULE:         CF-415
TYPE:         CONCURRENCY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Only one active publish job per siteId + tenantId at a time
ENTITIES:     IPublishJobService (F917)
TRIGGER:      Second EnqueuePublishAsync while first still RUNNING for same siteId
DETECTION:    F917 checks active job index before enqueue
RESOLUTION:   Second job queued (pending) until first completes
ENFORCED BY:  T336, IR-T336-? (single active publish per site)
SEVERITY:     HIGH — concurrent publish = artifact collision
```

### CF-416 — Rollback During Active Publish
```
RULE:         CF-416
TYPE:         CONCURRENCY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Rollback (T338) prohibited while active publish job running for same site
ENTITIES:     IPublishRollbackService (F922), IPublishJobService (F917)
TRIGGER:      RollbackToVersionAsync while F917 has RUNNING job for siteId
DETECTION:    F922 checks F917 active job status before proceeding
RESOLUTION:   Reject rollback; return DataProcessResult with active job reference
ENFORCED BY:  T338, IR-T338-3
SEVERITY:     HIGH — rollback + publish race = corrupted published state
```

### CF-417 — Token Update During Active Design Rebuild
```
RULE:         CF-417
TYPE:         WRITE_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Token update (T341) must not proceed while design rebuild (T339) is active
ENTITIES:     IDesignTokenService (F923), IDesignTokenRebuildService (F926)
TRIGGER:      F923 write while F926 has active rebuild job for same siteId
DETECTION:    F923 checks rebuild job status via F926 before write
RESOLUTION:   Queue token update until rebuild completes
ENFORCED BY:  T341, CF-402 (related)
SEVERITY:     MEDIUM — mid-rebuild token change = inconsistent build output
```

### CF-418 — Asset ID Cross-Tenant Collision
```
RULE:         CF-418
TYPE:         UNIQUENESS_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Asset IDs must be globally unique — no collision across tenants
ENTITIES:     IAssetUploadService (F932), IMediaLibraryService (F935)
TRIGGER:      Asset upload producing ID that collides with existing asset in any tenant
DETECTION:    F932 generates UUID v4 — collision probability negligible but validated
RESOLUTION:   Re-generate UUID on collision (extremely rare)
ENFORCED BY:  T344, IR-932-2
SEVERITY:     MEDIUM — cross-tenant CDN URL guessing risk
```

### CF-419 — Asset Replacement During Active Publish
```
RULE:         CF-419
TYPE:         CONCURRENCY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Asset replacement fan-out (T346) must not run while active publish job
              references the same siteId
ENTITIES:     IAssetReplacementService (F936), IPublishJobService (F917)
TRIGGER:      ReplaceAssetAsync while F917 has active publish job for siteId
DETECTION:    F936 checks F917 active job status before initiating fan-out
RESOLUTION:   Queue replacement until publish completes
ENFORCED BY:  T346, IR-T346-3
SEVERITY:     HIGH — replacement during publish = broken asset references in build
```

### CF-420 — Schema Removal Attempted
```
RULE:         CF-420
TYPE:         BACKWARD_COMPAT_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  PostType schema field removal is prohibited — only additive changes allowed
ENTITIES:     IPostTypeRegistryService (F907), IPostTypeSchemaService (F909)
TRIGGER:      UpdatePostTypeSchemaAsync with field count less than previous version
DETECTION:    F907 diffs incoming schema against stored schema before write
RESOLUTION:   Reject; return DataProcessResult with removed field list
ENFORCED BY:  T331, IR-907-2, IR-T331-2
SEVERITY:     HIGH — field removal breaks existing post data
```

### CF-421 — Sitemap Cross-Tenant URL Exposure
```
RULE:         CF-421
TYPE:         DATA_ISOLATION_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Sitemap (F921) and RSS (F921) must never contain URLs from other tenants
ENTITIES:     ISitemapRssService (F921)
TRIGGER:      Sitemap generation query missing tenantId + siteId filter
DETECTION:    AF-9 validates build_search_filter includes tenantId + siteId (DNA-5)
RESOLUTION:   Build failure if filter absent
ENFORCED BY:  T340, IR-T340-1, DNA-5
SEVERITY:     CRITICAL — cross-tenant SEO data exposure
```

### CF-422 — Redirect Scoping Violation
```
RULE:         CF-422
TYPE:         DATA_ISOLATION_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Redirect lookup must be scoped by tenantId + siteId — no global redirects
ENTITIES:     IRedirectService (F928)
TRIGGER:      ResolveRedirectAsync without tenantId + siteId in lookup
DETECTION:    F928 validates filter parameters — rejects unscoped lookup
RESOLUTION:   Build failure; reject unscoped redirect resolution
ENFORCED BY:  F928, IR-928-3, DNA-5
SEVERITY:     HIGH — wrong tenant's redirects served
```

### CF-423 — SSG Artifact Used for Preview
```
RULE:         CF-423
TYPE:         CORRECTNESS_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Live preview (T330) must NEVER return a cached SSG artifact — always fresh SSR
ENTITIES:     ILivePreviewService (F904), IStaticBuildService (F919)
TRIGGER:      Preview response served from F919 build artifact cache
DETECTION:    AF-9 validates T330 has no dependency on F919 in its resolution path
RESOLUTION:   Build failure if T330 task type references F919
ENFORCED BY:  T330, DD-193, IR-904-1
SEVERITY:     HIGH — preview shows stale published content, not live editor state
```

### CF-424 — Publish Without Idempotency Key
```
RULE:         CF-424
TYPE:         RELIABILITY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  All publish job enqueue operations (F917) must include an idempotency-key
ENTITIES:     IPublishJobService (F917)
TRIGGER:      EnqueuePublishAsync call without idempotency-key in payload
DETECTION:    F917 validates idempotency-key presence before enqueue
RESOLUTION:   Reject enqueue; return DataProcessResult with missing key error
ENFORCED BY:  T336, T337, IR-917-1
SEVERITY:     HIGH — duplicate publish events without idempotency = duplicate builds
```

### CF-425 — Non-CloudEvents Job Envelope
```
RULE:         CF-425
TYPE:         FORMAT_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  All QUEUE FABRIC events in FLOW-22 must use CloudEvents-compatible envelope
ENTITIES:     All QUEUE FABRIC factories (F905, F917, F918, F926, F932, F936, F940, F941, F943)
TRIGGER:      Event payload missing CloudEvents required fields (source, type, subject, time)
DETECTION:    AF-7 validates event schemas in generated service code
RESOLUTION:   Build failure; regenerate with CloudEvents wrapper
ENFORCED BY:  DD-194, AF-7 Compliance
SEVERITY:     MEDIUM — cross-service correlation/tracing breaks
```

### CF-426 — Analytics PII Without Consent Config
```
RULE:         CF-426
TYPE:         PRIVACY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Analytics events (F943) must not capture PII fields without consent config
              being explicitly enabled in FREEDOM config
ENTITIES:     IAnalyticsEventService (F943), ISiteConfigService (F938)
TRIGGER:      Analytics event with email/userId/IP without consent_enabled=true in F938
DETECTION:    F943 checks F938 consent config before including PII fields
RESOLUTION:   Strip PII fields from event if consent not configured
ENFORCED BY:  IR-943-1, AF-8 Security
SEVERITY:     HIGH — GDPR/CCPA violation risk
```

### CF-427 — RBAC Role Assignment Missing Composite Key
```
RULE:         CF-427
TYPE:         DATA_MODEL_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  Role assignments in IRbacSiteService (F939) MUST be composite
              (tenantId, siteId, userId, role) — no single-field role assignments
ENTITIES:     IRbacSiteService (F939)
TRIGGER:      Role assignment stored without all four fields
DETECTION:    AF-7 validates RBAC writes include composite key
RESOLUTION:   Build failure; schema validation enforces all four fields
ENFORCED BY:  IR-939-1, AF-7 Compliance
SEVERITY:     CRITICAL — role elevation across tenants or sites
```

### CF-428 — AI Output Auto-Committed Without User Confirmation
```
RULE:         CF-428
TYPE:         SAFETY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  AI-generated content (F914 writing assistant, F929 SEO meta, F933 alt-text)
              MUST NOT be committed to content store without explicit user confirmation
ENTITIES:     IAiWritingAssistantService (F914), ISeoMetadataService (F929),
              IImageTransformService (F933)
TRIGGER:      AI output written to F908/F929/F935 without confirmation flag
DETECTION:    AF-9 validates AI task types include confirmation step in pipeline
RESOLUTION:   Build failure; insert confirmation gate task
ENFORCED BY:  IR-914-1, IR-929-1, IR-933-2
SEVERITY:     HIGH — unreviewed AI content published inadvertently
```

### CF-429 — Tenant Provisioning Non-Idempotent
```
RULE:         CF-429
TYPE:         RELIABILITY_CONFLICT
DESCRIPTION:  ITenantProvisioningService (F944) must be idempotent — re-running provisioning
              for an existing tenant must not create duplicate resources
ENTITIES:     ITenantProvisioningService (F944)
TRIGGER:      ProvisionAsync called for tenantId that already has resources
DETECTION:    F944 checks existence of tenantId in workspace registry before provisioning
RESOLUTION:   No-op result with existing tenant detail returned
ENFORCED BY:  IR-944-1
SEVERITY:     HIGH — duplicate tenant resources = data isolation failure
```

---

## STRESS TESTS — FLOW-22

### ST-227 — Concurrent Editors Race Condition
```
TEST:         ST-227
SCENARIO:     Two editors simultaneously mutate same page tree node (T327)
              Second mutation arrives 50ms after first lock acquired
EXPECTED:     Second mutation rejected with CF-403 (lock ownership violation)
              Returns DataProcessResult with lock owner detail
              No data corruption in page tree
FAILURE MODE: Both mutations succeed = split-brain page tree
FACTORIES:    F901, F905
VERIFY:       Single winning version in ES; loser receives 409 result
```

### ST-228 — Token Update During Build Race
```
TEST:         ST-228
SCENARIO:     Admin updates design token while SSG build (T339) is 50% complete
EXPECTED:     CF-402 + CF-417 fire; token update queued until build completes
              Build artifact uses original tokens (consistent)
              Post-build: token update applies, new rebuild triggered
FAILURE MODE: Token update mid-build = partially tokenized artifact
FACTORIES:    F923, F919, F926
VERIFY:       Build artifact token hash matches pre-update snapshot
```

### ST-229 — Duplicate Publish Idempotency
```
TEST:         ST-229
SCENARIO:     Publish job enqueued twice with same idempotency-key (simulating retry)
EXPECTED:     CF-424: second enqueue returns no-op DataProcessResult
              Only one build artifact created
              Sitemap/cache invalidation runs exactly once
FAILURE MODE: Duplicate publish = two concurrent builds + race on CDN
FACTORIES:    F917, F919, F918
VERIFY:       Build count = 1; F917 job log shows duplicate detection
```

### ST-230 — Slug Change Without Redirect (Prevention Test)
```
TEST:         ST-230
SCENARIO:     Post slug changed via T332 without F928 redirect creation
EXPECTED:     CF-410 fires; slug change rejected unless atomic with redirect creation
              Old URL would return 404 without redirect
FAILURE MODE: Slug change succeeds silently; all old links broken
FACTORIES:    F927, F928
VERIFY:       F928 redirect record exists for old slug after any slug change
```

### ST-231 — Draft Post in Public Feed (Prevention Test)
```
TEST:         ST-231
SCENARIO:     T335 CMS feed query executed without status=Published filter
EXPECTED:     CF-413 fires; AF-9 build failure
              Query returns DataProcessResult error describing missing filter
FAILURE MODE: Draft post titles/content visible in public feed widget
FACTORIES:    F910, F908
VERIFY:       Zero Draft/Scheduled posts in any T335 response
```

### ST-232 — Schema Field Removal Attempt
```
TEST:         ST-232
SCENARIO:     Admin attempts to remove required field from PostType schema (T331)
EXPECTED:     CF-420 fires; UpdatePostTypeSchemaAsync returns DataProcessResult error
              Schema version unchanged; removed fields listed in error
FAILURE MODE: Existing posts with removed field become invalid; form breaks
FACTORIES:    F907, F909
VERIFY:       Schema version not incremented; field still in registry
```

### ST-233 — Cross-Tenant Sitemap Leak (Prevention Test)
```
TEST:         ST-233
SCENARIO:     ISitemapRssService called without tenantId + siteId filter
EXPECTED:     CF-421 fires; AF-9 build failure
              No multi-tenant URLs in sitemap response
FAILURE MODE: Tenant A's URLs appear in Tenant B's sitemap
FACTORIES:    F921
VERIFY:       All sitemap URLs belong to single tenantId + siteId
```

### ST-234 — Rollback During Active Publish
```
TEST:         ST-234
SCENARIO:     Admin triggers rollback (T338) while publish pipeline (T336) is at step 3/6
EXPECTED:     CF-416 fires; rollback rejected with active job reference
              Publish pipeline completes normally
              Rollback queued for after completion
FAILURE MODE: Partial publish + partial rollback = corrupted deployed state
FACTORIES:    F922, F917, F919
VERIFY:       Published version = completed publish artifact; rollback logged as deferred
```

### ST-235 — AI Writing Assistant Auto-Commit Prevention
```
TEST:         ST-235
SCENARIO:     IAiWritingAssistantService (F914) generates continuation; system attempts
              to write directly to F908 without user confirmation flag
EXPECTED:     CF-428 fires; AF-9 build failure
              AI suggestion stored in temp buffer only
FAILURE MODE: Unreviewed AI content auto-published to live blog
FACTORIES:    F914, F908
VERIFY:       AI output in temp buffer only; F908 not written until confirmation
```

### ST-236 — Responsive Layout Cache Stale After Token Update
```
TEST:         ST-236
SCENARIO:     Design tokens updated (T341); responsive layout cache (F903) not invalidated
EXPECTED:     CF-402 chain: F923 update triggers F918 → F918 invalidates F903 Redis cache
              Next layout resolution fetches from ES source of truth
FAILURE MODE: Old breakpoint layout served after token change
FACTORIES:    F923, F918, F903
VERIFY:       F903 cache miss after F923 write; ES re-fetch confirmed
```

### ST-237 — Concurrent PostType Registration Collision
```
TEST:         ST-237
SCENARIO:     Two admins simultaneously register PostType with same ID (T331)
EXPECTED:     CF-404 fires for second; DataProcessResult collision error
              Only one PostType registered
FAILURE MODE: Two conflicting schemas registered = factory resolution ambiguity
FACTORIES:    F907
VERIFY:       Single PostType entry in registry after race
```

### ST-238 — Theme Switch During Active Build
```
TEST:         ST-238
SCENARIO:     User switches theme (T343) while SSG build (T339) is running
EXPECTED:     CF-402 (active build check); theme switch queued
              Current build uses original theme tokens
              Post-build: full site rebuild with new theme triggers
FAILURE MODE: Theme-mixed build artifact
FACTORIES:    F925, F923, F919
VERIFY:       Build artifact has single consistent theme token set
```

### ST-239 — Asset Replacement Fan-out During Publish
```
TEST:         ST-239
SCENARIO:     Asset replacement (T346) triggered while active publish job uses replaced asset
EXPECTED:     CF-419 fires; replacement queued until publish completes
              Publish artifact references original asset (consistent)
              Post-publish: replacement fan-out executes
FAILURE MODE: Replacement mid-build = broken asset references in published pages
FACTORIES:    F936, F917, F901, F908
VERIFY:       Published artifact asset references consistent; replacement deferred log entry
```

### ST-240 — Tenant Provisioning Idempotency
```
TEST:         ST-240
SCENARIO:     ITenantProvisioningService called twice for same tenantId
              (simulating retry after network timeout)
EXPECTED:     CF-429: second call returns no-op DataProcessResult with existing resources
              No duplicate workspace, site, or config entries
FAILURE MODE: Duplicate tenant resources = data isolation breakdown
FACTORIES:    F944, F937, F938
VERIFY:       Resource count after float provision = same as after single provision
```

### ST-241 — Preview SSG Artifact Contamination
```
TEST:         ST-241
SCENARIO:     ILivePreviewService (F904) attempts to use IStaticBuildService (F919) artifact
              for performance optimization
EXPECTED:     CF-423 fires; AF-9 build failure at T330 task type definition
              Preview always renders fresh SSR
FAILURE MODE: Preview shows published version instead of current editor state
FACTORIES:    F904, F919
VERIFY:       T330 factory dependencies contain no F919 reference
```

# FLOW-23 — Visual Editor Extended: BFA Rules CF-430–CF-457
# Stress Tests ST-242–ST-259
# Merged: 2026-02-27
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# XIIGen V62 BFA STRESS TEST — FLOW-23: Visual Editor Extended
## Delta File — CF-430–CF-457 (20 conflict rules), ST-242–ST-259 (15 stress tests)
## Extends: V62_BFA_STRESS_TEST_MERGED.md (CF-1–CF-429, ST-1–ST-241)

---

## FLOW-23 BFA CONFLICT RULES (CF-430–CF-457)

### CF-430 — Canvas Node ID vs FLOW-02 Content Template ID Collision
**Rule:** Canvas node IDs (F945 Elasticsearch documents) must not collide with FLOW-02 content template document IDs.
**Detection:** BFA compares `nodeId` prefix format against FLOW-02 `templateId` prefix. Canvas uses `canvas_<tenantId>_<uuid>`. FLOW-02 uses `template_<tenantId>_<uuid>`.
**Resolution:** Enforce prefix namespacing at F945 StoreDocument call. Reject node documents without `canvas_` prefix.
**Severity:** HIGH — collision would cause content template to be overwritten by canvas node
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-02, FLOW-23
**Guardrail:** T360 (tenant isolation gate) checks prefix before T347 executes

---

### CF-431 — tenantId Canvas Queries vs FLOW-01 User Registration Tenant Boundary
**Rule:** Canvas queries via F945/F946 must use the same `tenantId` scope as FLOW-01 user registration. A user authenticated in FLOW-01 must not access another tenant's canvas.
**Detection:** BFA validates that `tenantId` in canvas operations matches the JWT claim `tenantId` from FLOW-01 auth context.
**Resolution:** F967 (`ITenantIsolationEnforcerService`) extracts `tenantId` from MicroserviceBase auth context — never from request body.
**Severity:** CRITICAL — cross-tenant canvas exposure is OWASP API1 (BOLA)
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-01, FLOW-23
**Guardrail:** T360 IRON RULE 1 — BUILD FAILURE if tenantId missing or mismatched

---

### CF-432 — Symbol Name vs FLOW-02 Content Template Name Collision
**Rule:** Symbol library names (F947) must not share names with FLOW-02 content template names. Both stored in Elasticsearch and queried by name.
**Detection:** BFA indexes all FLOW-02 template names and all FLOW-23 symbol names. Alerts on exact name match.
**Resolution:** Symbol names prefixed with `sym_`. Content templates prefixed with `tpl_`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM — name collision causes wrong template/symbol resolution in mixed queries
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-02, FLOW-23
**Guardrail:** T348 validation before symbol store

---

### CF-433 — Symbol Update Events vs FLOW-04 Notification Events — Redis Stream Collision
**Rule:** Symbol update events published to Redis Streams via F952 must use a distinct consumer group key from FLOW-04 notification pipeline events.
**Detection:** BFA scans Redis Stream group names. FLOW-04 uses `notifications-*`. FLOW-23 must use `canvas-ops-*`.
**Resolution:** F952 stream key: `canvas-ops-{canvasId}`. F950 collaboration stream: `canvas-collab-{canvasId}`. FLOW-04 stream: `notifications-{userId}`.
**Severity:** HIGH — stream key collision causes notification events routed to canvas consumer
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-04, FLOW-23
**Guardrail:** T361 CloudEvents envelope gate verifies stream key prefix

---

### CF-434 — Layout Solver Output vs FLOW-09 Search Result Documents
**Rule:** Layout solver result documents (F953 → F945 ES) must not use document IDs that match FLOW-09 search result document IDs.
**Detection:** Layout results stored with prefix `layout_result_<canvasId>`. FLOW-09 search results stored with `search_result_<queryId>`.
**Resolution:** Enforce prefix namespacing at layout result StoreDocument.
**Severity:** MEDIUM — overwriting search results with layout data would corrupt FLOW-09
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-09, FLOW-23

---

### CF-435 — Snap Guide Events vs FLOW-12 Chat Event Stream Keys
**Rule:** Canvas snap/alignment events (F958 via QUEUE FABRIC) must not collide with FLOW-12 chat event stream keys.
**Detection:** BFA compares Redis Stream key prefixes. Canvas uses `canvas-align-*`. Chat uses `chat-events-*`.
**Resolution:** T354 validation confirms stream key namespace before publishing.
**Severity:** LOW — collision would mix layout events with chat events; detectable but noisy
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-12, FLOW-23

---

### CF-436 — Breakpoint Patch Documents vs FLOW-13 AI Content Style Documents
**Rule:** Breakpoint patch documents (F955 Elasticsearch) must not share index names with FLOW-13 AI content pipeline style documents.
**Detection:** F955 writes to `canvas-breakpoints-{tenantId}` index. FLOW-13 writes to `ai-content-style-{tenantId}` index.
**Resolution:** Enforce index naming convention at F955 StoreDocument.
**Severity:** MEDIUM — index name collision causes breakpoint patches to overwrite AI content styles
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-13, FLOW-23

---

### CF-437 — Canvas Version IDs vs FLOW-18 Flow Version IDs
**Rule:** Canvas version IDs (F949 PostgreSQL) must not collide with FLOW-18 visual flow version IDs.
**Detection:** BFA cross-references PostgreSQL version tables. Canvas version: `cv_<canvasId>_<int>`. FLOW-18 version: `fv_<flowId>_<int>`.
**Resolution:** Enforce prefix at F949 StoreDocument. FLOW-18 prefix: `fv_`. FLOW-23 prefix: `cv_`.
**Severity:** HIGH — version ID collision would corrupt rollback operations in both flows
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-18, FLOW-23

---

### CF-438 — AI Layout Advisor Output vs Factory Dependency Injection
**Rule:** AI layout advisor (F954 / T352) must not inject new factory dependencies not registered in F945–F981 into generated canvas services.
**Detection:** AF-9 Judge scans AI layout suggestions for factory interface references. Any interface not in F945–F981 or F1–F944 registry → BUILD FAILURE.
**Resolution:** Layout advisor prompts include explicit factory registry constraint. AF-9 rejects suggestions with unregistered factories.
**Severity:** CRITICAL — unregistered factory would bypass fabric resolution and import providers directly
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-23 (internal)

---

### CF-439 — Token Sync Events vs FLOW-13 AI Content Style Events — Redis Stream Collision
**Rule:** Design token propagation events (F952 Redis Streams) must not collide with FLOW-13 AI content pipeline style events.
**Detection:** Token events use stream `canvas-tokens-{tenantId}`. FLOW-13 uses `ai-content-{tenantId}`.
**Resolution:** F977 token sync events published to `canvas-tokens-*` stream group only.
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-13, FLOW-23

---

### CF-440 — Z-Index State vs FLOW-18 Layer Ordering Documents
**Rule:** Z-index state documents in Redis (F959) must not overwrite FLOW-18 layer ordering documents.
**Detection:** F959 uses Redis key `canvas-zindex-{canvasId}-{nodeId}`. FLOW-18 uses `flow-layer-{flowId}-{nodeId}`.
**Resolution:** Enforce key prefix namespacing in F959.
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-18, FLOW-23

---

### CF-441 — Constraint Rules vs FLOW-01 Permission Model for User Roles
**Rule:** Canvas constraint role names (designer/editor/admin) must align with FLOW-01 user registration role definitions.
**Detection:** BFA compares role enum values in FLOW-01 user role registry against FLOW-23 constraint role values.
**Resolution:** Canvas role values sourced from FLOW-01 role registry — no custom role str literals in FLOW-23.
**Severity:** HIGH — role name mismatch would make constraint enforcement silently fail
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-01, FLOW-23

---

### CF-442 — CMS Binding Queries vs FLOW-02 Content Management Queries — ES Index Collision
**Rule:** F960 CMS binding queries must not target the same ES index as FLOW-02 content management service.
**Detection:** BFA checks Elasticsearch index names used by F960. Must be read-only access to FLOW-02 content index. No writes from FLOW-23 data binding service.
**Resolution:** F960 is READ-ONLY against FLOW-02 content indices. Template Mode (F961) also read-only.
**Severity:** HIGH — FLOW-23 writes to FLOW-02 content index would corrupt content management
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-02, FLOW-23
**Guardrail:** T357 IRON RULE 1 — Template Mode READ-ONLY

---

### CF-443 — AI Fallback Jobs vs FLOW-13 AI Content Pipeline Jobs — Queue Collision
**Rule:** AI content fallback jobs (F962 via AI ENGINE FABRIC) must not collide with FLOW-13 AI content pipeline jobs on the same AiDispatcher queue.
**Detection:** F962 jobs carry `jobType: CANVAS_FALLBACK`. FLOW-13 jobs carry `jobType: CONTENT_PIPELINE`. AiDispatcher routes by jobType.
**Resolution:** AiDispatcher routing config registers `CANVAS_FALLBACK` as FLOW-23 job type. FLOW-13 types: `CONTENT_PIPELINE`, `BLOG_GENERATION`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM — job type collision could route fallback responses to content pipeline consumers
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-13, FLOW-23

---

### CF-444 — Template Mode Preview vs FLOW-02 Production Content Indices
**Rule:** Template Mode (F961) must never write to FLOW-02 production content ES indices. Preview state is session-isolated in Redis (F946).
**Detection:** AF-8 Security scan traces all write operations from F961. Any write to `content-*` ES index → BUILD FAILURE.
**Resolution:** F961 operations: read from FLOW-02 content index; write preview state to Redis `canvas-preview-*` key only.
**Severity:** CRITICAL — preview writes to production would corrupt live content
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-02, FLOW-23

---

### CF-445 — Repeater Queries vs FLOW-09 Search Discovery Queries — ES Index Collision
**Rule:** Repeater executor (F963) queries must not conflict with FLOW-09 search & discovery queries on the same Elasticsearch index.
**Detection:** F963 repeater queries target `content-{tenantId}` index (read-only). FLOW-09 also reads `content-{tenantId}`. Conflict possible if both modify `_source` or `sort` in incompatible ways.
**Resolution:** F963 uses dedicated query template with `build_search_filter`. No index-altering operations. No field mappings changes.
**Severity:** LOW — performance conflict possible; data integrity not at risk if both read-only
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-09, FLOW-23

---

### CF-446 — Binding Validator vs Unknown Skill Output Paths from FLOW-01–FLOW-22
**Rule:** Binding validator (F965) must recognize all skill output paths registered in FLOW-01–FLOW-22. Any `$.skill_XX.field` path where XX is from a completed flow must resolve against that flow's output schema.
**Detection:** BFA maintains skill output schema registry from FLOW-01–FLOW-22. F965 validates against this registry.
**Resolution:** Skill output schema registry updated as part of each FLOW delta. FLOW-23 registers SK-211–SK-222 output schemas.
**Severity:** HIGH — unrecognized binding path returns silent null; AF-9 catches but designer experience degrades
**Flows Affected:** All FLOW-01–FLOW-23

---

### CF-447 — Tenant Isolation Gate Must Precede ALL FLOW-23 Task Type Executions
**Rule:** T360 (Tenant Isolation Enforcement Gate) must be the first step in every FLOW-23 DAG execution. No T347–T366 may execute before T360 passes.
**Detection:** BFA validates flow template step ordering. Step 1 must always be T360.
**Resolution:** FlowOrchestrator enforces T360 as pre-condition hook for all FLOW-23 templates (56–61).
**Severity:** CRITICAL — missing T360 means tenant isolation not enforced; OWASP API1 exposure
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-23 (internal)
**Guardrail:** T366 IRON RULE 1

---

### CF-448 — FLOW-23 Quotas vs FLOW-15 DevOps Quotas — Same Tenant
**Rule:** FLOW-23 canvas quotas (F968) and FLOW-15 DevOps pipeline quotas must not float-count against the same tenant's resource limit.
**Detection:** BFA checks quota counter keys. F968 uses `quota:canvas:{tenantId}:{type}`. FLOW-15 uses `quota:devops:{tenantId}:{type}`. No shared counter key.
**Resolution:** Quota counter keys namespaced by flow domain. Total quota = sum of domain quotas. No shared counter.
**Severity:** MEDIUM — float-counting would unfairly throttle tenants using both FLOW-15 and FLOW-23
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-15, FLOW-23

---

### CF-449 — FLOW-23 Artifact IDs vs FLOW-18 Code Injection Artifacts
**Rule:** FLOW-23 code export artifact IDs (F979 Elasticsearch) must not collide with FLOW-18 code injection artifact IDs.
**Detection:** BFA compares artifact ID prefixes. FLOW-18: `inject_artifact_<flowId>_<uuid>`. FLOW-23: `canvas_artifact_<canvasId>_<uuid>`.
**Resolution:** F979 enforces `canvas_artifact_` prefix at StoreDocument.
**Severity:** HIGH — artifact ID collision would cause wrong code version deployed from rollback
**Flows Affected:** FLOW-18, FLOW-23

---

## STRESS TESTS — FLOW-23 (ST-242–ST-259)

### ST-242 — Concurrent Multi-Tenant Canvas Operations
**Scenario:** 100 concurrent users from 10 different tenants all editing their canvases simultaneously.
**Test Steps:**
1. 100 threads, each with unique `tenantId`, each executing T366 full flow DAG
2. All threads access same Elasticsearch cluster via F945
3. Assert: zero cross-tenant node visibility
4. Assert: all T360 tenant isolation gates pass independently
5. Assert: Redis hot state (F946) keys correctly namespaced per tenant
**Expected Result:** All 100 operations succeed independently; zero cross-tenant leakage
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-431, CF-447

---

### ST-243 — Canvas Node ID Collision Attack
**Scenario:** Malicious request attempts to create a canvas node with FLOW-02 content template ID format.
**Test Steps:**
1. POST canvas node with `nodeId: "template_tenant_abc_1234"` (FLOW-02 format)
2. T360 → T347 pipeline executes
3. Assert: F945 StoreDocument rejects document without `canvas_` prefix
4. Assert: `DataProcessResult.Failure` returned; no document written
**Expected Result:** CF-430 protection rejects the malicious ID
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-430

---

### ST-244 — Layout Solver Determinism Under 1000 Node Tree
**Scenario:** Node tree with 1000 nested nodes (10 levels × 10 children).
**Test Steps:**
1. Create canvas with 1000-node tree via F945
2. Execute T349 layout solver 5 times with identical input
3. Assert: all 5 outputs are byte-identical (deterministic)
4. Assert: `DataProcessResult[T]` returned; no exceptions
5. Measure: execution time < 5 seconds
**Expected Result:** Deterministic layout; performance acceptable
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-434 (layout output IDs correct)

---

### ST-245 — Breakpoint Override Stack: 10 Breakpoints
**Scenario:** Node with 10 custom breakpoint overrides; resolve for each breakpoint.
**Test Steps:**
1. Store node with base style + 10 breakpoint patches via F955
2. Resolve for each breakpoint
3. Assert: correct patch applied at each breakpoint
4. Assert: patches do not bleed between breakpoints
5. Assert: AI suggestion via F954 invoked when suggestion requested
**Expected Result:** Correct patch per breakpoint; no cross-breakpoint contamination
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-436

---

### ST-246 — Template Mode Preview: Does Not Contaminate Production
**Scenario:** Designer activates Template Mode, edits preview content, deactivates.
**Test Steps:**
1. Activate Template Mode via F961; fetch 5 live posts from FLOW-02 content index
2. Simulate editing post title in preview
3. Assert: edit stored in Redis `canvas-preview-*` key only
4. Assert: FLOW-02 content ES index unchanged
5. Deactivate Template Mode; assert canvas returns to pre-preview state
**Expected Result:** Zero production content mutation
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-442, CF-444

---

### ST-247 — Quota Enforcement: Canvas Node Limit
**Scenario:** Tenant at node quota limit attempts to add one more node.
**Test Steps:**
1. Set tenant `quota:canvas:{tenantId}:CANVAS_NODES` = limit
2. Execute T347 node tree composition (add one node)
3. Assert: T360 quota check returns `DataProcessResult.Failure(QuotaExceeded)`
4. Assert: no node written to F945
5. Assert: idempotency key registered for this attempt
**Expected Result:** Hard quota rejection; no partial write
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-448 (quota not shared with FLOW-15)

---

### ST-248 — CloudEvents Envelope: Deduplication Under Retry
**Scenario:** Canvas operation event published 3 times with same `Idempotency-Key` (network retry scenario).
**Test Steps:**
1. Publish canvas event with `Idempotency-Key: abc123` via F969
2. Publish same event twice more (simulating retries)
3. Assert: consumer receives exactly 1 event
4. Assert: F970 idempotency store has key `abc123` with TTL
5. Assert: side effects (node update) executed exactly once
**Expected Result:** Exactly-once semantics; no duplicate side effects
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-433, CF-449

---

### ST-249 — Role Lock Enforcement: Editor Cannot Modify Locked Layout
**Scenario:** Content editor attempts to modify padding on a node with `lockLayout:true`.
**Test Steps:**
1. Create node with `constraints.lockLayout: true` via F945
2. Authenticated as EDITOR role (from MicroserviceBase auth context)
3. Attempt padding change via T355 constraint gate
4. Assert: `DataProcessResult.Failure("Layout locked")` returned
5. Assert: node document in ES unchanged
6. Switch to DESIGNER role; same padding change
7. Assert: change accepted and stored
**Expected Result:** Strict role boundary enforced
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-441

---

### ST-250 — Symbol Propagation: 500 Instances
**Scenario:** Update a shared Symbol (blog card) that has 500 instances across 10 canvases.
**Test Steps:**
1. Create symbol with 500 instance references via F947
2. Update symbol title binding via T348
3. Assert: propagation event published to `canvas-ops-*` stream via F952
4. Assert: all 500 instances receive update event within 10 seconds
5. Assert: FLOW-04 notification stream not polluted
**Expected Result:** Full propagation; no FLOW-04 stream collision
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-432, CF-433

---

### ST-251 — Code Export Quality Gate Failure
**Scenario:** AI generates code with a typed model (violates DNA-1). AF-9 must reject.
**Test Steps:**
1. Prime AiDispatcher to return code containing `class NodeDTO: { str NodeId; }`
2. Execute T363 code export pipeline
3. AF-7 DNA compliance check runs
4. Assert: AF-9 rejects with quality score < 0.8
5. Assert: artifact not stored in F979
6. Assert: `DataProcessResult.Failure("DNA-1 violation: typed model detected")` returned
**Expected Result:** DNA-1 violation caught before artifact storage
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-438, CF-449

---

### ST-252 — Tenant Tier Graduation: Pool → Bridge
**Scenario:** Tenant graduates from shared schema to separate schema (bridge model).
**Test Steps:**
1. Tenant `tenant_enterprise_001` at pool tier; active canvas operations running
2. Trigger tier graduation via T362 (F971)
3. Assert: in-flight canvas operations complete before migration lock
4. Assert: all canvas data migrated to tenant-specific schema
5. Assert: post-migration T360 validation passes with new schema
6. Assert: zero cross-tenant data exposure post-migration
**Expected Result:** Zero-downtime migration; isolation improved post-graduation
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-447, CF-448

---

### ST-253 — Binding Path Validation: Cross-Flow Skill References
**Scenario:** Designer enters binding path `$.skill_05.latest_post.title` (valid FLOW-02 CMS output).
**Test Steps:**
1. Submit binding path to T359 binding validator via F965
2. F965 queries RAG FABRIC for skill output schema
3. Assert: path `$.skill_05.latest_post.title` recognized as valid FLOW-02 output
4. Submit invalid path `$.skill_99.nonexistent.field`
5. Assert: rejected with descriptive error, `DataProcessResult.Failure`
**Expected Result:** Valid cross-flow skill paths accepted; invalid paths rejected
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-446

---

### ST-254 — Collaborative Editing: Concurrent Node Move Conflict
**Scenario:** Two designers move the same node simultaneously; conflict must resolve deterministically.
**Test Steps:**
1. Two users join same canvas via F950
2. Both submit `NODE_MOVED` operations for `node_abc` at same timestamp
3. Assert: SK-221 conflict resolution picks last-writer-wins or merge strategy
4. Assert: resolved state stored in F945 via DATABASE FABRIC
5. Assert: both users receive the resolved state via F952 stream
**Expected Result:** Conflict resolved deterministically; no divergent canvas states
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-433, CF-435

---

### ST-255 — Sandbox Flow Execution: Does Not Contaminate Production
**Scenario:** Flow executed in sandbox; result promoted to production.
**Test Steps:**
1. Create sandbox via SK-222 (F946 Redis)
2. Execute T366 full flow DAG in sandbox
3. Assert: no writes to F945 production Elasticsearch during sandbox execution
4. Assert: sandbox result stored in `canvas-preview-*` Redis key
5. Promote sandbox to production via `PromoteSandboxToProduction`
6. Assert: promoted canvas state correctly written to F945 production index
**Expected Result:** Sandbox fully isolated until explicit promotion
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-444, CF-447

---

### ST-256 — Design Token Update: Cascading Propagation Accuracy
**Scenario:** Update primary color token; verify all 1000 referencing nodes receive update.
**Test Steps:**
1. Store 1000 canvas nodes all referencing `token:primary-color` via F945
2. Update `primary-color` token value via F977
3. T353 design token sync executes
4. Assert: propagation event published to `canvas-tokens-{tenantId}` stream
5. Assert: all 1000 node documents updated within 30 seconds
6. Assert: FLOW-13 AI content style stream not polluted (CF-439)
7. Assert: F976 framework adapter generates correct CSS variable output
**Expected Result:** Complete, accurate token propagation; no stream collisions
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-439

---

## FLOW-22 CROSS-CHECK BFA RULES (CF-450–CF-457)

These rules are NEW — they check FLOW-23 artifacts against FLOW-22 (Visual Editor & Site Builder Platform), which did not exist when the original 23-* documents were authored.

### CF-450 — FLOW-23 Node Tree ID vs FLOW-22 Page Tree ID Collision

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F945 (INodeTreeService) stores a `nodeId` that collides with FLOW-22 F901 (IPageTreeService) `pageTreeNodeId` in the same Elasticsearch index namespace.
**Severity:** HIGH
**Detection:** BFA indexes both ES index prefixes; checks `nodeId` pattern overlap. FLOW-23 uses `canvas_` prefix; FLOW-22 uses `page_` prefix.
**Resolution:** Namespace enforcement: FLOW-23 nodes carry `canvas_` prefix on all IDs. FLOW-22 pages carry `page_` prefix. BFA rejects any ID without its flow's prefix.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F901 ↔ FLOW-23 F945
**Iron Rule:** Both flows share DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — ID collision = data corruption.

### CF-451 — FLOW-23 Symbol Registry vs FLOW-22 Component Registry Collision

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F947 (ISymbolLibraryService) registers a symbol name that collides with FLOW-22 F902 (IComponentRegistryService) component name.
**Severity:** HIGH
**Detection:** BFA checks symbol name uniqueness across both registries at index time.
**Resolution:** FLOW-23 symbols prefixed with `sym:` namespace. FLOW-22 components use `comp:` namespace.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F902 ↔ FLOW-23 F947

### CF-452 — FLOW-23 Design Token Sync vs FLOW-22 Design Token Update Race

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F977 (IDesignTokenSyncService) propagates token changes at the same time FLOW-22 F923 (IDesignTokenRegistryService) updates tokens via T341 propagation.
**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Detection:** BFA detects concurrent token update events on overlapping ES indices within 5-second window.
**Resolution:** F977 MUST acquire advisory lock from F923 before sync. Token updates serialized through shared QUEUE FABRIC consumer group.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F923 ↔ FLOW-23 F977
**Iron Rule:** Concurrent token updates = visual inconsistency. Serialize via queue.

### CF-453 — FLOW-23 Code Export vs FLOW-22 Live Preview Resource Contention

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F975 (IUICodeExportService) generates code from a canvas while FLOW-22 F904 (ILivePreviewService) is rendering the same canvas for preview.
**Severity:** HIGH
**Detection:** BFA checks for concurrent read operations on the same canvasId + tenantId from both flows.
**Resolution:** Code export operates on a snapshot (versioned via F949). Live preview reads current state. No contention because export reads immutable version.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F904 ↔ FLOW-23 F975

### CF-454 — FLOW-23 Canvas Collaboration vs FLOW-22 Session Management Conflict

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F950 (ICanvasCollaborationService) publishes collaboration events that conflict with FLOW-22 F906 (ICollaborationSessionService) session management events on same Redis Streams consumer group.
**Severity:** HIGH
**Detection:** BFA checks Redis Streams consumer group names for collision between flows.
**Resolution:** FLOW-23 uses `canvas:collab:{tenantId}` stream key. FLOW-22 uses `session:collab:{tenantId}` stream key. Different consumer group names.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F906 ↔ FLOW-23 F950

### CF-455 — FLOW-23 Canvas Version vs FLOW-22 Post Version ID Collision

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F949 (ICanvasVersionService) stores version records in PostgreSQL that could collide with FLOW-22 F912 (IContentVersionService) version IDs.
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Detection:** BFA checks PG table name and version ID sequences for overlap.
**Resolution:** FLOW-23 uses `canvas_versions` table; FLOW-22 uses `content_versions` table. Separate PG sequences.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F912 ↔ FLOW-23 F949

### CF-456 — FLOW-23 Deployment Pipeline vs FLOW-22 Publish Job Queue Collision

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F980 (IDeploymentPipelineService) dispatches deployment events on the same Redis Streams queue as FLOW-22 F917 (IPublishJobService) publish events.
**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Detection:** BFA checks queue names and consumer group overlap.
**Resolution:** FLOW-23 uses `deploy:canvas:{tenantId}` queue. FLOW-22 uses `publish:site:{tenantId}` queue. Different queue names + consumer groups.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F917 ↔ FLOW-23 F980
**Iron Rule:** Queue collision = lost messages. Different queue names mandatory.

### CF-457 — FLOW-23 Tenant Hardening vs FLOW-22 Workspace Provision Ordering

**Trigger:** FLOW-23 F967 (ITenantIsolationEnforcerService) enforces tenant isolation before FLOW-22 F944 (IWorkspaceProvisionService) has completed workspace provisioning.
**Severity:** HIGH
**Detection:** BFA checks that workspace provisioning event has been received before any FLOW-23 canvas operations begin for a new tenant.
**Resolution:** FLOW-23 T360 checks workspace provision status via BFA event index before allowing any canvas operations.
**Cross-Flow:** FLOW-22 F944 ↔ FLOW-23 F967

---

## ADDITIONAL STRESS TESTS (FLOW-22 Cross-Flow)

### ST-257 — FLOW-23 + FLOW-22 Page Tree and Node Tree Concurrent Write

**Scenario:** Simultaneously create a FLOW-22 page tree node and a FLOW-23 canvas node tree node for the same tenantId within 100ms.
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-450 (node ID collision), CF-431 (tenant boundary)
**Expected Result:** CF-450 namespace enforcement rejects collision. Both operations succeed independently.
**Verdict:** PASS if both flows store without ID overlap.

### ST-258 — FLOW-23 Design Token Sync During FLOW-22 Token Update

**Scenario:** FLOW-22 F923 updates primary-color token. Simultaneously, FLOW-23 F977 attempts to sync the same token to canvas nodes.
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-452 (design token race)
**Expected Result:** CF-452 advisory lock serializes operations. F977 waits for F923 update to complete before syncing.
**Verdict:** PASS if token sync reflects latest F923 value; no stale data.

### ST-259 — FLOW-23 Deployment During FLOW-22 Publish

**Scenario:** FLOW-22 publish saga (F917) is mid-execution when FLOW-23 deployment pipeline (F980) dispatches a code export deployment for the same siteId.
**BFA Rules Exercised:** CF-456 (queue collision), CF-414 (FLOW-22 concurrent publish)
**Expected Result:** Different queue names prevent collision. Both operations proceed independently on their own queues.
**Verdict:** PASS if no message loss; both pipelines complete.
