# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# FLOW-31 — BFA STRESS TEST
## Domain: Design Intelligence — Figma Screen Understanding, GraphRAG Module Mapping, Gap Completion
## Status: COMPLETE ✅
## BFA Rules: CF-652–CF-693 (42 rules)
## Stress Tests: ST-405–ST-430 (26 tests)
## Date: 2026-03-01

---

## STARTING NUMBERS (FLOW-31)
```
BFA Rule:    CF-652   (ends CF-693)
Stress Test: ST-405   (ends ST-430)
```

---

## SECTION 1 — BFA CONFLICT RULES (CF-652–CF-693)

### GROUP A — Ingestion & Rate Limit Safety (CF-652–CF-661)

```
CF-652 : FIGMA_SCOPE_ISOLATION
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Any FLOW-31 factory stores or queries without tenantId
  RULE: Every Figma ingestion document (node tree, image cache, IR, graph node) MUST carry tenantId as first-level
        field. Absence = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T489–T515
  CONFLICTS WITH: Any cross-tenant Figma data leak
  RESOLUTION: Engine adds tenantId to all store/search calls via Scope Isolation (DNA-5)

CF-653 : FIGMA_FILKEY_NAMESPACE_COLLISION
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-31 fileKey index overlaps with FLOW-18 visual flow editor's flow definition registry
  RULE: Figma fileKey namespace (figma::*) is reserved for FLOW-31. Flow definition IDs (flow::*) are reserved
        for FLOW-18. Collision = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T489, T490
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-18 (F472 IFlowExecutionService), FLOW-15 (F466 IFlowDefinitionRegistryService)
  RESOLUTION: Prefix all FLOW-31 ES document IDs with "figma31::{tenantId}::{fileKey}"

CF-654 : RATE_LIMIT_TOKEN_REQUIRED
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Any Figma API call attempted without acquiring rate-limit token from F1278
  RULE: F1271 (IFigmaApiClientService) MUST call F1278 (IFigmaRateLimitGuardService) BEFORE every REST call.
        Bypassing = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T489, T491
  CONFLICTS WITH: Figma API quota violations that affect all tenants on shared API key
  RESOLUTION: Rate limit token acquisition is first step in ingestion pipeline, enforced by MACHINE config

CF-655 : DOCUMENT_SCOPE_REQUIRED
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Any document stored without tenantId + fileKey composite scope
  RULE: All FLOW-31 ES indices require { tenantId, fileKey } on every document. Queries missing either field
        are rejected by build_search_filter (DNA-2).
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T489–T515
  CONFLICTS WITH: Global shared indices that ignore tenant boundaries
  RESOLUTION: MicroserviceBase (DNA-4) enforces scope injection; AF-7 (Compliance) gate validates

CF-656 : VERSION_CHECK_MANDATORY
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Figma file re-fetched without first checking IFigmaVersionManagerService (F1277)
  RULE: NEVER re-ingest a file that hasn't changed. Version comparison required before any GET /files/{key} call.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T489
  CONFLICTS WITH: Redundant API quota consumption + stale IR data coexisting with fresh data
  RESOLUTION: T489 ENTRY condition requires version delta confirmation before proceeding

CF-657 : COMPONENT_REGISTRY_COLLISION
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-31 component catalog (F1275) uses same ES index name as FLOW-22/FLOW-23 visual editor
           component registry
  RULE: Component catalog index for FLOW-31 must be prefixed "figma31_component_catalog", not "component_catalog"
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T490
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-22 (visual editor), FLOW-23 (component library)
  RESOLUTION: Index naming convention enforced at factory registration

CF-658 : TYPED_MODEL_PROHIBITION
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Generated service creates typed class (ProductNode, TextLayer, ScreenModel, etc.) instead of
           dict[str, Any]
  RULE: DNA-1 (parse_document) — ALL Figma IR, ScreenSemanticsIR, SystemModelIR MUST be dict[str, Any].
        Zero typed model classes = BUILD RULE. Violation = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T490–T515
  CONFLICTS WITH: Any typed model usage in AI-generated code
  RESOLUTION: AF-7 (Compliance) gate checks generated code for class declarations with UI/IR/Model suffix

CF-659 : IMAGE_STORAGE_SCOPE
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Rendered frame images stored in shared bucket without tenant partition
  RULE: All frame images must be stored under tenant-scoped path: {storageProvider}/{tenantId}/{fileKey}/{frameId}
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T491
  CONFLICTS WITH: Cross-tenant image URL exposure
  RESOLUTION: F1274 (IFigmaImageRendererService) enforces bucket path scoping via FREEDOM config

CF-660 : IMAGE_URL_EXPIRY_REQUIRED
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Frame image URLs stored without expiry timestamp (Figma image URLs expire after 30 days)
  RULE: EVERY stored image URL must carry expiresAt field. Stale URL access = data corruption risk.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T491
  CONFLICTS WITH: T498 multimodal AI prompt failing due to expired image URLs mid-pipeline
  RESOLUTION: F1274 stores expiresAt; T498 checks expiry before using URL, triggers re-render if expired

CF-661 : PLUGIN_EVENT_QUEUE_ISOLATION
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: Figma plugin events (F1272) published to shared Redis Stream without consumer group scoping
  RULE: Plugin events MUST use consumer group "figma31-{tenantId}-plugin" to prevent cross-tenant event pickup
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T492, T493
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-18 queue consumer groups
  RESOLUTION: QUEUE FABRIC consumer group naming enforced in F1272 factory config
```

---

### GROUP B — IR Processing Safety (CF-662–CF-669)

```
CF-662 : DESIGNIR_SCHEMA_COMPLETENESS
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: DesignIR document missing required top-level fields
  RULE: Every DesignIR document MUST contain: screens[], components[], designTokens, screenMap, prototypeLinks.
        Missing any = T497 (IR Validation Gate) fires REJECT.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T495–T497
  CONFLICTS WITH: Downstream T498 (semantics extraction) failing on incomplete DesignIR
  RESOLUTION: F1286 (IDesignIRValidatorService) runs schema completeness check as quality gate

CF-663 : SCREEN_FINGERPRINT_COLLISION
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: Two screens from different fileKeys share same fingerprint hash (component composition hash)
  RULE: Screen fingerprint collisions across tenants are acceptable (shared patterns), but WITHIN same
        fileKey a collision = duplicate screen error, not allowed.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T494
  CONFLICTS WITH: F1283 (IScreenFingerprintService) producing non-unique IDs within a file
  RESOLUTION: Fingerprint includes frameId + fileKey in hash seed; within-file uniqueness enforced

CF-664 : PROTOTYPE_LINK_ORPHAN
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: Navigation flow includes prototype link to non-existent frameId (deleted screen still referenced)
  RULE: All prototypeLinks must point to existing frameIds in the node tree. Orphan links are flagged as
        FLOW_GAP, not silent failures.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T493, T430 (flow gap analysis)
  CONFLICTS WITH: Gap completion producing false positives from orphan links
  RESOLUTION: F1272 (plugin extractor) resolves links against current frame set; orphans tagged separately

CF-665 : DESIGNIR_CROSS_TENANT_READ
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: T496 (DesignIR Assembly) attempts to load screen data from another tenant's fileKey
  RULE: build_search_filter (DNA-2) MUST apply tenantId filter on ALL DesignIR queries.
        Cross-tenant read = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T495–T497
  RESOLUTION: DNA-2 enforced; AF-7 gate validates all DB read calls include tenantId

CF-666 : LAYOUT_SEMANTIC_EMPTY_RESULT
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: F1281 (ILayoutSemanticsService) returns empty result for a non-empty screen
  RULE: A screen with ≥3 nodes MUST produce at least one layout semantic (list/grid/table/modal/tabs).
        Empty result on non-trivial screen = WARN + human review flag, not silent success.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T495
  CONFLICTS WITH: System type inference producing no evidence for screens that genuinely have semantics
  RESOLUTION: T495 QUALITY GATES require minimum semantic count; AF-9 fires WARNING if empty

CF-667 : INTERACTION_SEMANTIC_WITHOUT_TEXT
  SEVERITY: LOW
  TRIGGER: Interaction semantic (action) inferred with no text evidence (no CTA text, no label)
  RULE: Action patterns without any textual evidence MUST be tagged "low_confidence" and excluded from
        primary module mapping. Only high+medium confidence actions count for gap detection.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T496
  RESOLUTION: F1284 (IInteractionSemanticsService) tags confidence; T511 filters by confidence threshold

CF-668 : COMPONENT_SIGNATURE_DRIFT
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Same component (by main component ID) has significantly different signatures across two ingestion
           runs (>30% feature change)
  RULE: Signature drift > 30% triggers RE-PROCESS for all screens using that component. Silent ignore = invalid.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T494, T489
  CONFLICTS WITH: Stale screen mappings based on outdated component signatures
  RESOLUTION: F1282 (IComponentSignatureService) computes drift score; T489 version check includes component
              signature comparison

CF-669 : DESIGNIR_BATCH_PARTIAL_FAILURE
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Batch DesignIR processing (T497) partially fails — some screens succeed, some fail
  RULE: Partial batch = PARTIAL_COMPLETE state. System MUST record which screenIds succeeded/failed.
        Downstream tasks MUST only operate on succeeded screens. No silent full-batch retry on partial failure.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T495–T497
  RESOLUTION: DataProcessResult[T] (DNA-3) carries per-item success/failure; T497 aggregates and emits
              PARTIAL_COMPLETE with lists of succeeded/failed screenIds
```

---

### GROUP C — AI Semantic Safety (CF-670–CF-676)

```
CF-670 : EVIDENCE_COVERAGE_GATE_FAIL
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: AI (T498–T500) produces ScreenSemanticsIR where any module/action/entity claim has zero
           evidence items
  RULE: EVERY claim (module candidate, user action, data entity) MUST reference ≥1 evidence item (nodeId,
        text snippet, or component name). Zero evidence = BUILD FAILURE, judge blocks promotion.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T498, T499, T500
  IRON RULE: This is the primary guard against AI hallucination in FLOW-31
  RESOLUTION: F1334 (IEvidenceCoverageGateService) enforces; AF-9 judge validates before promotion

CF-671 : ARCHETYPE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: ScreenSemanticsIR produced with archetype confidence < 0.4 for ALL candidates
  RULE: If maximum archetype confidence < 0.4, screen is tagged UNCLASSIFIED — NOT assigned a random archetype.
        UNCLASSIFIED screens are flagged for human review; gap detection excludes them.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T498
  CONFLICTS WITH: System type inference being distorted by low-confidence misclassifications
  RESOLUTION: F1289 (IScreenArchetypeClassifierService) outputs confidence; T498 IRON RULES enforce floor

CF-672 : MULTIMODAL_IMAGE_REQUIRED
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: T498 (ScreenSemanticsIR Extraction) runs without rendered image URL (image-only or text-only mode)
  RULE: For screens with ≥500 pixels estimated height, multimodal (image + node tree) is REQUIRED.
        Text-only analysis of complex screens must be blocked.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T498
  CONFLICTS WITH: T491 image rendering failing upstream — pipeline should pause T498, not skip image
  RESOLUTION: T498 ENTRY condition requires imageUrl from T491; if missing, T491 re-triggered first

CF-673 : SEMANTIC_IR_SCHEMA_VIOLATION
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: AI produces ScreenSemanticsIR that violates required schema (missing required fields)
  RULE: Required fields: screen_archetype, ui_controls[], user_actions[], data_entities[], evidence[].
        Missing any = AF-9 fires REJECT; build stops.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T498
  RESOLUTION: F1288 (IPromptTemplateLibraryService) uses strict output schema in prompt
              AF-9 validates schema before downstream use

CF-674 : ENTITY_NAMING_INCONSISTENCY
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Same data entity referenced by different names across screens within same file
           (e.g., "Product" on one screen, "Item" on another)
  RULE: Entity naming must be consistent within a fileKey. Contradiction detected by F1335 triggers
        CROSS_SCREEN_ENTITY_CONFLICT; judge blocks until human resolves canonical name.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T499, T503
  CONFLICTS WITH: BFA entity registry receiving duplicate entity definitions under different names
  RESOLUTION: F1335 (ICrossScreenConsistencyGateService) detects; judge fires WARNING or BLOCK per policy

CF-675 : CROSS_FLOW_ENTITY_COLLISION
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-31 inferred entity (e.g., "Product") conflicts with same-named entity registered in
           BFA by FLOW-16 (Giant Shop) or FLOW-17 (Freelancer)
  RULE: BFA entity registry is checked. If FLOW-31 infers entity X and FLOW-N has X registered:
        - Same schema → allowed (confirmed shared entity, annotated)
        - Different schema → BLOCKED until schema reconciliation
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T509, T510, T511
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-16 (F249 IProductService), FLOW-17 (F297 IJobPostingService), etc.
  RESOLUTION: F1311 (IModuleCandidateResolverService) checks BFA entity registry before finalizing module map

CF-676 : AI_PROVIDER_FALLBACK_REQUIRED
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Primary AI provider for multimodal call (T498) fails; no fallback configured
  RULE: Multimodal semantic extraction MUST have fallback AI provider configured.
        Single-provider failure with no fallback = pipeline halt + operator alert.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T498
  CONFLICTS WITH: Single-model dependency creating full pipeline failure
  RESOLUTION: IAiProvider (SK-385 (IAiProvider)) + AiDispatcher (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) handle fallback via FREEDOM config
```

---

### GROUP D — Graph & Vector Safety (CF-677–CF-682)

```
CF-677 : GRAPH_NODE_ORPHAN_PREVENTION
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: UI graph node created (T501) without at least one edge connecting to another node
  RULE: Orphan graph nodes (no edges) are only allowed for single-screen files. Multi-screen files with
        orphan nodes flag as NAVIGATION_ISOLATION — not silently ignored.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T501, T502
  RESOLUTION: F1296 (IUIGraphEdgeBuilderService) runs immediately after F1295; orphan check in T501 QUALITY GATES

CF-678 : GRAPHRAG_CROSS_TENANT_LEAK
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: GraphRAG retrieval (T504, F1302) returns nodes from another tenant's graph
  RULE: RAG FABRIC (IRagService) calls with graph strategy MUST include tenantId filter.
        Cross-tenant graph retrieval = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T504
  CONFLICTS WITH: Global system-type and module-dependency graphs (F1299, F1301) which are intentionally
                  global — differentiate: PROJECT GRAPHS are tenant-scoped, ONTOLOGY GRAPHS are global-read-only
  RESOLUTION: F1302 (IGraphRAGRetrievalService) applies tenant filter to project graphs; reads ontology graphs
              in read-only mode without tenant filter

CF-679 : VECTOR_EMBEDDING_STALENESS
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: Screen embedding (F1304) generated for a screen version N but screen has since been updated to
           version N+1 via new ingestion
  RULE: On re-ingestion (T489 version change), all screen embeddings for changed screens MUST be invalidated
        and regenerated. Stale embeddings cause false similarity results.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T505, T489
  RESOLUTION: F1277 (version manager) emits "screens-changed" list; T505 checks embedding version vs screen version

CF-680 : SIMILARITY_SCORE_FLOOR
  SEVERITY: LOW
  TRIGGER: Vector similarity search (T506) returns result with score < 0.6
  RULE: Results below 0.6 similarity are excluded from module candidate scoring. They are optionally surfaced
        as "weak signals" but never counted as evidence.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T506
  RESOLUTION: F1307 (IScreenSimilaritySearchService) applies score floor; results below threshold tagged
              "weak_signal" not "evidence"

CF-681 : MODULE_DEPENDENCY_GRAPH_WRITE_LOCK
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Any FLOW-31 factory attempts to WRITE to the global module dependency graph (F1301)
  RULE: Global ontology graphs (F1299 system type graph, F1301 module dependency graph) are READ-ONLY for
        tenant-level analysis. Only admin-level migrations can write. Runtime write attempt = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T503, T504, T507
  RESOLUTION: Factory F1301 is registered with read-only mode; write attempts are rejected with 403

CF-682 : GRAPH_BUILD_RACE_CONDITION
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Two concurrent ingestion pipelines for same fileKey run T501 graph build simultaneously
  RULE: Graph build for a fileKey MUST be serialized using Redis distributed lock.
        Concurrent build = data corruption risk (duplicate edges, split-brain graph).
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T501
  RESOLUTION: F1295 (IUIGraphNodeBuilderService) acquires Redis lock on "{tenantId}::{fileKey}::graph-build"
              before writing; releases on completion or timeout
```

---

### GROUP E — Module Mapping & System Type Safety (CF-683–CF-689)

```
CF-683 : MODULE_MATRIX_VERSION_MISMATCH
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Module matrix loaded by F1310 (IModuleMatrixLoaderService) has different version than what
           was used for previous module mapping of same fileKey
  RULE: If module matrix version changes mid-project, ALL screen mappings for the project MUST be
        invalidated and re-run. Partial re-mapping (only new screens) = inconsistency risk.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T507, T508
  RESOLUTION: F1310 stores matrix version in each mapping document; version change triggers full re-run alert

CF-684 : SYSTEM_TYPE_SINGLE_SCREEN_PROHIBITION
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: System type inference (T512) attempted on project with only 1 screen
  RULE: System type CANNOT be confidently inferred from a single screen. Minimum 3 screens required.
        Single-screen attempt = INSUFFICIENT_DATA result, not a guess.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T512
  CONFLICTS WITH: Engine making confident system type declaration from minimal data
  RESOLUTION: T512 IRON RULES enforce 3-screen minimum; F1317 returns INSUFFICIENT_DATA if not met

CF-685 : DEPENDENCY_CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Module mapping declares Cart present but Checkout absent, without explicit "cart-only" declaration
  RULE: F1336 (IModuleDependencyConstraintGateService) enforces module matrix dependency rules.
        Unresolved dependency violations = GAP (not error), but must appear in gap report.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T511, T513
  CONFLICTS WITH: Gap completion missing critical flow gaps due to unenforced dependency rules
  RESOLUTION: F1336 emits DEPENDENCY_VIOLATION → F1323 (gap detector) converts to gap item with CRITICAL severity

CF-686 : WIRING_PLAN_HARDCODED_VALUE
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Module wiring plan (F1314) contains hardcoded site-type str (e.g., "system_type: 'shop'")
           instead of config document reference
  RULE: DNA-6 (DynamicController/fabric-first) — ALL system type decisions MUST flow through module config
        documents in Elasticsearch. Hardcoded values = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T509, T510, T511
  IRON RULE: This is the UI fabric-first enforcement for FLOW-31 — no platform-specific hardcoding
  RESOLUTION: F1314 (IModuleWiringPlanService) generates config document references only; AF-7 gate validates

CF-687 : CONFIG_DOC_WITHOUT_SCHEMA
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: F1315 (IConfigDocumentRequirementsService) generates config doc requirement without referencing
           the Genie DNA module schema for that module type
  RULE: Every required config doc (view_definitions, cart_rules, form_definitions, etc.) MUST reference the
        canonical schema from the module-architecture registry.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T510
  RESOLUTION: F1315 loads module schemas from RAG FABRIC before generating requirements

CF-688 : CONFIDENCE_WITHOUT_EVIDENCE
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Module candidate assigned high confidence (>0.7) but has fewer than 2 evidence items
  RULE: Confidence score is derived from evidence count + quality, not from AI self-rating.
        High confidence requires minimum 2 distinct evidence items (nodeId + text at minimum).
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T509, T508
  CONFLICTS WITH: CF-670 (evidence gate) — this is the confidence calibration version
  RESOLUTION: F1312 (IConfidenceScoringService) computes confidence from evidence count; self-rated
              confidence from LLM output is used only as a signal, not the primary score

CF-689 : SYSTEM_TYPE_INFERENCE_CONFLICT
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: System type inference produces two candidates with confidence within 0.1 of each other
           (ambiguous system type — e.g., 0.72 "social" vs 0.68 "marketplace")
  RULE: Ambiguous system type (top-2 candidates within 0.1 confidence) = AMBIGUOUS_SYSTEM_TYPE result.
        Engine presents both candidates to user with evidence. Does NOT auto-select.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T512, T513
  RESOLUTION: F1321 (ISystemTypeRankerService) detects ambiguity and sets result type = AMBIGUOUS
              FLOW orchestrator pauses for user input
```

---

### GROUP F — Gap Completion & Learning Loop Safety (CF-690–CF-693)

```
CF-690 : AUTO_STUB_CRITICAL_ONLY
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: F1328 (IStubGenerationOrchestratorService) auto-generates stubs for MEDIUM/LOW severity gaps
           without explicit admin configuration to do so
  RULE: Auto-stub generation is ONLY allowed for CRITICAL severity gaps by default. MEDIUM/LOW gaps are
        surfaced in gap report but NOT auto-stubbed unless FREEDOM config explicitly enables.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T513
  CONFLICTS WITH: Excessive auto-generated stubs cluttering the project
  RESOLUTION: F1328 FREEDOM config: auto_stub_severities[] defaults to ["CRITICAL"] only

CF-691 : LEARNING_LOOP_BEFORE_BENCHMARK
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: T515 (Learning Loop Orchestration) applies exemplar updates to production corpus WITHOUT first
           running benchmark validation
  RULE: Benchmark validation MUST complete and pass BEFORE any exemplar injection is applied.
        Pre-validation injection = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T515
  IRON RULE: Protects against learning loop causing accuracy regression
  RESOLUTION: T515 IRON RULES enforce benchmark-first sequence; F1333 orchestrates in correct order

CF-692 : CORRECTION_WITHOUT_AUDIT
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: User correction stored (T514) without userId + timestamp in audit log
  RULE: EVERY correction MUST be stored with: screenId, before, after, reason, userId, tenantId, timestamp.
        Missing any audit field = REJECT.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T514
  CONFLICTS WITH: Compliance requirements for AI-assisted classification corrections
  RESOLUTION: F1329 (IFeedbackCorrectionStoreService) validates audit fields before commit
              AF-9 validates correction record completeness

CF-693 : ACCURACY_DROP_HUMAN_GATE
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: T515 benchmark run shows accuracy drop > 5% vs previous run
  RULE: Accuracy regression > 5% MUST trigger HUMAN_REVIEW gate. Engine pauses learning loop updates.
        Auto-continue without human approval = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T515
  IRON RULE: Protects against automated system degrading itself via feedback poisoning
  RESOLUTION: F1333 checks accuracy delta; sends alert; sets status = HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED
              FLOW orchestrator waits for admin approval before applying changes
```

---

## SECTION 2 — STRESS TESTS (ST-405–ST-430)

### Group 1 — Ingestion & Rate Limit Stress (ST-405–ST-410)

```
ST-405 : FIGMA_RATE_LIMIT_BURST
  TESTS: CF-654, CF-661
  SCENARIO: 50 concurrent tenants each submit a different Figma fileKey at the same time.
            All attempt to call Figma REST API simultaneously.
  EXPECTED: Rate limit token bucket (F1278) serializes/throttles API calls.
            No tenant starves; all eventually complete.
            No Figma 429 errors propagate to user layer.
            Redis Streams consumer groups correctly isolate per-tenant plugin events (CF-661).
  FAILURE MODE: Any Figma 429 escalating to user = CF-654 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-406 : VERSION_UNCHANGED_NO_REFETCH
  TESTS: CF-656
  SCENARIO: Same tenant submits same fileKey 3 times. File unchanged between submissions.
  EXPECTED: First submission: fetches + caches. Second + third: version match → skip fetch, use cache.
            API call count = 1, not 3.
  FAILURE MODE: Any submission re-fetching unchanged file = CF-656 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-407 : CROSS_TENANT_DATA_ISOLATION
  TESTS: CF-652, CF-655, CF-665, CF-678
  SCENARIO: Tenant A ingests fileKey "abc". Tenant B queries for fileKey "abc".
  EXPECTED: Tenant B receives zero results (tenantId isolation active on all indices).
            No DesignIR, no graph nodes, no embeddings bleed across.
  FAILURE MODE: Any FLOW-31 data visible cross-tenant = CRITICAL violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-408 : PARTIAL_FILE_FAILURE_RECOVERY
  TESTS: CF-669
  SCENARIO: Figma file has 200 screens. Screens 150–200 fail during T490 (node tree normalization — 
            simulated API timeout on batch 8 of 8).
  EXPECTED: T490 emits PARTIAL_COMPLETE with succeeded=[1..149], failed=[150..200].
            Downstream T495 processes only succeeded=[1..149].
            Retry mechanism re-attempts failed=[150..200] without re-processing succeeded.
  FAILURE MODE: Either silent success (ignoring failed) or full retry of all 200 = CF-669 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-409 : IMAGE_URL_EXPIRY_PIPELINE
  TESTS: CF-660, CF-672
  SCENARIO: T491 renders 50 frame images. Pipeline pauses (e.g., int queue). 31 days later, T498 
            multimodal extraction runs and finds expired image URLs.
  EXPECTED: T498 detects expired URLs → triggers T491 re-render for expired frames → proceeds with fresh URLs.
            No extraction run with expired URLs.
  FAILURE MODE: T498 using expired URLs (returning 403 from storage) = CF-660 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-410 : COMPONENT_SIGNATURE_DRIFT_REPROCESS
  TESTS: CF-668
  SCENARIO: Tenant ingests a file. Designer updates a shared component "ProductCard" (adds 3 new fields).
            File version changes. Re-ingestion runs.
  EXPECTED: F1282 detects >30% signature drift on "ProductCard".
            All screens using "ProductCard" are flagged for re-processing in T494.
            Module mappings for those screens are invalidated.
  FAILURE MODE: Drift ignored = stale mappings for affected screens = CF-668 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL
```

---

### Group 2 — AI Semantic & Evidence Stress (ST-411–ST-417)

```
ST-411 : HALLUCINATION_EVIDENCE_GATE
  TESTS: CF-670, CF-671
  SCENARIO: AI (T498) produces ScreenSemanticsIR claiming "Checkout" module with zero evidence items.
            Confidence: 0.82. No nodeId, no text, no component name referenced.
  EXPECTED: F1334 (evidence coverage gate) fires EVIDENCE_COVERAGE_GATE_FAIL.
            AF-9 blocks promotion. Screen goes to UNCLASSIFIED + human review queue.
  FAILURE MODE: High-confidence claim with zero evidence passes = CF-670 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-412 : LOW_CONFIDENCE_ARCHETYPE
  TESTS: CF-671
  SCENARIO: Screen has only a navigation bar and a blank content area. AI produces:
            archetype confidence: {"list": 0.31, "form": 0.28, "detail": 0.25}.
            All below 0.4 floor.
  EXPECTED: Screen tagged UNCLASSIFIED. NOT assigned "list" (highest). Not used in system type inference.
            Surfaced in human review queue with raw evidence.
  FAILURE MODE: "list" assigned because it's highest = CF-671 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-413 : MULTIMODAL_REQUIRED_ENFORCEMENT
  TESTS: CF-672
  SCENARIO: T491 fails for 5 complex screens (>500px height). T498 attempts text-only analysis for those 5.
  EXPECTED: T498 rejects text-only mode for these screens. Pauses. Triggers T491 re-render.
            Only runs after images available.
  FAILURE MODE: Text-only analysis runs on complex screens = CF-672 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-414 : ENTITY_NAMING_COLLISION
  TESTS: CF-674, CF-675
  SCENARIO: File has 20 screens. Screen 3 calls entity "Product". Screen 12 calls same entity "Item".
            FLOW-16 (Giant Shop) already has "Product" registered in BFA.
  EXPECTED: F1335 (cross-screen consistency gate) fires CROSS_SCREEN_ENTITY_CONFLICT for "Product" vs "Item".
            F1311 (module candidate resolver) detects FLOW-16 "Product" BFA registration.
            Judge blocks until user selects canonical name.
            BFA confirms selected name matches FLOW-16 schema or flags schema difference.
  FAILURE MODE: Both names accepted without reconciliation = CF-674 + CF-675 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-415 : AI_PROVIDER_FAILOVER
  TESTS: CF-676
  SCENARIO: Primary vision model (e.g., Claude claude-sonnet-4-6) returns 503 during T498.
            Secondary provider (e.g., GPT-4o) is configured as fallback.
  EXPECTED: AiDispatcher (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) automatically routes to secondary provider.
            Pipeline continues. Result annotated with "provider: fallback" in audit log.
  FAILURE MODE: Pipeline halts with no fallback attempt = CF-676 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-416 : TYPED_MODEL_DETECTION
  TESTS: CF-658
  SCENARIO: AI-generated service code (via AF-1 Genesis) for FLOW-31 accidentally creates:
            class ScreenModel: { public str Archetype  ... }
  EXPECTED: AF-7 (Compliance) gate detects class declaration with "Model" suffix in generated service.
            Fires DNA-1 violation (parse_document rule). Build blocked.
  FAILURE MODE: Typed model class passes into codebase = CF-658 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-417 : SEMANTIC_SCHEMA_INCOMPLETE
  TESTS: CF-673
  SCENARIO: AI outputs ScreenSemanticsIR missing "user_actions[]" field (forgot to populate it).
  EXPECTED: AF-9 validates required schema fields. Missing user_actions[] = REJECT.
            Screen re-queued for T498 with AF-3 fetching updated prompt template.
  FAILURE MODE: Incomplete schema accepted downstream = CF-673 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL
```

---

### Group 3 — Graph & Module Mapping Stress (ST-418–ST-424)

```
ST-418 : CONCURRENT_GRAPH_BUILD
  TESTS: CF-682
  SCENARIO: Two identical pipeline runs for same fileKey triggered simultaneously (user float-click).
  EXPECTED: First run acquires Redis distributed lock. Second run detects lock → waits or yields.
            Only ONE graph built. No duplicate nodes/edges. Lock released after completion.
  FAILURE MODE: Both runs write simultaneously → split-brain graph = CF-682 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-419 : GRAPHRAG_TENANT_ISOLATION
  TESTS: CF-678
  SCENARIO: Tenant A has graph with "Product → Cart → Checkout" flow. Tenant B queries GraphRAG.
  EXPECTED: Tenant B's GraphRAG (T504) retrieves zero nodes from Tenant A's graph.
            Global ontology graphs (system_type_graph, module_dependency_graph) are readable by both,
            but project-specific graphs are isolated.
  FAILURE MODE: Tenant B retrieves Tenant A's project graph nodes = CF-678 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-420 : MODULE_MATRIX_VERSION_UPGRADE
  TESTS: CF-683
  SCENARIO: Mid-project, admin updates module matrix (v2 → v3, adds new "Loyalty" module).
            Project has 50 screens with mappings based on v2.
  EXPECTED: F1310 detects matrix version mismatch for this project.
            ALL 50 screen mappings invalidated. Full re-run triggered (not partial).
            Alert sent to project admin.
  FAILURE MODE: Only new screens re-mapped; existing 50 use stale v2 mappings = CF-683 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-421 : SINGLE_SCREEN_SYSTEM_TYPE_BLOCK
  TESTS: CF-684
  SCENARIO: User submits Figma file with only 1 screen (landing page skeleton).
  EXPECTED: T512 fires INSUFFICIENT_DATA. System type = UNKNOWN. Gap completion skipped.
            User presented with message: "Minimum 3 screens required for system type inference."
  FAILURE MODE: System type inferred from 1 screen (e.g., "landing page") = CF-684 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-422 : AMBIGUOUS_SYSTEM_TYPE_PAUSE
  TESTS: CF-689
  SCENARIO: 15-screen file produces: social confidence 0.71, marketplace confidence 0.68.
            Within 0.1 threshold.
  EXPECTED: T512 returns AMBIGUOUS_SYSTEM_TYPE with both candidates + evidence.
            FlowOrchestrator pauses pipeline. Admin notified. User selects system type.
            Pipeline resumes with selected type.
  FAILURE MODE: Auto-selection of "social" (highest) without user confirmation = CF-689 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-423 : HARDCODED_SYSTEM_TYPE_DETECTION
  TESTS: CF-686
  SCENARIO: AF-1 (Genesis) generates wiring plan code that contains str constant "system_type = 'shop'"
            hardcoded in service logic.
  EXPECTED: AF-7 (Compliance) gate detects hardcoded system type str.
            DNA-6 (fabric-first) violation fired. Build blocked.
  FAILURE MODE: Hardcoded str passes compliance check = CF-686 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-424 : DEPENDENCY_GAP_DETECTION
  TESTS: CF-685
  SCENARIO: File has Cart + Product screens but no Checkout screen.
            Module matrix says: Cart → requires → Checkout.
  EXPECTED: F1336 (dependency constraint gate) fires DEPENDENCY_VIOLATION.
            F1323 (gap detector) converts to gap item: severity=CRITICAL, missing=Checkout.
            Gap report includes this item. User notified.
  FAILURE MODE: Dependency violation silent or treated as warning-only = CF-685 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL
```

---

### Group 4 — Gap Completion & Learning Loop Stress (ST-425–ST-430)

```
ST-425 : AUTO_STUB_SEVERITY_ENFORCEMENT
  TESTS: CF-690
  SCENARIO: Gap report has: 2 CRITICAL gaps, 3 MEDIUM gaps, 5 LOW gaps.
            Default FREEDOM config: auto_stub_severities = ["CRITICAL"].
  EXPECTED: F1328 auto-generates stubs ONLY for the 2 CRITICAL gaps.
            MEDIUM and LOW gaps appear in gap report but no stubs generated.
  FAILURE MODE: Stubs generated for MEDIUM/LOW gaps without explicit config = CF-690 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-426 : LEARNING_LOOP_BENCHMARK_FIRST
  TESTS: CF-691
  SCENARIO: 15 corrections accumulated. T515 learning loop triggered.
            Benchmark run starts but times out after 5 minutes (simulated slow ES query).
  EXPECTED: T515 waits for benchmark completion. Exemplar injection NOT applied until benchmark returns.
            If benchmark times out → BENCHMARK_TIMEOUT result → learning cycle aborted for this run.
            Retry scheduled for next cycle.
  FAILURE MODE: Exemplar injection applied before benchmark timeout = CF-691 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-427 : CORRECTION_AUDIT_COMPLETENESS
  TESTS: CF-692
  SCENARIO: User submits correction missing the "reason" field (just provides before/after labels).
  EXPECTED: F1329 validation rejects correction. Returns error: "reason field required".
            Correction NOT stored. Audit log not written with incomplete record.
            User prompted to provide reason.
  FAILURE MODE: Partial correction stored without reason = CF-692 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-428 : ACCURACY_DROP_HUMAN_GATE
  TESTS: CF-693
  SCENARIO: T515 benchmark: previous accuracy = 0.89, current accuracy = 0.82.
            Delta = -0.07 (> 5% threshold).
  EXPECTED: F1333 detects 7% accuracy drop. Fires ACCURACY_REGRESSION alert.
            Learning loop paused. Status = HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED.
            Exemplar updates NOT applied. Admin receives alert with before/after metrics.
            Pipeline resumes only after admin approves.
  FAILURE MODE: Updates applied despite >5% drop = CF-693 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-429 : VECTOR_STALE_EMBEDDING_INVALIDATION
  TESTS: CF-679
  SCENARIO: File v3 ingested. Screens 10-20 changed from v2. T505 runs.
  EXPECTED: F1277 version manager provides list of changed screen IDs.
            F1304 invalidates existing embeddings for screen IDs 10-20.
            New embeddings generated only for 10-20 (unchanged screens reuse v2 embeddings).
  FAILURE MODE: Stale v2 embeddings used for screens 10-20 in similarity search = CF-679 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL

ST-430 : GLOBAL_GRAPH_WRITE_PREVENTION
  TESTS: CF-681
  SCENARIO: T503 (module signature graph build) mistakenly attempts to write a new node to the global
            system_type_graph (F1299, read-only ontology).
  EXPECTED: DATABASE FABRIC IDatabaseService rejects write call with 403 (read-only factory mode).
            DataProcessResult[T] returns Failure("read-only index"). Pipeline logs write-blocked event.
            Admin alerted.
  FAILURE MODE: Write succeeds to global ontology graph = CF-681 violation.
  VERDICT: PASS / FAIL
```

# FLOW-32: SHARABLE FLOWS & RAG TEMPLATE MARKETPLACE — BFA STRESS TEST
## Date: 2026-03-03 | BFA Rules: CF-715-CF-738 (24 rules) | Stress Tests: ST-431-ST-454 (24 tests)
## Extends: V62_BFA_STRESS_TEST_MERGED.md (CF-1-CF-714, ST-1-ST-430)

---

## SECTION 1 — BFA CONFLICT RULES (CF-715–CF-738)

### GROUP A — Template Package Integrity (CF-715–CF-719)

```
CF-715 : TEMPLATE_MARKETPLACE_ENTITY_COLLISION
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 template_packages index or marketplace_listings index collides with FLOW-16 (Giant Shop) product entity or FLOW-17 (Freelancer) service listing
  RULE: Template packages use "tmpl::" prefix namespace. Product entities use "prod::" (FLOW-16). Freelancer services use "frlc::" (FLOW-17). Collision = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T516, T519
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-16 (F368 IProductCatalogService), FLOW-17 (F580 IFreelancerListingService)
  RESOLUTION: Engine prefixes all FLOW-32 ES document IDs with "tmpl::{tenantId}::{packageId}"

CF-716 : TEMPLATE_VS_FREELANCER_LISTING
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 marketplace listing shares index or API path with FLOW-17 freelancer marketplace
  RULE: FLOW-32 marketplace_listings index is separate from FLOW-17 freelancer_listings. API paths: /api/templates/* vs /api/freelancers/*. No overlap.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T519, T520
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-17 (F581 IFreelancerSearchService)
  RESOLUTION: Separate ES indices, separate API path prefixes, DynamicController routes by entity type

CF-717 : TEMPLATE_VS_FLOW_DEFINITION_NAMESPACE
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Installed template flow definition IDs collide with FLOW-18 visual flow editor's native flow definition IDs
  RULE: Installed template flows use "tmpl-inst::{consumerTenantId}::{installId}::*" prefix. Native flows use "flow::{tenantId}::*" prefix. Collision = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T522, T525
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-18 (F472 IFlowExecutionService, F466 IFlowDefinitionRegistryService)
  RESOLUTION: Prefix strategy enforced by installation executor (T522)

CF-718 : MANIFEST_FACTORY_REGISTRY_INTEGRITY
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Template manifest declares factory dependencies that don't exist in F1-F1418 registry
  RULE: Every factory reference in a manifest MUST exist in the global factory registry. Phantom factory reference = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T517
  CONFLICTS WITH: All flows (factory registry is global)
  RESOLUTION: Manifest validation (T517) cross-references against authoritative factory registry

CF-719 : TEMPLATE_SIGNING_VS_CICD_SIGNING
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 artifact signatures share namespace or key infrastructure with FLOW-19 CI/CD code signing
  RULE: Template artifact signatures stored in "tmpl-sig::" namespace. CI/CD signatures stored in "cicd-sig::" namespace. Key material is separate.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T518
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-19 (code signing infrastructure)
  RESOLUTION: Separate signature indices, separate key namespaces, shared cosign infrastructure with namespace isolation
```

### GROUP B — Installation & Data Isolation (CF-720–CF-726)

```
CF-720 : LISTING_VS_PRODUCT_ENTITY
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Marketplace listing entity event (template.listed) collides with product listing event (product.listed) in FLOW-16
  RULE: Template events use "template.*" event namespace. Product events use "product.*". Event bus routing must distinguish.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T519
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-16 (Giant Shop product events)
  RESOLUTION: Event namespace prefixes enforced by QUEUE FABRIC routing rules

CF-721 : LISTING_VS_FREELANCER_SERVICE
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: Template marketplace category taxonomy overlaps with FLOW-17 freelancer service categories
  RULE: Template categories stored in marketplace_categories index (FLOW-32). Freelancer categories stored in freelancer_categories (FLOW-17). Separate taxonomies.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T519, T520
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-17 freelancer category system
  RESOLUTION: Separate category indices, no shared taxonomy

CF-722 : SEARCH_INDEX_NAMESPACE
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 marketplace search index aliases collide with FLOW-16 product search aliases
  RULE: Template search alias: "tmpl-search-*". Product search alias: "prod-search-*". No shared aliases.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T520
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-16 product search
  RESOLUTION: Index alias naming convention enforced

CF-723 : DEPENDENCY_VS_CONSUMER_FACTORY_SET
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Template installation requires factories that conflict with consumer's existing factory versions
  RULE: Dependency resolution (T521) must detect version conflicts between template's required factory versions and consumer's installed versions. Conflict = BLOCK with explanation.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T521
  CONFLICTS WITH: Consumer's existing installed flows (dynamic — varies per tenant)
  RESOLUTION: T521 dependency resolution checks consumer's full factory set before proceeding

CF-724 : INSTALLED_FLOW_VS_EXISTING_FLOWS
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Installed template creates flow definitions that conflict with consumer's existing flows (entity names, event schemas, API paths)
  RULE: BFA must run on installed flow definitions against consumer's full flow inventory. Any CRITICAL conflict = installation blocked.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T522
  CONFLICTS WITH: All of consumer's existing flows (dynamic per tenant)
  RESOLUTION: T522 triggers BFA revalidation post-install; critical conflicts block

CF-725 : METERING_VS_BILLING_EVENTS
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 template.* metering events collide with FLOW-08 billing events in shared queue
  RULE: Template metering uses "template.*" event prefix. Billing uses "billing.*" prefix. Both flow through QUEUE FABRIC but with separate consumer groups.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T531, T532
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-08 payment/billing events
  RESOLUTION: Separate consumer groups in Redis Streams, separate processing pipelines

CF-726 : CONNECTOR_BINDING_VS_CREDENTIAL_VAULT
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 connector bindings reference credentials that overlap with FLOW-15 DevOps credential vault namespace
  RULE: Template connector bindings use "tmpl-conn::" prefix. DevOps credentials use "devops-cred::". secret_ref always points to correct namespace.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T523
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-15 (F484 credential vault)
  RESOLUTION: Namespace-aware secret_ref resolution
```

### GROUP C — Upgrade & Migration Safety (CF-727–CF-730)

```
CF-727 : UPGRADE_VS_FLOW_VERSION_EVENTS
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 upgrade.detected events collide with FLOW-18 flow.version.published events
  RULE: Template upgrade events: "template.upgrade.*". Flow version events: "flow.version.*". Separate event streams.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T524
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-18 (flow versioning system)
  RESOLUTION: Event namespace separation

CF-728 : MIGRATION_VS_CONSUMER_BFA
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Template migration (T525) modifies consumer's installed flow definitions in a way that introduces new BFA conflicts
  RULE: Every migration MUST be followed by BFA revalidation (T526). Migration without revalidation = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T525, T526
  CONFLICTS WITH: Consumer's full flow set (dynamic)
  RESOLUTION: T526 is mandatory post-migration gate; cannot be skipped

CF-729 : REVALIDATION_COVERAGE
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Post-upgrade BFA revalidation doesn't cover all consumer's flows
  RULE: BFA revalidation (T526) must scan ALL consumer-installed flows + custom flows. Partial scan = BUILD FAILURE.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T526
  CONFLICTS WITH: All consumer flows
  RESOLUTION: Revalidation loads consumer's full flow inventory; completeness verified

CF-730 : ROLLBACK_STATE_INTEGRITY
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: Rollback (T527) doesn't fully restore pre-upgrade state
  RULE: Post-rollback state MUST match pre-upgrade snapshot exactly. Partial restore = ESCALATION.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T527
  CONFLICTS WITH: Pre-upgrade snapshot integrity
  RESOLUTION: Byte-level comparison of rolled-back state vs stored snapshot
```

### GROUP D — RAG & Revenue Safety (CF-731–CF-734)

```
CF-731 : RAG_BLUEPRINT_VS_FLOW29_CONFIG
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 RAG blueprint config namespace collides with FLOW-29 RAG Deep Research adaptive config
  RULE: Template RAG blueprints use "tmpl-rag::" prefix. FLOW-29 adaptive RAG uses "arag::" prefix. No shared config keys.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T528, T530
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-29 (F1176-F1247 RAG deep research)
  RESOLUTION: Namespace prefixes enforced in RAG FABRIC config layer

CF-732 : RAG_BLUEPRINT_VS_CONSUMER_RAG
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Installing RAG blueprint overwrites consumer's existing RAG configuration
  RULE: RAG blueprint installation (T530) uses MERGE strategy by default. Consumer's existing RAG config is preserved. Override requires explicit confirmation.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T530
  CONFLICTS WITH: Consumer's existing RAG setup
  RESOLUTION: Merge/replace/additive strategy selection (FREEDOM config)

CF-733 : INSTALLED_RAG_VS_EXISTING_FLOWS
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: Installed RAG blueprint's metadata schema conflicts with consumer's existing RAG metadata schemas
  RULE: Schema field name collisions detected before installation. Conflict = WARN + prompt for field aliasing.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T530
  CONFLICTS WITH: Consumer's existing RAG metadata schemas
  RESOLUTION: Field aliasing wizard during RAG blueprint installation

CF-734 : SETTLEMENT_VS_PAYMENT_PROCESSING
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 settlement/payout transactions collide with FLOW-08 payment processing
  RULE: Template settlements use "tmpl-settle::" namespace. FLOW-08 payments use "pay::". Settlement triggers payout via FLOW-08's payment fabric, but with separate transaction IDs.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T532
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-08 (payment processing)
  RESOLUTION: Settlement creates payment instruction with "tmpl-settle::" prefix; FLOW-08 processes as external payment type
```

### GROUP E — Analytics & Sandbox Safety (CF-735–CF-738)

```
CF-735 : ANALYTICS_VS_DATA_WAREHOUSE
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 template analytics metrics collide with FLOW-13 data warehouse dimensions
  RULE: Template analytics stored in FLOW-32 specific indices (funnel_events, quality_scores). FLOW-13 can ingest from these indices but doesn't write to them.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T533
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-13 (data warehouse)
  RESOLUTION: Read-only ingestion from FLOW-32 indices into FLOW-13 warehouse (one-way)

CF-736 : FRAUD_VS_FREELANCER_TRUST
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 fraud detection signals overlap with FLOW-17 freelancer trust system
  RULE: Template trust_incidents and FLOW-17 freelancer_trust_incidents are separate indices. Fraud patterns are domain-specific and not shared.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T534
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-17 (freelancer trust scoring)
  RESOLUTION: Separate fraud detection pipelines, separate indices, potentially shared AI models via AI ENGINE FABRIC

CF-737 : SANDBOX_VS_FLOW_EXECUTION
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 sandbox execution accidentally accesses production flow execution engine (FLOW-18)
  RULE: Sandbox environments use synthetic tenantId ("sandbox::{uuid}"), never consumer's real tenantId. Sandbox flow execution routed to isolated orchestrator instance. Production FlowOrchestrator NEVER processes sandbox flows.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T535
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-18 (FlowOrchestrator production), FLOW-19 (CI/CD sandboxes)
  RESOLUTION: Synthetic tenantId + isolated orchestrator instance + network egress allowlist

CF-738 : SANDBOX_VS_CICD_SANDBOX
  SEVERITY: HIGH
  TRIGGER: FLOW-32 template sandbox shares infrastructure with FLOW-19 CI/CD sandbox
  RULE: Template sandboxes and CI/CD sandboxes use separate resource pools. No shared compute/storage.
  AFFECTED TASK TYPES: T535
  CONFLICTS WITH: FLOW-19 (CI/CD sandbox infrastructure)
  RESOLUTION: Separate sandbox resource pools, separate namespace prefixes
```

## SECTION 2 — STRESS TESTS (ST-431–ST-454)

### STRESS TEST GROUP A — Publish Pipeline (ST-431–ST-436)

```
ST-431 : PUBLISH_SECRET_LEAK_TEST
  SCENARIO: Publisher submits template containing AWS access key in flow definition node config
  EXPECTED: T516 DLP scanner (F1348 via AI ENGINE FABRIC) detects secret → package REJECTED
  VALIDATES: CF-715 (template integrity), IR-1 of T516
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-432 : PUBLISH_INVALID_MANIFEST_TEST
  SCENARIO: Publisher submits template with manifest referencing non-existent factory F9999
  EXPECTED: T517 manifest validation rejects → DataProcessResult.Failure with "factory F9999 not in registry"
  VALIDATES: CF-718 (manifest factory registry integrity), IR-2 of T517
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-433 : PUBLISH_CYCLIC_DEPENDENCY_TEST
  SCENARIO: Template manifest declares A depends on B, B depends on C, C depends on A
  EXPECTED: T517 cycle detection rejects → BUILD FAILURE
  VALIDATES: CF-718, IR-1 of T517
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-434 : PUBLISH_UNSIGNED_ARTIFACT_TEST
  SCENARIO: Template attempts to publish with skipped T518 signing step
  EXPECTED: T519 listing publication rejects → IR-1 of T519 ("listing without signed artifact")
  VALIDATES: CF-719 (signing integrity)
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-435 : PUBLISH_DUPLICATE_VERSION_TEST
  SCENARIO: Publisher attempts to publish version "1.2.3" when "1.2.3" already exists for same package
  EXPECTED: T516 rejects → IR-3 ("version semver must be unique")
  VALIDATES: Immutability invariant, SK-330
  SEVERITY: HIGH

ST-436 : PUBLISH_PII_IN_TEMPLATE_TEST
  SCENARIO: Template flow definition contains hardcoded email addresses and phone numbers in node configs
  EXPECTED: T516 DLP scanner (AI ENGINE FABRIC) detects PII → package REJECTED or PII redacted based on policy
  VALIDATES: CF-715, IR-1 of T516
  SEVERITY: HIGH
```

### STRESS TEST GROUP B — Installation Safety (ST-437–ST-443)

```
ST-437 : INSTALL_TENANT_ISOLATION_TEST
  SCENARIO: Template installed by consumer tenant → verify all cloned assets carry consumer's tenantId, not publisher's
  EXPECTED: Every document in installed_flow_definitions has consumerTenantId. Zero documents with publisherTenantId.
  VALIDATES: CF-724, DNA-5, IR-1 of T522
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-438 : INSTALL_LINKED_VERSION_PIN_TEST
  SCENARIO: LINKED mode installation → publisher publishes new version → consumer's installation stays on pinned version
  EXPECTED: Consumer's execution resolves from pinned version, not latest
  VALIDATES: IR-2 of T522 (LINKED mode pins exact semver)
  SEVERITY: HIGH

ST-439 : INSTALL_MISSING_REQUIRED_DEPENDENCY_TEST
  SCENARIO: Consumer's tenant missing a required factory declared in template manifest
  EXPECTED: T521 dependency resolution → BLOCKED with specific missing factory ID
  VALIDATES: CF-723, IR-1 of T521
  SEVERITY: HIGH

ST-440 : INSTALL_CONNECTOR_TYPE_MISMATCH_TEST
  SCENARIO: Template declares PostgreSQL connector_ref, consumer binds Redis connector
  EXPECTED: T523 connector rebinding → REJECT with type mismatch
  VALIDATES: CF-726, IR-2 of T523
  SEVERITY: HIGH

ST-441 : INSTALL_SECRET_VALUE_LEAK_TEST
  SCENARIO: Verify connector_bindings index never contains actual secret values, only secret_ref IDs
  EXPECTED: Full scan of connector_bindings — zero records contain plaintext secrets
  VALIDATES: CF-726, IR-1 of T523
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-442 : INSTALL_BFA_CONFLICT_TEST
  SCENARIO: Installing template that creates entity names colliding with consumer's existing FLOW-16 products
  EXPECTED: T522 triggers BFA → CF-724 detects collision → installation BLOCKED
  VALIDATES: CF-724 (installed flow vs existing flows)
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-443 : INSTALL_METERING_EVENT_DELIVERY_TEST
  SCENARIO: Template installed → verify metering event emitted with both publisherTenantId and consumerTenantId
  EXPECTED: Metering event in queue with all required dimensions
  VALIDATES: CF-725, IR-3 of T522, IR-1 of T531
  SEVERITY: HIGH
```

### STRESS TEST GROUP C — Upgrade & Rollback (ST-444–ST-448)

```
ST-444 : UPGRADE_FORCED_SECURITY_PATCH_TEST
  SCENARIO: Publisher marks new version as security-critical → consumer has NOTIFY_ONLY policy
  EXPECTED: T524 detects → FORCED_SECURITY overrides consumer policy → upgrade event emitted regardless
  VALIDATES: CF-727, IR-1 of T524 (forced security patches)
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-445 : MIGRATION_ATOMIC_FAILURE_TEST
  SCENARIO: Migration (T525) fails mid-execution (e.g., 3 of 5 nodes migrated, then error)
  EXPECTED: Full rollback to pre-migration state → zero partial modifications
  VALIDATES: CF-728, IR-1 of T525 (atomic migration)
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-446 : MIGRATION_FREEDOM_PRESERVATION_TEST
  SCENARIO: Consumer customized FREEDOM config on installed template → upgrade to new version
  EXPECTED: Post-migration FREEDOM values match consumer's customizations, not publisher defaults
  VALIDATES: IR-2 of T525 (preserve custom FREEDOM values)
  SEVERITY: HIGH

ST-447 : REVALIDATION_FULL_COVERAGE_TEST
  SCENARIO: Consumer has 50 installed flows → template upgrade → BFA revalidation runs
  EXPECTED: Revalidation report covers all 50 flows, not just the upgraded template
  VALIDATES: CF-729 (revalidation coverage)
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-448 : ROLLBACK_STATE_MATCH_TEST
  SCENARIO: Migration + BFA critical conflict → auto-rollback (T527) → compare state
  EXPECTED: Byte-level comparison: rolled-back state == pre-upgrade snapshot
  VALIDATES: CF-730 (rollback state integrity), IR-1 of T527
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
```

### STRESS TEST GROUP D — RAG & Revenue (ST-449–ST-451)

```
ST-449 : RAG_BLUEPRINT_DATA_LEAK_TEST
  SCENARIO: RAG blueprint export (T528) → verify zero actual documents/embeddings/query logs in blueprint
  EXPECTED: Blueprint contains only strategy config, chunking rules, ranking weights, prompt IDs. Zero data content.
  VALIDATES: CF-731, IR-1/IR-2 of T528
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-450 : RAG_INSTALL_PRESERVE_DATA_TEST
  SCENARIO: Consumer has existing RAG data → installs RAG blueprint → verify consumer data untouched
  EXPECTED: Consumer's existing data indices unchanged. Blueprint applies CONFIG only.
  VALIDATES: CF-732, IR-1 of T530
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-451 : SETTLEMENT_DOUBLE_BILLING_TEST
  SCENARIO: Settlement job (T532) runs twice for same period due to scheduler retry
  EXPECTED: Second run detects idempotency key → no duplicate settlement record
  VALIDATES: CF-734, IR-3 of T532, DNA-7
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
```

### STRESS TEST GROUP E — Sandbox & Fraud (ST-452–ST-454)

```
ST-452 : SANDBOX_PRODUCTION_ACCESS_TEST
  SCENARIO: Sandbox execution (T535) attempts to connect to production database endpoint
  EXPECTED: Network egress blocked → connection refused → sandbox uses stub connector only
  VALIDATES: CF-737, IR-1 of T535
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL

ST-453 : FRAUD_SELF_INSTALL_LOOP_TEST
  SCENARIO: Publisher creates consumer tenant → installs own template → triggers metering events → claims revenue
  EXPECTED: T534 fraud detection identifies publisher-consumer loop → trust_incident created → human review required
  VALIDATES: CF-736, IR-1/IR-2 of T534
  SEVERITY: HIGH

ST-454 : FRAUD_FALSE_POSITIVE_PROTECTION_TEST
  SCENARIO: Legitimate high-volume template gets flagged by anomaly detection
  EXPECTED: Trust incident created BUT no auto-suspension. Human review gate required before any action.
  VALIDATES: IR-2 of T534 (human review required before suspension)
  SEVERITY: HIGH
```

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FLOW-33 — System Initiation: Self-Building Bootstrap Engine
# Added: 2026-03-03 | Post FLOW-32 state
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# FLOW-33 — System Initiation: Self-Building Bootstrap Engine
## BFA CONFLICT RULES & STRESS TESTS

**FLOW Reference:** FLOW-33  
**Backward Compatibility:** CF-1-CF-738 and ST-1-ST-454 UNCHANGED  
**New BFA Rules:** CF-739 through CF-750  
**New Stress Tests:** ST-455 through ST-462  

---

## BFA RULES — FLOW-33

### CF-739 — Plan Bundle Oracle Validation Before Registry Writes

**Severity:** CRITICAL  
**Entities:** PlanBundle, FactoryRegistry, TaskTypeRegistry, SkillRegistry, BFARuleRegistry  
**Events:** PlanBundleUploaded → PlanBundleValidated | PlanBundleRejected  
**APIs:** F1420:ValidatePlanBundle, F1420:CompileRegistry  

**Conflict:**
Registry compilation (F1420:CompileRegistry) begins BEFORE ValidatePlanBundle confirms ranges match the session state oracle (F1-F1418, T1-T535, SK-1-SK-345, CF-1-CF-738). Result: partial or corrupt registries that pass integrity checks but contain wrong artifact counts.

**Resolution:**
ValidatePlanBundle MUST emit PlanBundleValidated before any CompileRegistry call is triggered. FlowOrchestrator enforces ordering via `dependsOn` on the import_plan_bundle node. If ValidatePlanBundle returns valid=false, flow emits PlanBundleRejected and routes to DLQ — NO registry mutations occur.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-install-gate` (Elasticsearch index tracking bundle validation states)

**Detection Pattern:**
```
IF (event: CompileRegistry) AND NOT (event: PlanBundleValidated for same bundleId)
→ BFA VIOLATION: halt compilation, emit BfaViolationDetected { cfId: 'CF-739' }
```

**Cross-Flow Impact:** FLOW-01–FLOW-32 registries (F1-F1418, T1-T535) are read-only from this flow's perspective. CF-739 ensures FLOW-33 additions (F1419-F1428, T536-T542) do not overwrite existing ranges.

---

### CF-740 — Outbox Ordering: Phase State Before Event Emission

**Severity:** CRITICAL  
**Entities:** BootstrapPhase, BootstrapSentinel  
**Events:** BootstrapPhaseAdvanced, BootstrapCompleted  
**APIs:** F1419:AdvanceBootstrapPhase, F1419:SetBootstrappedSentinel  

**Conflict:**
BootstrapCompleted event is emitted BEFORE BootstrapSentinel is written to Elasticsearch. If the process restarts, the sentinel is missing, causing a second bootstrap run — float-registration of schemas, registries, and graph nodes.

**Resolution:**
Outbox pattern: F1419:SetBootstrappedSentinel writes sentinel doc to ES with `status: 'committed'`, THEN emits BootstrapCompleted via queue. On restart, F1419:CheckBootstrapStatus reads sentinel — if present and version ≥ desired, emit BootstrapCompleted again (idempotent) and exit. Never re-execute bootstrap phases.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-bootstrap-phases` (tracks committed phase states per version)

**Detection Pattern:**
```
IF (event: BootstrapCompleted) AND NOT (document: bootstrap-sentinel in ES with status='committed')
→ VIOLATION: process likely died mid-write; next run will re-bootstrap (data integrity risk)
```

---

### CF-741 — Schema-Before-Event: Schema Registry Before First Use

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Entities:** SchemaRegistry, EventSchema  
**Events:** BootstrapCompleted, PlanBundleImported, GraphCatalogSeeded, FamilyImplementationCompleted  
**APIs:** F194:RegisterSchema  

**Conflict:**
Bootstrap flow emits events (e.g., CoreSchemasRegistered) whose schemas have not yet been registered in the SchemaRegistry. Consumers that validate incoming events against registered schemas will reject them.

**Resolution:**
Node `register_core_schemas` (F194:RegisterSchema) MUST be the first non-sentinel-check node in T536 DAG. All FLOW-33 event schemas (BootstrapCompleted, PlanBundleImported, GraphCatalogSeeded, FamilyImplementationCompleted, RegressionRunRequested, CapabilityGapDetected) registered before any node that emits them.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-schema-registry` (tracks which schemas are registered per tenant/scope)

---

### CF-742 — Atomic Registry Compilation: No Partial Updates on Failure

**Severity:** CRITICAL  
**Entities:** FactoryRegistry, TaskTypeRegistry, SkillRegistry  
**Events:** RegistryCompiled, RegistryCompilationFailed  
**APIs:** F1420:CompileRegistry  

**Conflict:**
F1420:CompileRegistry processes 7 registry types sequentially. If it fails mid-way (e.g., after factory_registry but before skill_registry), the system has a partial state — factory_registry up-to-date but skill_registry stale — causing mismatches in AF-4 retrieval and BFA rule lookups.

**Resolution:**
CompileRegistry uses a bundle version marker. On failure: write `registry-compilation-state` to ES with `status: 'partial', lastCompletedType, bundleId`. On retry: resume from `lastCompletedType`. All compiled docs carry `bundleVersion` — consumers filter by version to avoid mixing partial state.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-registry-compilation` (tracks bundle version + completion state per registry type)

---

### CF-743 — Graph Fabric Isolation: No Direct Graph Driver in Service Code

**Severity:** CRITICAL  
**Entities:** GraphCatalogSeeder, ProviderImpl, FlowDef  
**APIs:** F1423:ExecuteGraphQuery, F1423:SeedCatalogGraph  

**Conflict:**
Service code directly imports Neo4j driver (or Neptune SDK) to execute Cypher queries, bypassing F1423:IGraphCatalogSeeder. Result: graph provider becomes a hard dependency — swapping to Neptune requires code changes (violates fabric-first rule).

**Resolution:**
ALL graph operations MUST go through F1423:IGraphCatalogSeeder.ExecuteGraphQuery with a query template name. Cypher strings MUST reside in stored query templates (prompt_registry) — not embedded in service files. AF-7 (Compliance) checks for `Neo4jDriver`, `IAsyncSession`, `driver.AsyncSession()` imports — any match = BUILD FAILURE.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-fabric-isolation` (scans generated code for direct provider imports)

**Detection Pattern:**
```
Generated file contains: 
  'using Neo4j' OR 'IDriver' OR '_neo4jDriver' OR 'AsyncSession()'
→ DNA VIOLATION + CF-743 violation → BUILD FAILURE
```

---

### CF-744 — Graph Scope Isolation: No Cross-Tenant Graph Edges

**Severity:** CRITICAL  
**Entities:** Graph nodes (Family, Factory, TaskType, etc.)  
**APIs:** F1423:SeedCatalogGraph, F1423:UpsertProviderImplementation  

**Conflict:**
A SYSTEM-scoped catalog graph node is linked to a TENANT-scoped implementation node without scope validation. Result: a tenant's provider implementation appears in the SYSTEM catalog graph — corrupts global context for all other tenants.

**Resolution:**
F1423:SeedCatalogGraph enforces: `source.scope` and `target.scope` must match OR the edge type explicitly permits cross-scope (none defined for FLOW-33). Node schema validation on every upsert: if `scope != 'SYSTEM'` AND the node connects to a SYSTEM node via a Catalog Graph edge → reject with `ScopeBoundaryViolation`.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-graph-scope` (edge validation log)

---

### CF-745 — Graph Integrity Before Completion Event

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Entities:** GraphCatalogSeeded event, Family nodes, Factory nodes  
**APIs:** F1423:ValidateGraphIntegrity  

**Conflict:**
GraphCatalogSeeded event is emitted with node counts below the oracle (e.g., 40 families instead of ≥85). Downstream flows that trust GraphCatalogSeeded will operate on incomplete graph data.

**Resolution:**
F1423:ValidateGraphIntegrity runs BEFORE emitting GraphCatalogSeeded. Validation checks: familiesCount ≥ (registry oracle), factoriesCount ≥ (registry oracle), all DEPENDS_ON edges resolve. If deviation > 5% → emit GraphCatalogSeedFailed (not GraphCatalogSeeded) → bootstrap halts at this phase.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-graph-integrity` (oracle comparison log)

---

### CF-746 — BFA Registration Before Promotion

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Entities:** GeneratedFactory, BFAEntityIndex, BFAEventIndex  
**Events:** FamilyImplementationCompleted  
**APIs:** F1421:UpdateImplementationStatus  

**Conflict:**
Generated factory is promoted from GENERATED to INJECTED (or higher) without registering its entities/events/APIs in BFA indices. Future BFA conflict checks will miss this factory's entities — cross-flow conflicts go undetected.

**Resolution:**
Arbiter E (BFA) in T540 checks that BFA registration docs exist before passing. F1421:UpdateImplementationStatus rejects promotions above GENERATED without `bfaRegistrationCompleted: true` in evidence dict. BFA registration = entities doc + events doc + APIs doc in `bfa-entity-index`, `bfa-event-index`, `bfa-api-index`.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-registration-gate` (tracks registration completeness per factory)

---

### CF-747 — Multi-Model Candidate Isolation

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Entities:** GenerationCandidates (AF-5 outputs)  
**APIs:** F1424:StartFamilyImplementation (AF-5 parallel execution)  

**Conflict:**
Two parallel model generations (e.g., OpenAI + Claude) write to the same candidate buffer or share mutable state. Result: merged output contains code from both in a non-deterministic mix — arbiter evaluation is not reproducible.

**Resolution:**
AiDispatcher (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) allocates separate result buffers per model provider. Each candidate identified by `{ runId, modelProvider, iteration }`. AF-10 merge receives all candidates as a list — never mutates individual candidate buffers. All candidate results stored in ES (with modelProvider tag) before merge begins.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-candidate-isolation` (generation run audit log)

---

### CF-748 — Verbatim Failure Trace Propagation

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Entities:** ArbiterResult, GenerationPrompt  
**APIs:** F1425:BuildArbiterPrompt  

**Conflict:**
Arbiter failure trace is paraphrased or summarized before injection into the next generation prompt. The original error location, rule reference, and line reference are lost. Next generation attempt does not fix the exact issue because it received a vague description.

**Resolution:**
F1425:BuildArbiterPrompt injects `priorFailures[].failureTrace` verbatim — no transformation. AiDispatcher is invoked with the raw trace str from the previous arbiter result. AF-11 checks that the trace in the prompt matches the stored arbiter output (str equality on first 200 chars).

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-trace-fidelity` (traces per run are stored and compared)

---

### CF-749 — Arbiter Prompt Citations Required

**Severity:** MEDIUM  
**Entities:** ArbiterOutput, ContextPack  
**APIs:** F1425:RunSingleArbiter  

**Conflict:**
Arbiter returns a pass/fail verdict but does not cite which ContextPack skills or BFA rules it checked. Result: AF-11 cannot score which skills are effective — prompt evolution is blind.

**Resolution:**
Arbiter output schema requires `citedSkills[]` and `citedRules[]` fields (non-empty). F1425 validates response schema before accepting. If `citedSkills` is empty → treat as "analysis incomplete" → reduce score by 20 points (still may pass if score ≥ threshold, but logged as quality concern).

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-arbiter-citations` (per-run citation completeness log)

---

### CF-750 — Regression Completeness: All Impacted Providers

**Severity:** CRITICAL  
**Entities:** ProviderImpl, TestSuite, RegressionRunRequested  
**APIs:** F1427:ComputeImpact, F1427:TriggerRegressionRun  

**Conflict:**
After a factory interface change, T541 (Regression Impact Trigger) retests only SOME providers implementing the changed interface (e.g., tests Elastic but not Mongo). The untested provider may have a missing method implementation — goes to production broken.

**Resolution:**
F1427:ComputeImpact queries Impact Graph exhaustively: ALL (ProviderImpl)-[:IMPLEMENTS]->(Method {factoryId: X}) nodes. The returned `impactedProviders[]` list must match the count of provider implementations in F1421 for that factory. If counts differ → F1427 emits `ImpactScopeIncomplete` and halts regression trigger until reconciled.

**Enforcement Index:** `bfa-regression-completeness` (provider count validation per factory per regression run)

---

## STRESS TESTS — FLOW-33

### ST-455 — Double Bootstrap Scenario

**Scenario:** Two `PlatformBootRequested` events arrive within the debounce window (30s) — one from a service restart and one from an admin trigger. Both reach the flow orchestrator simultaneously.

**Attack Vector:** Race condition — both instances check sentinel, both find it absent, both proceed to Phase 1 (schema registration) in parallel.

**Defense Layers:**
1. Debounce window (30s, latest_wins policy) collapses duplicate events at queue level
2. F1419:CheckBootstrapStatus uses ES optimistic locking — only one writer succeeds for `bootstrap-sentinel` document
3. F1419:AdvanceBootstrapPhase is idempotent — duplicate phase-advance writes are safe (same phase key + version)
4. CF-740 enforcement: if sentinel exists with correct version → immediate exit

**Expected Result:** Only one bootstrap sequence completes. Second instance detects sentinel and exits. No float-registration of schemas, registries, or graph nodes.

**Verification:**
- `factory_registry` doc count == oracle (F1-F1428), not 2×
- `bootstrap-sentinel.version` == 1 (not 2)
- Queue: exactly 1 BootstrapCompleted event

---

### ST-456 — Plan Bundle Range Mismatch Attack

**Scenario:** A corrupted or outdated plan bundle is uploaded with factory range F1-F500 (instead of F1-F1428). CF-739 must catch this before any registry mutations.

**Attack Vector:** Stale plan bundle from a previous session is accidentally re-uploaded after FLOW-33 has been designed (which adds F1419-F1428).

**Defense Layers:**
1. F1420:ValidatePlanBundle compares `expectedRanges.factories = F1-F500` against session state oracle `F1-F1428` → mismatch detected
2. PlanBundleRejected emitted with structured error report
3. Zero registry writes occur — CF-739 enforced by `dependsOn` in T536 DAG

**Expected Result:**
- `PlanBundleRejected { reason: 'RANGE_MISMATCH', expected: 'F1-F1428', got: 'F1-F500' }` emitted
- No factory_registry docs written
- Bootstrap phase remains at `VALIDATING` (not COMPILED)
- Admin receives rejection report with exact gap (F1419-F1428 missing)

---

### ST-457 — Graph Seeding Partial Failure Mid-Run

**Scenario:** During T538 (GraphRAG Catalog Seed), the process fails after seeding Factory nodes but before wiring DEPENDS_ON edges. System restarts.

**Attack Vector:** Infrastructure failure (OOM, container restart) mid-graph-seeding.

**Defense Layers:**
1. F1423:SeedCatalogGraph uses a phase-based seeding state stored in ES (which node types have been seeded)
2. On restart: reads seeding state → resumes from edge wiring phase (nodes already exist)
3. Node upserts are idempotent (unique constraint on `id` — duplicate upsert = no-op)
4. CF-745 enforcement: GraphCatalogSeeded only emitted after ValidateGraphIntegrity passes

**Expected Result:**
- After restart, seeding resumes from DEPENDS_ON edge wiring
- No duplicate Family/Factory nodes
- GraphCatalogSeeded emitted only after full integrity check passes
- `edgesCount` in result matches expected value

---

### ST-458 — Arbiter Loop Infinite Retry Prevention

**Scenario:** T539 (Implement-Family Meta-Loop) runs 10 iterations but every candidate fails Arbiter C (DNA_COMPLIANCE) because the model consistently uses typed models. Max iteration limit must trigger escalation, not infinite loop.

**Attack Vector:** Systematic prompt failure — every candidate violates DNA-1 regardless of prior failure traces.

**Defense Layers:**
1. MACHINE rule: maxIterations = 10 — no config override
2. At iteration 10 with failure: F1424 emits FamilyImplementationFailed { runId, failedArbiter: 'DNA', trace }
3. Event routes to DLQ — not re-queued
4. AF-11 stores all 10 failure traces for prompt analysis (SK-354 evolution trigger)
5. Human escalation: DLQ message includes family spec + all failure traces for manual review

**Expected Result:**
- Exactly 10 iterations run
- FamilyImplementationFailed emitted to DLQ
- No 11th attempt (hard limit enforced)
- AF-11 feedback stored for all 10 iterations
- Prompt performance report (F1428) shows 10 failures → triggers prompt review flag

---

### ST-459 — Cross-Tenant Graph Contamination

**Scenario:** A tenant-scoped ProviderImpl node for TenantA is accidentally linked with a SYSTEM-scope TaskType node via DEPENDS_ON edge. This would expose TenantA's implementation details in SYSTEM-level context queries.

**Attack Vector:** Missing scope check in F1423:UpsertProviderImplementation when creating Impact Graph edges.

**Defense Layers:**
1. CF-744 enforcement: F1423 validates `source.scope == target.scope` before edge creation
2. If cross-scope edge attempted → ScopeBoundaryViolation error, edge rejected
3. Q1/Q2/Q3 queries always filter by `WHERE n.scope = $scope` — cross-scope data not returned even if edge exists
4. Graph integrity validation includes scope check on edges

**Expected Result:**
- Edge creation rejected with `ScopeBoundaryViolation`
- SYSTEM graph Q1/Q2/Q3 do not return TenantA nodes
- `bfa-graph-scope` enforcement index records the violation attempt
- No contamination of SYSTEM context packs with tenant data

---

### ST-460 — Regression Scope Explosion Containment

**Scenario:** IDatabaseService (the shared database fabric interface) is updated with a new method. F1427:ComputeImpact runs on this — theoretically every factory using DATABASE FABRIC is impacted. This could trigger regression for 600+ factories simultaneously.

**Attack Vector:** Unscoped regression trigger on a shared fabric interface causes system overload.

**Defense Layers:**
1. CF-750 scopes regression to the specific factory (e.g., F1419:IBootstrapService), not the base fabric interface
2. Impact computation uses factory-level graph edges (ProviderImpl-[:IMPLEMENTS]->Method {factoryId: X}), not fabric-level
3. Regression trigger for fabric changes: deferred batch mode (queue with `batchSize: 50`, spread over 2h window)
4. Priority system: CORE-tier factories regressed first, GENERATED-tier deferred

**Expected Result:**
- Regression triggered only for factories directly implementing the changed interface
- Batch processing prevents queue overflow
- CORE factories regressed within 1h, GENERATED factories within 4h
- No timeout or DLQ overflow

---

### ST-461 — Plan Bundle Referential Integrity Failure

**Scenario:** T537 (Plan Bundle Install Gate) extracts factory registry but T47 task type references F999 (a factory that doesn't exist in the extracted factory_registry). Referential integrity check must catch this.

**Attack Vector:** Documentation drift — a task type was updated to add a factory dependency but the factory definition wasn't included in the same plan bundle update.

**Defense Layers:**
1. CF-739 referential integrity check: for every TaskType in task_type_registry, validate all `factoryDependencies[]` exist in factory_registry by ID
2. Missing F999 → `IntegrityViolation { taskTypeId: 'T47', missingFactory: 'F999' }` included in validation report
3. PlanBundleRejected (not partial install)
4. Admin receives structured gap report identifying which T/F pairs are inconsistent

**Expected Result:**
- PlanBundleRejected with referential integrity report
- Zero registry writes
- Report: `{ violations: [{ taskTypeId: 'T47', missingFactories: ['F999'] }] }`
- No partial factory_registry state (CF-742 enforced)

---

### ST-462 — Verbatim Failure Trace Drift

**Scenario:** After Arbiter C (DNA) returns a failure trace "Line 47: typed model BootstrapSentinel used", F1425:BuildArbiterPrompt injects a modified version "DNA violation found in sentinel handling" into the next generation prompt. CF-748 must detect this.

**Attack Vector:** A middleware layer accidentally summarizes failure traces (e.g., a logging/truncation layer) before they reach the next generation prompt.

**Defense Layers:**
1. CF-748 enforcement: F1425:BuildArbiterPrompt stores both original trace (from ArbiterResult) and injected trace (in prompt)
2. AF-11 compares: `injectedTrace.startsWith(originalTrace.substring(0, 200))` — must match
3. If drift detected: `TraceFidelityViolation` logged, run paused pending manual inspection
4. Prompt template uses raw str interpolation — no AI paraphrasing of failure content

**Expected Result:**
- Verbatim trace "Line 47: typed model BootstrapSentinel used" appears unchanged in next generation prompt
- AF-11 trace fidelity check passes
- If truncation was applied: TraceFidelityViolation emitted, generation paused
- Model on next iteration addresses the exact line 47 issue

---

## BFA RULES SUMMARY — FLOW-33

| CF ID | Title | Severity | Key Guard |
|-------|-------|----------|-----------|
| CF-739 | Oracle validation before registry writes | CRITICAL | PlanBundleValidated before CompileRegistry |
| CF-740 | Outbox: phase state before event | CRITICAL | ES write before queue emit |
| CF-741 | Schema-before-event: schemas registered first | HIGH | F194 first node in T536 |
| CF-742 | Atomic registry compilation | CRITICAL | bundle version + partial-state resume |
| CF-743 | Graph fabric isolation | CRITICAL | no direct Neo4j driver import |
| CF-744 | Graph scope isolation | CRITICAL | no cross-tenant edges |
| CF-745 | Graph integrity before completion | HIGH | ValidateGraphIntegrity before event |
| CF-746 | BFA registration before promotion | HIGH | evidence check in F1421 |
| CF-747 | Multi-model candidate isolation | HIGH | separate buffers per model |
| CF-748 | Verbatim failure trace propagation | HIGH | str equality check |
| CF-749 | Arbiter prompt citations required | MEDIUM | citedSkills[] non-empty |
| CF-750 | Regression completeness | CRITICAL | all providers tested |

## STRESS TESTS SUMMARY — FLOW-33

| ST ID | Scenario | Defense | Expected |
|-------|----------|---------|---------|
| ST-455 | Double bootstrap | debounce + ES lock + idempotency | single completion |
| ST-456 | Range mismatch bundle | CF-739 + zero writes | PlanBundleRejected |
| ST-457 | Graph seed partial failure | phase state + node idempotency | resume from edges |
| ST-458 | Arbiter infinite loop | maxIterations=10 MACHINE | DLQ + escalation |
| ST-459 | Cross-tenant graph contamination | CF-744 scope check | edge rejected |
| ST-460 | Regression scope explosion | factory-level scoping + batch | controlled rollout |
| ST-461 | Referential integrity failure | CF-739 ref check | PlanBundleRejected |
| ST-462 | Failure trace drift | CF-748 str equality | TraceFidelityViolation |

## BFA Rules CF-751–CF-788 | Stress Tests ST-463–ST-497
